title: capture/renewal: bound resume-hook retries -> REFUND_DUE on permanent failure (at daemon wiring)
description:
## Outcome
When the daemon supervisor (lnrent-7fp.21) wires the recipe `resume` hook around a RENEWAL settlement that resumes a SUSPENDED sub, it must run the hook CRASH-SAFELY: bound the retries and, on permanent failure, route the subscription to REFUND_DUE (refund executed by .11) — mirroring provision's capture-then-refund bounded-retry escape. A failing resume hook must NOT strand a settled buyer with no service and no refund.

## Why
capture_txn is a PURE sqlite transaction and cannot run the async resume hook; the renewal-resume path only flips state='ACTIVE' + journals renew_resume (capture.rs:176-216). The recipe `resume` hook therefore runs OUTSIDE capture, in the supervisor (.21). If that hook keeps failing and the code `?`-propagates around the OPEN->PAID capture, a settled renewal can strand: invoice never PAID -> never refunded, and reconcile can't rescue it (backend status Paid). Provision already has the bounded-retry -> REFUND_DUE escape (ADR-0003 'funds never vanish'); the resume path needs the same. Split out of lnrent-g5p during implementation: g5p's fix-2 premise ('capture runs resume before the CAS') does NOT match the code — it is a .21 wiring concern, not a capture.rs change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- When .21 runs the resume hook on a renewal-resume and it permanently fails (after bounded retries), the sub is routed to REFUND_DUE + a refund_attempt is created (refund executed by .11) — the buyer is never left settled-but-unserved-and-unrefunded.
- Integration: a persistently-failing resume hook on a renewal settlement -> REFUND_DUE + refund, not an infinite strand.
close_reason: DONE + gate-verified: RESUMING state + ResumeDriver (daemon/src/resume.rs) runs the recipe resume hook on a paid suspended-renewal -> ACTIVE, or on permanent failure refunds the renewal + restores SUSPENDED (instance/reservation preserved). Spec docs/specs/resume-hook-driver.md (3-pass codex-review-loop). 5 resume tests + e2e green; independent codex gate confirmed the capture/driver/reconcile money paths safe. Cancel/renew-during-RESUMING split to a new bead (the naive fix was a fund-safety P1, reverted).

title: Refunder bounded-concurrency drive (pre-public throughput)
description:
h18 item 2 (deferred): Refunder::drive() processes refunds SERIALLY (refund.rs); each LNURL resolution can take up to the 60s RESOLUTION_DEADLINE, so one slow/hostile buyer endpoint delays every other pending refund behind it (bounded to <=60s each by the per-row deadline, so no indefinite head-of-line-block). This is THROUGHPUT/DoS hardening, NOT money correctness — only needed once broad hostile PUBLIC traffic is expected (M1c public exposure). Add bounded concurrency to the drive, or confirm the per-row deadline is sufficient. NOT on the M1a/pre-real-money path (single operator). Verified this session: h18 item 1 (transient-failure escalation: per-row deadline + stuck-PENDING operator-loud alert, no terminalize) is DONE, item 3 (SPEC schema doc) synced.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Refunder::drive() no longer serializes every pending refund behind one slow LNURL resolution: either add bounded concurrency, OR explicitly confirm + record that the per-row 60s RESOLUTION_DEADLINE is a sufficient bound.
- A slow/hostile buyer refund endpoint does not delay unrelated pending refunds beyond the per-row deadline.
- Test: N pending refunds with one endpoint stalling to the deadline; assert the others complete without queuing behind it (or, if deadline-only is chosen, a test documenting the bound).
- Scope note preserved: throughput/DoS hardening for M1c public exposure, NOT money correctness; not on the M1a single-operator path.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `clippy` clean.

title: Fedimint refund safety core: gen-0 bare key + pay-oplog recovery
description:
## Outcome
Close the two latent Fedimint refund crash/upgrade money-safety hazards BEFORE any main.rs Fedimint wiring (lnrent-o6p). Carved out of o6p per the codex forward review (2026-06-29) — smaller, local, directly closes the live refund correctness risk. The two items close DIFFERENT residuals; BOTH are required (codex verified).

## 1. gen-0 = BARE KEY (closes the legacy upgrade DOUBLE-PAY class)
gen_key() currently suffixes EVERY generation incl 0 (refund.rs:147); bolt11 pass-through picks gen 0 (refund.rs:360). Change gen-0 (bolt11 / not-yet-resolved) to pay under the BARE `refund:<external_id>`; only gen>=1 (resolved LNURL) keeps `:g<gen>`. CAVEAT (codex): changing gen_key() ALONE is not enough — the legacy bare-key `Pending` branch currently just DEFERS (refund.rs:239); for bolt11/gen-0 it must RE-ENTER pay() on the bare key so FedimintPayment re-awaits the stored op (fedimint_backend.rs:390). WHY SUFFICIENT: the old binary could only ever pay a bolt11 dest, under the bare key; old rows are anchored `refund:<external_id>` (capture.rs:278, provision.rs:857); resolution_gen defaults 0 (store.rs:104) so old/unresolved bolt11 -> bare-key gen-0; order/renewal external_ids disjoint (order:.../renew:req:.../renew:auto:..., no collision). Removes ug8's legacy bare-key fall-through entirely + closes the legacy in-flight-Unknown double-pay.

## 2. fedimint_pay OPLOG RECOVERY (closes the refund STRANDING class)
A crash AFTER pay_bolt11_invoice commits but BEFORE pay_idx_upsert (fedimint_backend.rs:449/463) leaves the gen key with no fedimint_pay row -> payment_status_by_key()==Unknown (fedimint_backend.rs:480/844). The refund planner never re-resolves Unknown (refund.rs:409), and fedimint checks invoice expiry before returning a prior completed payment, so re-calling pay without the op id can FAIL before discovering the old success -> the refund SITS PENDING INDEFINITELY (stranded, not double-paid). FIX: on FedimintPayment open, recover the fedimint_pay index from the fedimint operation log. TEMPLATE = recover_index_from_oplog (the receive-side backfill, fedimint_backend.rs:592) which paginates the op log + parses LightningOperationMeta. For Pay: lnrent stamps lnrent_idempotency_key into extra_meta (fedimint_backend.rs:452); fedimint's LightningOperationMetaVariant::Pay carries extra_meta + is_internal_payment (v0.11.1). Backfill the fedimint_pay row (idempotency_key, operation_id, pay_kind ln/internal, status) so Unknown -> a real status + the refund re-drives to terminal.

## DONE
- gen-0 uses the bare `refund:<external_id>` key; gen>=1 uses `:g<gen>`; the legacy bare-key Pending bolt11 path RE-ENTERS pay() (not just defers); ug8's legacy bare-key fall-through removed; schema/SPEC comments updated.
- recover_fedimint_pay_from_oplog runs on FedimintPayment open (alongside recover_index_from_oplog), backfilling missing pay rows.
- LIVE REGRESSION (extend daemon/tests/fedimint_live.rs, feature + #[ignore]): pay a refund, DELETE/miss the fedimint_pay row, reopen FedimintPayment, recover from oplog, prove payment_status_by_key is NOT Unknown + a re-drive reaches terminal (the current fedimint_live only proves the receive/pay primitive, fedimint_live.rs:48).
- whole-workspace cargo test + clippy (default AND --features fedimint) green; the refund.rs generation-gate tests updated for bare-key gen-0.

## Files
daemon/src/refund.rs (gen_key gen-0 bare key + legacy Pending re-enter-pay + tests), daemon/src/fedimint_backend.rs (recover_fedimint_pay_from_oplog), daemon/src/store.rs + SPEC.md (comments), daemon/tests/fedimint_live.rs (live regression).

## Non-goals

## STATUS (2026-06-29)
PART 1 (gen-0 = bare key) DONE + MERGED (master 0e18769) via watched rb-lite (panel cleared, no P0/P1; the :g0-vs-bare window documented as unreachable). refund.rs net -40 lines (legacy fall-through deleted). The round-2 codex P2 (in-flight legacy-bolt11 upgrade safety has no backend-dedup TEST) is now covered by PART 2's live regression below.
PART 2 (fedimint_pay OPLOG RECOVERY) = REMAINING, built directly (the --features fedimint compile + the devimint live proof make rb-lite a poor fit). recover_fedimint_pay_from_oplog on open (template: recover_index_from_oplog), + a live fedimint_live.rs regression: pay, drop the fedimint_pay row, reopen, recover, assert NOT Unknown + re-drive terminal.
close_reason: DONE + merged. PART 1 gen-0=bare-key (master 0e18769, via rb-lite, panel-cleared) + PART 2 fedimint_pay oplog recovery (master 31fed0b, direct build). recover_pay_from_oplog backfills the fedimint_pay index from the operation log's Pay extra_meta on open (op id + ln/internal kind, only MISSING keys, fail-closed) so a crash-window pay (op committed, row not yet upserted -> Unknown) is re-awaited via the OP by the next pay(key) instead of re-parsing the maybe-expired bolt11 -> no strand, no double-pay. PROVEN LIVE against devimint v0.11.1 (new fedimint_live regression: pay, DELETE the row, reopen, recover, re-drive to Succeeded; 2/2 live tests pass). codex merge gate: MERGE, no P0/P1. Satisfies o6p's safety-core prerequisites (oplog recovery + gen-0=bare-key).

title: Sweep stuck on a permanently-ambiguous pay livelocks future sweeps with no durable alert
description:
# Permanently-ambiguous sweep blocks all future sweeps silently (no durable operator alert)

**Discovered during lnrent-y4m.16** via the adversarial codex pass (its P2). With y4m.16, an ambiguous terminal pay leaves the sweep PENDING (correct: never re-pay). But sweep.rs blocks ANY new sweep while one is PENDING, and durable SweepFailed alerts fire only on a FAILED transition — a permanently-ambiguous sweep produces repeated warn logs but no operator DM, unlike the refund path's 6h RefundStuck alert.

## Scope
Add a RefundStuck-style durable alert (e.g. SweepStuck after N hours PENDING) in sweep.rs so a livelocked sweep queue is surfaced to the operator. Driver-side only; no backend/classification changes.

title: M1a — Durable payment/order handshake (prove the loop)
description:
## Outcome
A single-box lnrent daemon that proves the full rent loop end to end with a trivial, instant recipe: publish a NIP-99 listing, take a NIP-17 order, reserve capacity, issue a Fedimint invoice, capture the settlement idempotently, provision, deliver credentials over a NIP-17 DM, let the buyer manage the rented service through one ops interface, and run the prepaid-expiry subscription lifecycle (renew / suspend / destroy) with capture-then-refund and crash recovery.

## Why this exists
The order/payment handshake is the product and its riskiest part. M1a de-risks it independent of VM/Incus/Iroh complexity (M1b/M1c) by exercising the whole durable money path against a trivial recipe (a WireGuard peer or a dummy service). If this is correct, everything else is recipes.

## Scope
The handshake control plane only: store actor, the Fedimint backend payment client, Nostr engine (NIP-99 + NIP-17 DM protocol), recipe runner + one trivial recipe, pre-flight + capacity reservation, idempotent capture, reconcile-loop state machine + downtime credit, provision + delivery outbox, capture-then-refund ledger, the CLI<->daemon IPC surface, the buyer service-management ops dispatch (recipe-declared operations dispatched through one buyer interface, request-kind in M1a — §7.4/ADR-0013), a minimal CLI buyer and a web WASM buyer (browser access), operator identity/config bootstrap, and the end-to-end money-path tests.

## Non-goals
No Incus/VM provisioning, no Iroh/Tor reachability (so no interactive-kind ops), no security tiers beyond Tier 0 labeling, no per-box key split / operator manifest (single account-0 key), no NWC pull. Those are M1b/M1c/M5. (The web WASM buyer IS in M1a — see Scope.)

## Critical constraints
AI-free control plane (pure Rust, no LLM at runtime — including every management op). Daemon is the SOLE writer of sqlite (ADR-0001). The Fedimint backend/Fedimint cannot hold invoices, so capture-then-refund (ADR-0003). Durable, crash-safe handshake (ADR-0009). Management ops are a recipe's declared narrow surface, never arbitrary host shell (ADR-0013). Both sides are increasingly AI agents (ADR-0014): the agent surface is a COMPLETE CLI (no MCP/HTTP server) — build the buyer (lnrent-7fp.13) and operator (lnrent-7fp.12/.16) CLIs agent-grade from the start (`--json`, non-interactive, deterministic exit codes, structured errors); the AI-free plane is the injection firewall that makes agent mediation safe (§13).

## Spec refs
SPEC.md v0.28 §4.7, §5.1 (incl. order/renew idempotency), §6 (§6.2/§6.3 total state machine/§6.5/§6.6 refund idempotency), §7.4, §9.3, §11 (15 tables), §13; ADR-0001, ADR-0003, ADR-0005, ADR-0009, ADR-0013, ADR-0014; eng-review T1-T14.

## Success Criteria
- The full M1a loop runs on one box with the trivial recipe: list -> order -> reserve -> invoice -> out-of-band wallet settlement -> capture -> provision -> deliver -> renew -> suspend -> destroy.
- A buyer can run a recipe-declared management operation (e.g. status/restart) on an active subscription through the same buyer interface; an unauthorized op is refused.
- All four mandatory money-path tests pass green in CI (see lnrent-7fp.15).
- The daemon can be killed at any handshake step and recovers correctly on restart.
- A buyer who pays always ends in exactly one of: credentials delivered, or funds refunded. Never stranded, never silently charged.
- The web WASM buyer (lnrent-7fp.18) completes the full loop in a headless browser (browser marketplace access), after the CLI money-path gate (lnrent-7fp.15).
close_reason: M1a COMPLETE + proven. The durable payment/order handshake loop is built, wired as a supervised daemon, buyer-CLI + web-SPA driven, e2e-tested (mock + live-fedimint-regtest + headless-browser), fedimint is the default build with real payments proven live, refund/provisioning hardened (6 codex findings), and CI gates it. All numbered children .1-.22 closed (.18 web buyer last). Remaining work is standalone POST-M1a P2/P3 polish/hardening (7qc/h18/d6n/k82/xjn/18v/ux7/c95/xov) + the operator's mainnet go-live (docs/go-live.md).

title: Store actor + sqlite layer (sole writer)
description:
## Outcome
One tokio task owns the rusqlite Connection and is the only writer; reconcile, Nostr, payment, and IPC tasks send typed commands over an mpsc channel and await replies. Provides transactional CRUD for all §11 tables and the atomic multi-row transitions the handshake needs (e.g. invoice OPEN->PAID + sub PENDING->PROVISIONING in one txn).

## Why
ADR-0001: the daemon is the sole writer so the AI-free invariant is structural, not a convention, and there are no write races. The actor keeps rusqlite (sync) on one thread.

## Scope
store module, command enum + mpsc, transaction helpers, event_log append on every transition, sqlite WAL mode, the §11 schema (already in store.rs: **15 tables** — operator, recipe, subscription, invoice, event_log, reservation, daemon_state, refund_attempt, outbox, op_invocation, inbound_request, box, instance, listing, native_connect_session). Two durable idempotency tables, both PRIMARY KEY (sender_pubkey, request_id): `op_invocation` (management ops, §7.4/ADR-0013) and `inbound_request` (order/renew request DMs, §5.1) — a duplicate request DM is a no-op insert, and the handler resends the cached response rather than creating a second reservation/order/invoice. The `inbound_request` cached response is written in the SAME transaction as the order's PENDING-sub + invoice (so there's no in-flight gap — lnrent-7fp.17). Lifecycle transitions are compare-and-swap UPDATEs on `(state, next_deadline)` (the row is the guard, no separate table — §6.5). The `invoice` table carries `expires_at` / `kind` / `applied_at` + backend ids (`external_id NOT NULL UNIQUE`); `refund_attempt.idempotency_key` is **NOT NULL UNIQUE** (`refund:<external_id>`) so a redelivered terminal settlement inserts ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING → exactly one refund row (§6.6). `instance`/`box`/`listing` model the prose entities (§4.4, one-recipe-many-listings); `native_connect_session` holds interactive-op tickets (M1b).

## Non-goals
Business logic (capture/reconcile/refund/op-dispatch/order-intake call the store; they live in their own beads).

## Constraints / failure handling
Sole writer; every mutation journaled to event_log; atomic captures use one transaction; WAL is enabled (durability + headroom for a future read-only connection); no lock held across an await. Note: in the current actor every access — reads included — runs on the one connection thread, so reads serialize with writes through the actor; a separate RO WAL connection is a later optimization, not part of the sole-writer guarantee. The unique keys on `op_invocation` and `inbound_request` make a duplicate request a no-op insert (the handler then resends the cached result).

## Dependencies
Depends on: injectable clock (lnrent-7fp.2). Unblocks: migrations, reservation, capture, reconcile, provision, refund, IPC, backup, operator-init, order-intake, op-dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20).

## Verification
Unit: each txn helper + the atomic capture txn (rollback on error). Keep the schema test (**15 tables**). A concurrency test that command senders serialize. A test that a second insert with the same (sender_pubkey, request_id) no-ops, for BOTH op_invocation and inbound_request.

## Spec refs
ADR-0001, ADR-0009, ADR-0013; SPEC §11; eng-review T1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 15 §11 tables exist; the schema test passes.
- The capture transaction (invoice OPEN->PAID + sub PENDING->PROVISIONING + event_log append) commits or rolls back atomically (unit test proves rollback on injected error).
- Every write goes through the actor; a concurrency test shows commands serialize with no lost update.
- WAL is enabled (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL); the schema/migration path sets it. (Reads still serialize through the single-connection actor in M1a — a non-blocking RO read path is deferred, not claimed here.)
- A duplicate insert on the same (sender_pubkey, request_id) does not create a second row, for BOTH `op_invocation` and `inbound_request` (the two idempotency keys).
- A duplicate insert on the same `refund_attempt.idempotency_key` does not create a second row (UNIQUE + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) — one refund row per terminal settlement even on backend redelivery.

title: Provision + delivery outbox (crash-safe)
description:
## Outcome
The crash-safe settle->capture->provision->deliver sequence. On PROVISIONING, run the recipe provision hook (idempotent, re-run on restart); on success, in ONE transaction move the subscription -> ACTIVE, set `paid_through = settled_at + period`, record the `instance` row, and write an outbox row carrying the provision.ready DM. A sender task drains the outbox, publishes the NIP-17 DM, marks it SENT, and retries until sent. A crash after ACTIVE but before send re-sends on restart, so a paid buyer is never stranded. On **permanent provision failure**, run a **best-effort `destroy`** to purge any partial resources, then hand off to the refund bead (-> REFUND_DUE, lnrent-7fp.11).

## Why
If ACTIVE is committed before the credentials DM is delivered, the buyer paid but may never receive access; relays also drop DMs. The durable outbox guarantees delivery and is the dropped-DM resync answer (codex #6 / ADR-0009). A failed provision can leave half-created VM/network/volume resources; cleaning them up before refund avoids leaks and stuck capacity (codex).

## Scope
provision invocation via the recipe runner; the atomic ACTIVE+instance+outbox transaction; the outbox sender (drain/publish/retry/mark SENT); restart re-drive of PROVISIONING and unsent outbox; the best-effort `destroy` on permanent provision failure before handoff to refund.

## Non-goals
Refund execution (lnrent-7fp.11); the Nostr publish primitive (lnrent-7fp.5).

## Constraints / failure handling
lifecycle hooks (provision/destroy) MUST be idempotent (§7.2, re-run safe); outbox write is in the same txn as ACTIVE; sender retries with backoff; restart re-runs stuck PROVISIONING and resends unsent outbox rows; a destroy failure during cleanup is logged + alerted but does not block the refund.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store, recipe runner, nostr, capture. Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Integration: kill the daemon between ACTIVE and outbox-send -> the DM is resent on restart; the buyer processes provision.ready idempotently (at-least-once delivery, deduped by message id); a forced permanent provision failure runs best-effort destroy then -> REFUND_DUE.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §6.3, §6.6; ADR-0009; eng-review T4.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kill the daemon between sub->ACTIVE and outbox-send: provision.ready is resent on restart (integration).
- provision.ready is delivered at-least-once and deduped by a stable message id (NOT exactly-once network delivery); the buyer processes it idempotently.
- A subscription stuck in PROVISIONING is re-driven on restart.
- On permanent provision failure, a best-effort `destroy` runs (purging partial resources) before the subscription enters REFUND_DUE.
- ACTIVE sets `paid_through = settled_at + period` (not wall-clock now).

## Note (codex)
The ACTIVE transaction also CONSUMES the order's reservation (HELD->CONSUMED, lnrent-7fp.7) and records the `instance` row.

## Constraints (codex next-steps review)
- `paid_through = invoice.settled_at + period` — use the SETTLED invoice's `settled_at` (set by capture .8), NEVER the wall clock, so a delayed provision can't shift the paid window.
- CONSUME the capacity reservation (reservation.consume, .7) in the SAME transaction that moves the sub to ACTIVE + writes the instance row + the outbox provision.ready — so an ACTIVE sub always holds its capacity atomically.
close_reason: Provision + delivery outbox implemented (daemon/src/provision.rs, ~385 impl lines): Provisioner::drive runs the recipe provision hook (idempotent, bounded retry) then ONE store txn moves PROVISIONING->ACTIVE (CAS), paid_through = invoice.settled_at + period, CONSUMES the reservation (reservation::consume_txn, same txn), records the instance row + a PENDING outbox provision.ready DM; re-drive of an ACTIVE sub = Skipped no-op. Permanent failure -> best-effort destroy -> REFUND_DUE (refund exec is .11). OutboxSender drains/publishes via the .5 Outbound/marks SENT/bounded retry; recover()+drain_once() for .21 startup. Added reservation::consume_txn/release_txn (txn-composable). Built via rb-lite (17-round claude,codex hardening) + final codex xhigh GO (no P0/P1; atomic ACTIVE, idempotent re-drive, durable at-least-once outbox all verified). 159 tests green. Commit 113b869.

title: Capture-then-refund + refund ledger
description:
## Outcome
Capture-then-refund with a **crash-safe, idempotent** durable ledger. On permanent provision failure (after retries, and after lnrent-7fp.10's best-effort `destroy`) -> REFUND_DUE + a refund_attempt row written as `PENDING` (durable intent) with a durable `idempotency_key` **before** calling `PaymentBackend::pay(dest, amount, key)`. `pay` is idempotent on `key`, so recovery is simply to **retry `pay(key)`** for any non-terminal refund on restart — safe whether the crash was before OR after a prior call (the key dedups, the PENDING row is always there to retry); `payment_status_by_key` only skips a redundant call once a prior attempt `Succeeded`. After N genuinely-failed attempts the subscription stays REFUND_DUE and the operator is alerted (billing.refund DM, status=failed). Also executes settled-but-terminal refunds (a settlement on a terminal/expired sub -> auto-refund, order not resurrected).

## Why
phoenixd/Fedimint can't hold invoices, so funds are captured before provisioning; if provision fails (or a payment lands on a terminal order) the buyer must be made whole without a manual op (ADR-0003). The refund is an OUTBOUND payment that can crash mid-flight, so it must be idempotent on a durable key — recording `backend_payment_id` AFTER `pay` would double-refund on a between-success-and-persist crash, and an earlier draft's "persist SUBMITTED, never retry Unknown" STRANDED a refund that crashed before `pay` ever ran. Fix: persist `PENDING` intent, then `pay(key)`; retry `pay(key)` on restart — safe at every crash point because the key dedups (§6.6, codex).

## Scope
the refund_attempt ledger lifecycle (PENDING -> SENT/FAILED); the refunder task (persist PENDING -> pay(key) -> on restart retry pay(key), optionally skip via payment_status_by_key -> SENT/FAILED/alert); executing settled-but-terminal refunds (detection/creation owned by lnrent-7fp.8); the billing.refund DM.

## Non-goals
Receiving (the payment backend bead); the capture transition (capture bead); the partial-resource `destroy` cleanup (lnrent-7fp.10 runs it before handing off).

## Constraints / failure handling
NEVER double-pay AND never strand: idempotency keyed on a durable `idempotency_key` passed to `pay`, NOT on a backend_payment_id learned after the fact. The key (`refund:<external_id>`) is also `UNIQUE` on the row (lnrent-7fp.8 creates it ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), so the refunder always operates on exactly one row per terminal settlement. On restart a `PENDING` refund **retries `pay(key)`** — safe at any crash point because the key dedups; `payment_status_by_key` returns a **`PayStatus` (Unknown/Pending/Succeeded/Failed)** (the OUTBOUND status, distinct from the inbound-invoice `PaymentStatus`), used only to skip a redundant call when a prior attempt already `Succeeded`. This REQUIRES a backend that dedups `pay` on the key (natively or a durable key->payment map) — the v1 Fedimint backend must; a backend offering neither falls back to operator reconciliation. REFUND_DUE-stuck after N attempts alerts. Refund dest collected up front in order.request (BOLT12 offer preferred, LN address fallback).

## Dependencies
Depends on: store, the Fedimint backend (lnrent-7fp.4, for pay + payment_status_by_key), reconcile. Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Integration: provision failure -> auto-refund to a test dest -> REFUNDED; refund send fails -> stays REFUND_DUE + alert; a retry does NOT double-pay; crash AFTER pay-success but BEFORE recording backend_payment_id -> restart retries pay(key), key dedups, no double-refund; crash AFTER writing PENDING but BEFORE pay() ever ran -> restart retries pay(key) and the refund completes (NOT stranded); settled-but-terminal -> refund not provision.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.1, §6.3, §6.4, §6.6; ADR-0003, ADR-0009; eng-review T7; codex (refund idempotency).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provision failure -> auto-refund to a test dest; subscription -> REFUNDED (integration).
- Refund send fails -> subscription stays REFUND_DUE + operator alert; a retry does NOT double-pay (idempotency keyed on `idempotency_key`).
- Crash after pay() succeeds but before persisting backend_payment_id -> restart retries `pay(key)`; the key dedups so there is NO double-refund.
- Crash after the PENDING row is written but BEFORE pay() ever executes -> restart retries `pay(key)` and the refund completes; it is NOT stranded.
- A settlement on a terminal/expired sub -> refund_attempt, not provision.

## Note (codex)
Releases the reservation on REFUND_DUE (lnrent-7fp.7). Sends billing.refund (status sent/failed) by enqueueing a billing.refund outbox row (delivered by the sender in lnrent-7fp.10).
close_reason: Implemented in daemon/src/refund.rs (merged master e03a504): Refunder drains PENDING refund_attempt rows, key-idempotent pay, single CAS-guarded txn, crash-safe idempotent drive() recovery, reservation release on REFUND_DUE, no resurrection for settled-but-terminal, operator-loud alert + buyer billing.refund at the failure cap. 8 unit tests cover the AC scenarios. Built via a watched rb-lite run (3 rounds, stopped at convergence) + independent verify (cargo test/clippy, caught+fixed a clippy needless_borrow the panel missed). Pending-at-cap real-backend edge deferred to lnrent-nvg (blocked by .4).

title: lnrent CLI <-> daemon IPC surface
description:
## Outcome
A local CLI<->daemon IPC surface: a Unix-domain socket the daemon owns, with a small JSON/line command set (status, recipes, subs, and admin: force suspend/resume, inspect a subscription). The lnrent CLI and the Claude skills act ONLY through this; they never touch sqlite directly. The `lnrent` CLI over this socket is the OPERATOR's agent surface (ADR-0014): an operator agent runs the same CLI, no MCP/HTTP server.

## Why
ADR-0001 says skills act through the lnrent CLI with the daemon as sole writer; the surface was never specified. Without it skills hit sqlite directly and break the invariant (eng-review #2). ADR-0014: most operators will be agents, and the complete CLI is their surface — so it must be machine-drivable, not just human-friendly.

## Scope
the UDS server in the daemon (commands routed to the store actor); the lnrent CLI client wired to it (replace the M0 stub prints); read commands + a few admin mutations. **Agent-native (ADR-0014, §4.7):** every command supports `--json` (stable machine-readable output), is fully non-interactive (flags/env/stdin, no required prompt), returns deterministic exit codes + structured errors, and `status`/`recipes`/`subs` are scriptable JSON listings an operator agent can branch on.

## Non-goals
Remote/networked admin API and any HTTP/MCP server (forbidden by the VM guidelines + ADR-0014 — the CLI is the surface); the buyer client (separate bead, lnrent-7fp.13).

## Constraints / failure handling
socket in the data dir with tight perms; every mutation goes through the store actor (sole writer) and is journaled; no public/networked exposure.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: e2e (operator-side checks).

## Verification
Unit/integration: lnrent status/recipes/subs return live state via the socket; an admin suspend goes through the store actor and is journaled; a `--json` invocation emits stable parseable output and a failing command returns a nonzero exit + structured error.

## Spec refs
ADR-0001, ADR-0014; SPEC §4.2, §4.7, §10; eng-review architecture #2.

## Acceptance Criteria
- lnrent status / recipes / subs return live state via the Unix socket (integration).
- An admin suspend routes through the store actor and is journaled to event_log.
- The socket is not network-reachable.
- Every command supports `--json` with stable fields and runs non-interactively; failures return deterministic nonzero exit codes + a structured error (operator agent can drive it without parsing prose). (ADR-0014.)
- CLI conformance gate: enumerate ALL M1a operator commands, snapshot each command's `--json` output schema (golden), run every command with no TTY, assert structured stderr per error class, and pin the exit-code taxonomy — "agent-grade" is tested, not just asserted.

title: Minimal CLI buyer
description:
## Outcome
A shared **buyer-core** Rust lib (the lnrent DM protocol, order flow, gift-wrap encode/decode, AND the unified service-management `ops` interface — §7.4, with injectable relay + signer seams) that is target-agnostic (compiles native AND wasm32), plus a thin native **CLI buyer** over it: discover a NIP-99 listing, send order.request (+ refund dest + params), receive order.invoice, **return the bolt11** (the buyer pays it out-of-band from their own wallet — §4.7), await/poll provision.ready, print credentials — and after delivery, manage the rented service through the same client. The client is a protocol client, NOT a wallet: it never holds funds or pays.

## Why
M1a needs a buyer side to prove list->order->pay->provision->deliver. Extracting buyer-core (instead of a CLI-only impl) lets the web WASM buyer (lnrent-7fp.18) reuse ONE protocol implementation. The same buyer-core is where a rented service is managed (ADR-0013): one client both rents AND operates, with no per-service tooling.

## Scope
buyer-core lib (protocol/order/gift-wrap; relay + signer as injected traits — NO payer seam, payment is not the client's job); the unified `ops` surface — discover a subscription's recipe-declared operations (from the Listing's published recipe metadata), build/send op.request, await op.result for `request`-kind ops; the native CLI shell (local-key signer, native WS relays) that RETURNS the bolt11 for the buyer to pay out-of-band, with `lnrent-buyer ops <sub> list` / `lnrent-buyer ops <sub> <op> [params]`.

## Agent-native CLI (ADR-0014, §4.7)
The buyer CLI is the AGENT surface (the web shell lnrent-7fp.18 is the human surface), so build it agent-grade from the start: every command takes `--json` (stable machine-readable output), is fully non-interactive (flags/env/stdin, never a required prompt), returns deterministic exit codes, and emits structured errors (the `{code,message,retryable}` taxonomy, incl. the structured `order.error`). Discovery is scriptable: `listings` / `subs` / `ops <sub> list` all emit JSON. **Payment is OUT of scope (§4.7):** the CLI returns the invoice (`order ... --json` → bolt11/amount/expiry); the buyer's wallet pays it out-of-band (a human, or an agent's own payment logic), then the CLI awaits/polls `provision.ready`. buyer-core has no payer seam and never holds funds. **Injection-safe discipline (§13)** — two parts: (1) **Provenance** (milestone-aware): act only on signature-verified fields — in M1a the operator's account-0 event signature (manifest membership + attestations are M5, NOT claimed in M1a). (2) **Taint** (signed != safe): operator-produced payloads (`provision.ready.payload`, `op.result.data`, hook delivery JSON) are untrusted as instructions — buyer-core surfaces typed known fields distinctly from opaque/display-only blobs + free-text, never auto-executes a URL/command from a payload, so a client agent cannot be hijacked by hostile (even validly-signed) operator content.

## Non-goals
The WASM/browser shell (lnrent-7fp.18); operator manifest verification (M5); **paying invoices / any wallet or payer (payment is the buyer's wallet's job, out-of-band, never in the client — §4.7)**. The fully-headless agent-loop + hostile-prose adversarial test are M1d (this bead fixes the protocol client + the act-on-structured discipline). `interactive`-kind ops (shell/console/logs -f over the Iroh Native-connect session, §9.2) — the ops interface RECOGNIZES the kind and routes to a (deferred, M1b+) Iroh transport seam, it does not silently drop it; only `request`-kind ops are wired in M1a.

## Constraints / failure handling
buyer-core must avoid native-only deps in its core path so it compiles to wasm32; signer/relay (and the deferred Iroh interactive transport) are injected per target. There is NO payer seam — the client returns invoices, it does not pay them.

## Dependencies
Depends on: the wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19, incl. op.request/op.result). Injects its own relay/signer seams; it does NOT depend on the operator engine (lnrent-7fp.5). The ops-interface integration runs against the operator-side dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20) and is gated jointly in the e2e (lnrent-7fp.15), so this bead's own tests need only the codec. Unblocks: e2e (lnrent-7fp.15), web WASM buyer (lnrent-7fp.18).

## Verification
Integration against a running daemon + local relay + payment-backend mock for the order flow; buyer-core unit-tests the op.request/op.result encode/decode path through the codec. Full ops integration (real op.result from the operator) is proven in lnrent-7fp.15.

## Spec refs
SPEC §3, §4.2, §4.7 (agent-native CLI), §5.1, §7.4, §13 (injection), §14; ADR-0013, ADR-0014; eng-review T13.

## Acceptance Criteria
- buyer-core compiles for BOTH native and wasm32 (no native-only deps in the core path).
- The CLI buyer completes a full loop against a running daemon + local relay + payment-backend mock: order -> the CLI RETURNS the bolt11 -> settlement is performed out-of-band (the test harness/wallet pays; the CLI does not) -> the CLI receives creds. No payer in the client.
- The signer/relay seams are injectable so the web shell can supply NIP-07 / browser-WS; there is no payer seam (the web shell's WebLN/QR is the user's wallet paying, handled in lnrent-7fp.18, not buyer-core).
- Every CLI command supports `--json` (stable fields), is non-interactive, and returns deterministic exit codes; an `order.error` / `op.result` error surfaces as the nested `error{code,message,retryable}` JSON (agent can branch). (Agent-native, ADR-0014.)
- CLI conformance gate: enumerate ALL M1a buyer commands, snapshot each command's `--json` output schema (golden), run every command with no TTY (non-interactive), assert structured stderr per error class, and pin the exit-code taxonomy — so "agent-grade" is tested, not just asserted.
- Provenance (§13, milestone-aware): buyer-core acts only on signature-verified fields — in M1a the operator's account-0 event signature (no manifest; manifest membership + attestations are M5). An injected hostile-prose listing title does NOT alter client behavior.
- Taint (§13, signed != safe): operator-produced payloads (`provision.ready.payload` credentials, `op.result.data`) are treated as OPAQUE data, surfaced distinctly from typed known fields; buyer-core never auto-executes a URL or command from any payload, even a validly-signed one.
- buyer-core exposes an `ops` interface that lists a subscription's declared operations and dispatches a `request`-kind op (op.request -> op.result); the CLI surfaces `ops <sub> list` and `ops <sub> <op>`.
- An `interactive`-kind op is recognized and routed to the deferred Iroh transport seam, not silently dropped.
close_reason: Implemented the minimal buyer CLI (master, clients/core lnrent-buyer-core + clients/cli lnrent-buyer): AI-free agent-native --json CLI (ADR-0014). buyer-core target-agnostic + wasm32-clean (lnrent-wire+serde+async-trait, injected Signer/Relay/Clock); buyer-cli owns the native nostr-sdk relay + 0600 no-clobber key files (secret never printed). Flows: discover provenance-verified NIP-99 listings, order create (returns bolt11, never pays §4.7), order wait, renew, subs status, delivery resend, cancel, ops. Replies correlated by folding sender==operator + request_id (+ subscription_id, + op for op.result) into the gift-wrap matcher so stale/planted wraps are skipped not fatal; optional --request-id makes a retry idempotent vs the operator (sender,id) dedup; broadcast Lagged continues. JSON envelope + stable exit taxonomy. 8 core unit tests + a buyer-cli integration test booting a real Supervisor over an in-process relay + MockPayment (listings->order->settle->provision.ready->ops). Native suite + clippy green; buyer-core wasm32 builds in the nix devshell. Built via watched rb-lite (full panel after a claude session-limit pause; loop codex blind to untracked clients/) — fixed a create_order reply-correlation P0 + 2 P2s (retry idempotency, Lagged); a final codex gate caught the renew/invoke_op correlation P1 (fixed: + subscription_id/op), no remaining P0/P1. Note: operator sub.cancel is sent but currently ignored operator-side.

title: Daemon state backup
description:
## Outcome
A backup mechanism for the daemon's durable state so the operator's commitments AND ecash survive box death: (1) the **sqlite** state (subscriptions, invoices, paid_through, reservations, refund ledger, outbox, op_invocation), (2) the **WHOLE `data_dir/fedimint/<federation_id>/` dir** — the fedimint client's RocksDB (`client.db`) AND the lnrent-owned `lnrent_index.db` (the fedimint_invoice/fedimint_pay idempotency index, fedimint_backend.rs:64/169) — plus the **federation invite/config** (ADR-0015/0012), and (3) the **BIP39 seed** (backed up separately, ADR-0004). All needed to restore — the seed alone does NOT recover the ecash position (it needs the federation config + the client db), and WITHOUT lnrent_index.db a restored backend loses its invoice/pay idempotency anchor (re-mint / double-pay / stranding risk), §4.6.
## Why
ADR-0004 forces seed backup, but losing the sqlite loses who-paid-through-when for a system holding other people's prepaid time, and losing the fedimint client db/config OR the lnrent index risks the ecash position + payment idempotency (eng-review #5; ADR-0015; codex 2026-06-29).
## Scope
a periodic consistent backup of the sqlite (online backup API or litestream-style) AND the whole data_dir/fedimint/<fed_id>/ (client.db + lnrent_index.db) + federation config, operator-configurable destination; a documented restore path that brings back subscription state AND the fedimint client + its lnrent index.
## Non-goals
Multi-box replication/HA (M7); seed backup itself (ADR-0004, separate).
## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1), the Fedimint backend (lnrent-7fp.4, for the client db/index/config locations). P2 (not a ship blocker for the loop, but a REAL-MONEY blocker for lnrent-o6p).
## Verification
Integration: kill+restore from backup reproduces subscription/paid_through/ledger state AND the fedimint client + lnrent_index.db (ecash recoverable from the federation by seed + config; invoice/pay idempotency intact).
## Spec refs
SPEC §4.6, §11, §12; ADR-0004, ADR-0012, ADR-0015; eng-review T14; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Kill + restore from backup reproduces subscription / paid_through / reservation / refund-ledger state (integration).
- The backup includes the WHOLE data_dir/fedimint/<fed_id>/ (client.db RocksDB + lnrent_index.db) + federation invite/config (not just sqlite); restore re-establishes the fedimint client AND its lnrent idempotency index so the ecash position is recoverable and create_invoice/pay stay idempotent (ADR-0015).
close_reason: DONE + merged. PART A cold backup/restore CLI (master cb950b9, via rb-lite 3 rounds: restore-union P1, fsync/atomic P2s, crash-safe-swap P1 via renameat2(RENAME_EXCHANGE), data-dir-resolution P1, self-copy P2 — all cleared; + my fsync-parent fix). PART B live proof (master, this): backup the stopped data dir -> restore to a fresh dir -> reopen FedimintPayment -> prior pay status preserved + ecash SPENDABLE after restore. backup() VACUUM INTOs the state sqlite + raw-copies the closed fedimint/<id>/ (client.db + lnrent_index.db) + config/seed + MANIFEST; restore() staged-then-swapped (atomic, wholesale, no stale union). CLI backup/restore (--json, --force, data_dir resolved as the daemon does). 12 offline tests + 3/3 fedimint_live tests PROVEN on devimint v0.11.1. Remaining minor P2/P3 (lexical-path edge fails loudly; move-aside-fallback test gap; minor hardening) noted.

title: M1a end-to-end loop + money-path tests (ship gate)
description:
## Outcome
The M1a ship gate: an end-to-end test that runs the whole loop on one box against a payment-backend mock (Fedimint test federation) and a local relay with the trivial recipe — publish listing -> order.request -> pre-flight+reserve -> invoice (the buyer CLI RETURNS the bolt11; the harness settles it out-of-band, the client never pays — §4.7) -> idempotent capture -> provision -> provision.ready delivered -> run a buyer management op -> renew -> suspend -> destroy. PLUS the mandatory money-path tests (these gate ship): idempotent-capture replay (no double-extend), settled-but-expired auto-refund (no silent theft), crash recovery (kill between capture and deliver -> recovered), and downtime credit (operator outage doesn't suspend a buyer unfairly).
## Why
M1a's whole purpose is to prove the handshake is correct. These are money paths: if any is wrong and silent, funds vanish or buyers are stranded. They are non-negotiable (eng-review failure-modes; outside-voice; codex). M1a also proves the buyer can MANAGE what they rented through the unified ops interface (ADR-0013), so a minimal request-kind op is part of the gate.
## Scope
the e2e harness (daemon + local relay + payment-backend mock + trivial recipe + CLI buyer); the four mandatory money-path tests; the minimal buyer-op proof (a request-kind op through op.request/op.result + a refused unauthorized op); CI wiring.
## Non-goals
VM/Incus, Iroh/Tor, tiers (M1b/M1c); interactive/Iroh ops (M1b+).
## Constraints
deterministic via the injectable clock; no real funds.
## Dependencies
Depends on: reservation, capture, reconcile, provision+outbox, refund, IPC, buyer, AND the operator-side op dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20) for the buyer-op proof. This is the milestone-completion bead.
## Verification
This bead IS the verification. Acceptance signal: the full loop test + all four money-path tests + the buyer-op proof pass green in CI.
## Spec refs
SPEC §6.6, §7.4, §15 (M1a); ADR-0003/0005/0009/0013; eng-review failure modes + Implementation Tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full loop test green: list -> order -> reserve -> invoice -> out-of-band wallet settlement (harness settles; client does not pay) -> capture -> provision -> deliver -> renew -> suspend -> destroy.
- Money-path 1 (idempotent capture): a duplicate settlement extends paid_through exactly once.
- Money-path 2 (settled-but-expired): a late settlement auto-refunds, no provision, no kept funds.
- Money-path 3 (crash recovery): killing the daemon after capture, mid-provision, and after ACTIVE-before-deliver each recovers correctly.
- Money-path 4 (downtime credit): an operator outage across a renewal window does NOT suspend the buyer on restart.
- Ops path: after provision.ready, the buyer runs a request-kind op (status/restart) via the CLI and receives a matching `op.result` (request_id correlated); a DUPLICATE op.request (same id) resends the cached result and the non-idempotent `restart` hook runs exactly once; an op.request from a non-buyer key is refused (`unauthorized`, no hook run, no subscription-existence leak).
- All deterministic via TestClock, no real funds, wired into CI.

## Scope note (codex)
This is the CLI/daemon money-path gate (the loop proven via the CLI buyer). The web WASM buyer (lnrent-7fp.18) is the final browser-access proof and runs AFTER this gate; M1a is complete only when BOTH lnrent-7fp.15 and lnrent-7fp.18 are green.

## Payment backend = MockPayment (codex next-steps review)
The e2e/money-path gate runs against **MockPayment** (daemon/src/backends.rs) — a deterministic in-memory PaymentBackend — NOT a live/test Fedimint federation. Any spec wording implying a 'Fedimint test federation' here means the mock for M1a. Also: this gate REQUIRES the production `lnrentd` runtime wiring (see the new daemon-runtime-wiring bead) to be in place — store + recipe + IPC alone is not a runnable money path.
close_reason: M1a e2e money-path ship gate DONE (master ee16215, tests-only): daemon/tests/e2e_money_path.rs (15 tests via the Supervisor seams, deterministic crash points, exact row/state + negative assertions) + clients/cli/tests/money_gate.rs (1 CLI golden path via the real lnrent-buyer subprocess). Covers the full loop end-to-end: order->invoice->settle->provision (ACTIVE once), renewal extend, soft-date reminder (one auto-invoice), suspend->terminate, provision-failure refund, settled-but-terminal auto-refund, duplicate-settlement idempotency, late-settlement g5p refund, the crash/restart matrix (catch-up/PROVISIONING re-drive/outbox drain/mid-refund no-double-pay/orphaned-op recovery), op status/dup-restart/unauthorized, and downtime credit. 16/16 green in <1s (no hangs — deterministic), clippy clean, no product changes. Built via watched rb-lite (panel clean after determinism-hardening). M1a is SHIPPABLE on MockPayment.

title: Operator identity + config bootstrap
description:
## Outcome
Bootstrap the operator: from a BIP39 seed derive the account-0 Nostr identity (NIP-06, m/44'/1237'/0'/0/0), persist the operator row (master_pubkey, op_pubkey, box_index=0), and load runtime config — the Fedimint federation invite + gateway, and the default relay set. Enough for the daemon to sign listings/DMs and receive payments in M1a.

## Why
The handshake cannot run without an operator identity + backend/relay config. M1a uses a single account-0 key (the master/per-box split + operator manifest are M5, ADR-0004). Without this nothing can sign a 30402 or open the Fedimint client / gateway connection.

## Scope
seed -> account-0 key (NIP-06) via rust-nostr; operator-row persistence through the store actor; load relays + the Fedimint federation invite + gateway from config / data dir. **Non-interactive (ADR-0014, §4.7):** onboarding runs headless — seed + config from flags / env / a config file / stdin, never a required interactive prompt — so an OPERATOR AGENT can bootstrap a box without a human. (The lnrent-onboard skill is a human convenience on top; the CLI path must work prompt-free.)

## Non-goals
Per-box operational keys + operator manifest (M5); the lnrent-onboard skill UX.

## Constraints / failure handling
single key in M1a (account 0 = master, used directly); seed/key live in the data dir with tight perms, never logged; a headless bootstrap with missing/invalid config fails with a deterministic nonzero exit + structured error, not a prompt.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Used by the Nostr engine + payment config at wire-up. Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Unit: deterministic derivation from a fixed seed (Nostr account-0 AND the Fedimint root secret). Integration: a fully non-interactive bootstrap from flags/env/config produces a ready operator with no prompt.

## Spec refs
ADR-0004, ADR-0012, ADR-0014; SPEC §4.6, §4.7, §11 (operator table).

## Acceptance Criteria
- From a fixed test seed, the account-0 npub is deterministic and matches a NIP-06 derivation vector.
- The operator row is persisted (master_pubkey, op_pubkey, box_index=0) via the store actor.
- The daemon signs and publishes a 30402 listing with the derived key.
- the Fedimint federation invite + gateway, and default relays, load from config.
- Bootstrap completes fully non-interactively (flags/env/config/stdin, no prompt); a missing-config run fails with a structured error + nonzero exit (ADR-0014).

## Seed derivation + backup for the payment backend (codex)
- The **Fedimint client root secret** derives from the operator seed via **HKDF-SHA256 with the fixed info string `lnrent:fedimint:v1`** (disjoint from the NIP-06 Nostr paths so the key spaces can't collide), deterministic from a fixed seed (test vector) — so one seed regenerates identity AND the ecash client (ADR-0004/0012, §4.6).
- **The seed alone is not a full ecash backup:** restoring the ecash position also needs the **federation invite/config** (which federation to rejoin). The daemon stores it in the data dir and onboard's backup step must include it — assert backup completeness, not just seed capture.

## M1a mock-mode (codex next-steps review)
M1a uses MockPayment, not a live federation (the .4 decision). Config bootstrap MUST support `payment_backend=mock` with NO federation invite / gateway required — a Fedimint invite is only needed once the real .4 backend lands. The operator account-0 Nostr key from this bead is what wires into the .5 engine (replacing test keys).
close_reason: Operator identity + config bootstrap implemented (daemon/src/{identity,config}.rs + main.rs bootstrap subcommand): NIP-06 account-0 key (m/44'/1237'/0'/0/0) + PROVISIONAL Fedimint root secret (HKDF-SHA256 'lnrent:fedimint:v1'), both with pinned test vectors (codex-verified vs the NIP-06 spec + leader-monkey vector); headless multi-source config (flags>env>file>stdin precedence) with a never-prompts bootstrap entrypoint + structured errors/nonzero exit; mock-mode (no federation needed); idempotent singleton operator-row persistence; Zeroizing secrets, 0600 files, foreign-owned/symlinked/unsafe data-dir rejection. Built via rb-lite (claude,codex) + 2 fix rounds + direct hardening; final codex GO. 173 workspace tests. Commits on master through 9669fa3.

title: Order intake + invoice issuance
description:
## Outcome
The order-intake + invoice-issuance integrator that owns ADR-0009's "the PENDING subscription IS the order." Two paths:
- New order: on `order.request` (carrying a client `id` + `listing_id`), validate params + check the listing price is current, pre-flight/reserve capacity (lnrent-7fp.7); derive `external_id` deterministically from `(sender_pubkey, request_id)`; call the payment backend `create_invoice(external_id)` (idempotent on external_id) to GET the bolt11; THEN in ONE store transaction insert the subscription PENDING + the invoice OPEN (with bolt11 + backend ids) + the cached `inbound_request` response; send `order.invoice` only AFTER commit (or a structured `order.error` + release the reservation on failure).
- Renewal invoice: at soft_date (driven by reconcile, lnrent-7fp.9) or on `renew.request` (carrying a client `id`), issue a renewal invoice with a **deterministic external_id** — `renew:req:<sender>:<request_id>` for a buyer-requested renewal, `renew:auto:<subscription_id>:<cycle_anchor>` for a daemon soft-date renewal (cycle_anchor = the paid_through being renewed, so one cycle yields exactly one invoice even across restarts, §6.6) — and send `billing.invoice`.

## Why
Launch review: no bead owned the order->invoice orchestration. ADR-0009 makes the PENDING sub the durable order that settlement correlates to via external_id. NIP-17 has no delivery guarantee, so a retried `order.request`/`renew.request` must NOT create a second reservation/order/invoice (codex).

## Scope
order.request/renew.request handling; the create-PENDING-sub + OPEN-invoice transaction; create_invoice with external_id; sending order.invoice/order.error/billing.invoice via the Nostr transport (lnrent-7fp.5); renewal-invoice issuance.
- **Inbound idempotency (§5.1):** dedup on `inbound_request (sender_pubkey, request_id)` — a duplicate request resends the cached `order.invoice`/`order.error`/`billing.invoice`, never creating a second reservation/order/invoice.
- **listing_id** is the NIP-99 addressable coordinate `30402:<pubkey>:<d>` (§5.4); a stale-price order (referenced listing's price != current) is rejected with `order.error {code:"price_changed"}`.
- **Structured order.error (§5.1):** `order.error {request_id, order_id?, error:{code,message,retryable}}` — `order_id` is OPTIONAL (absent for a pre-order validation failure); `code` in `capacity_full`/`params_invalid`/`price_changed`/`unavailable`/`rejected`. The response carries `request_id` so the buyer correlates it before any order_id exists.

## Non-goals
Settlement capture (lnrent-7fp.8); provisioning (lnrent-7fp.10); the DM transport primitives (lnrent-7fp.5); deadline firing (lnrent-7fp.9).

## Constraints / failure handling
PENDING sub + OPEN invoice + the `inbound_request` cached response are created in ONE transaction, so there is NO in-flight gap: either the response is durably cached (a retry resends it) or nothing was created (a retry redoes it cleanly). On failure send a structured order.error and release the reservation, leaving no dangling PENDING sub; external_id unique per invoice; a reservation orphaned by a crash before the commit is released on its TTL (§9.3), so a clean retry never leaks capacity.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1, for inbound_request), the payment backend (lnrent-7fp.4), Nostr transport (lnrent-7fp.5), reservation (lnrent-7fp.7). Unblocks: reconcile renewal (lnrent-7fp.9), e2e (lnrent-7fp.15).

## Verification
Integration over a local relay + payment-backend mock.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §5.4, §6.2, §6.4, §6.6; ADR-0009, ADR-0014.

## Acceptance Criteria
- order.request -> a PENDING subscription + an OPEN invoice (unique external_id) are created in one transaction and order.invoice (carrying request_id + order_id) is sent with the bolt11.
- Invalid params / insufficient capacity / stale price -> a structured `order.error {request_id, error:{code,...}}` (`params_invalid`/`capacity_full`/`price_changed`) is sent + reservation released; no dangling PENDING sub; a pre-order failure carries NO order_id.
- At soft_date or on renew.request, a renewal invoice is issued and billing.invoice is sent.
- A duplicate order.request OR renew.request (same sender_pubkey + request_id) does not create a second order/invoice; it resends the cached response from `inbound_request` (also the dropped-response recovery).

## Ordering / atomicity (codex)
The `bolt11` comes FROM `create_invoice`, so it cannot be cached before that call. Correct order: (1) dedup on inbound_request (a hit resends the cached response); (2) reserve capacity (lnrent-7fp.7); (3) derive `external_id` deterministically from `(sender_pubkey, request_id)` and call `create_invoice(external_id)` — idempotent on external_id, so a retry returns the same invoice; (4) in ONE store transaction insert PENDING sub + OPEN invoice (with bolt11 + backend ids) + the cached `inbound_request` response; (5) send the DM AFTER commit. A crash after (3) before (4) leaves an orphaned backend invoice never bound to a committed order — it expires unpaid, harmless; a retry regenerates the same external_id and create_invoice returns that same invoice (no duplicate). If create_invoice or persistence fails, RELEASE the reservation, leaving no dangling PENDING sub or stuck reservation.
close_reason: Order intake + invoice issuance implemented (daemon/src/order_intake.rs): the OrderHandler the .5 engine routes order/renew DMs to. Dedup-first idempotency on inbound_request; validate params + current-listing price; reserve (.7); deterministic external_id + MockPayment.create_invoice; ONE-txn PENDING sub + OPEN invoice + cached response + reservation-TTL finalize; order.invoice after commit; structured order.error + correct reservation release on failure. Renewal issuance (buyer + soft-date) with deterministic external_id, owner/state/retention gating. Built via a bounded single rb-lite implementer pass (wrapper crashed; orphan finished) + a 6-pass codex-review-loop hardening the money path. 135 tests green. Deferred: settlement-time invoice-expiry enforcement -> capture (lnrent-g5p). Commits 67b61f3 + review-fix.

title: Web WASM buyer (browser marketplace access)
description:
## Outcome
A static **web (WASM) buyer**: buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13) compiled to wasm32 inside a minimal browser SPA so the marketplace is usable from a website with no backend. Browse NIP-99 listings, place an order, pay the Lightning invoice, receive credentials — and run the browser-feasible subset of the service-management `ops` interface — all in the browser.

## Why
The two-sided marketplace needs a website, not just a CLI. A WASM client reuses the one Rust protocol implementation (no duplicate JS protocol), and static hosting preserves the no-central-server property (§1): the site is a pure buyer front-end over relays + the buyer's wallet. The same site should also let a buyer manage what they rented (ADR-0013), at least for request/response ops. This is the **human** surface (ADR-0014): agents use the buyer CLI (lnrent-7fp.13), not this SPA, and the SPA stays static — it does NOT expose an HTTP/agent API (that would reintroduce a server and break the no-central-server property).

## Scope
wasm32 build of buyer-core (wasm-pack/trunk); a minimal SPA (browse listings, order form, invoice view, creds view, and a service-management view); browser glue — NIP-07 (window.nostr) signer, and an **invoice hand-off to the user's wallet** (present the bolt11 to `window.webln` / show QR / copy) with NO payer trait and NO autonomous client payment — the SPA never pays, the user's wallet does (ADR-0014, §4.7); relays over browser WebSockets; the `request`-kind ops subset (op.request/op.result over the same buyer-core); static hosting.

## Non-goals
Operator/seller UI (operator side stays daemon/CLI/skills); a hosted backend OR an HTTP/agent API (must stay static — agents use the CLI, ADR-0014); embedded custodial wallet; the VM access/reachability plane (separate from buying); `interactive`-kind ops (Iroh shell/console/logs -f, §9.2) — out of browser scope for M1a (the buyer-core seam exists but the web shell does not wire an Iroh transport).

## Critical constraints / risks
The rust-nostr-on-wasm32 gift-wrap gate is already PROVEN (the .19 codec spike + flake.nix; build inside `nix develop`). NIP-07 / WebLN need a compatible extension/wallet -> ship the copy-bolt11/QR fallback. No secrets in the static bundle.

## Dependencies
Depends on: buyer-core + CLI buyer (lnrent-7fp.13) and a working loop to test against (lnrent-7fp.15, which also gates the operator-side op dispatch). Follows the CLI buyer.

## Verification
A headless-browser e2e drives the SPA through the full loop, including a request-kind management op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §1, §3, §4.7 (human surface, no agent API; invoice hand-off), §5.1, §7.4, §9.2, §14; ADR-0013, ADR-0014; eng-review (web client, formerly M4).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The SPA loads as static files (no backend) and connects to relays over browser WebSockets.
- A headless-browser test completes the full buyer loop: browse a listing -> order.request -> the SPA presents the invoice and hands it off to the user's wallet (a MOCKED `window.webln` extension settles it out-of-band — the SPA itself does not pay) -> receive provision.ready -> display credentials.
- Signing uses NIP-07 (embedded-key fallback, export-prompted since it decrypts creds); the invoice is handed to the user's wallet via WebLN, else shown as copy-bolt11 / QR — the SPA holds no wallet credentials and runs no payer.
- A buyer with NEITHER NIP-07 nor WebLN completes the loop via embedded key + copy-bolt11/QR hand-off, paying from their own phone wallet (the headless-browser test covers this fallback path).
- The SPA runs a `request`-kind management op (e.g. status) on an active subscription and displays op.result.data.
- buyer-core is shared with the CLI (lnrent-7fp.13) -- no duplicated protocol logic.

## Capability degradation (grill)
Detect and degrade: signing prefers NIP-07, else an embedded key the SPA generates + persists (and prompts to export/back up, since it decrypts credentials); the invoice hand-off prefers WebLN, else copy-bolt11/QR — either way the user's own wallet pays, the SPA never does. A buyer with no Nostr extension and no WebLN wallet — just a phone Lightning wallet — can still complete the loop.
close_reason: DONE + acceptance proven: web WASM buyer (clients/web) built across 5 steps (scaffold+Clock 2ae0e1a, relay+?Send seam bba7a5c, signer+flows 0ce3977, static shell 5ee4ca4, dev-settle 1fb9848 + headless e2e). Both acceptance scenarios GREEN in real headless Chrome: full loop (connect->discover->order->WebLN handoff->dev-settle->credentials->request-kind op) AND the no-NIP-07/no-WebLN QR/copy fallback. Reuses buyer-core (no protocol dup), static-hosted, SPA never pays.

title: lnrent wire-codec lib (DM types + gift-wrap + 30402)
description:
## Outcome
A shared, target-agnostic Rust lib for the lnrent wire protocol: the DM message types (order.request + renew.request carrying a client `id`; order.invoice, order.error + billing.invoice carrying the matching `request_id`; provision.ready, delivery.resend.request, billing.notice, billing.refund, sub.cancel, op.request, op.result) as typed structs with JSON ser/de; NIP-17 gift-wrap (NIP-59/44) encode/decode of those messages; and the NIP-99 (30402) listing build/parse, INCLUDING the recipe's published operation declarations (name/label/kind/params — NOT `hook`) so a buyer can discover the ops surface from a listing (§5.4, §7.4). Pure codec, no I/O. Compiles native AND wasm32. This bead PINS the on-wire schema (§5.4 listing layout + DM payloads, incl. op.request `id` and op.result `request_id`).

## Why
Both the OPERATOR Nostr engine (lnrent-7fp.5) and the BUYER buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13) must speak the exact same wire format — including the service-management op.request/op.result pair (§7.4, ADR-0013). Owning it once stops operator/buyer drift and removes the wrong coupling of buyer-core depending on the operator's daemon engine. wasm32 support lets the web buyer (lnrent-7fp.18) reuse it.

## Scope
message-type structs + JSON ser/de (incl. op.request/op.result); gift-wrap encode/decode (rust-nostr NIP-59/44); 30402 listing build/parse (incl. the recipe's `[[operation]]` declarations — name/label/kind/params); stable per-message id for idempotent dedupe. Pure functions — no relay/network/signer/payment I/O (those are seams in the consumers).

## Non-goals
Relay connections, signing, payment (consumers inject those); operator manifest (M5). The op DISPATCH logic (auth, running hooks) is the operator-side bead lnrent-7fp.20 and the buyer-side bead lnrent-7fp.13 — this bead is only the typed op.request/op.result codec they share.

## Constraints / risks
Must compile to wasm32 — the EARLIEST place to run the rust-nostr gift-wrap-on-wasm gate (also gates lnrent-7fp.18). **PROVEN (2026-06-24):** nostr (nip44/nip59) + secp256k1-sys + getrandom/js compiles for wasm32 in the nix devshell (flake.nix); secp's C cross-compile needs an unwrapped clang (CC_wasm32_unknown_unknown). Build wasm inside `nix develop`.

## Dependencies
None (root). Unblocks: operator Nostr engine (lnrent-7fp.5), buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13), operator op dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20).

## Verification
Unit: encode->decode round-trips identically for every message type (including op.request/op.result), including through NIP-17 gift-wrap; a 30402 listing builds and parses; the crate builds for native AND wasm32.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §5.3, §5.4 (listing op-declaration schema), §7.4; ADR-0009, ADR-0013.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every DM message type round-trips through JSON and through NIP-17 gift-wrap (encode->decode == identity), including op.request (with `id`) and the two error carriers — op.result and order.error — which use the SAME nested `error{code,message,retryable}` shape (NOT a top-level code), so agents branch uniformly (ADR-0014). The codec pins one shape; it does not infer both.
- A NIP-99 30402 listing builds and parses (the `listing_id` is the addressable coordinate `30402:<pubkey>:<d>`, §5.4), and round-trips the recipe's published operation declarations (name/label/kind/params; `hook` is never serialized into a listing) — build->parse == identity, with a schema `version` field and unknown-field tolerance.
- The crate compiles for native AND wasm32 (no native-only deps).
- Each message carries a stable id for idempotent dedupe; op.result.request_id correlates to the op.request id.
close_reason: lnrent-wire codec implemented (wire/ crate): typed NIP-17 DM messages + JSON serde, NIP-59/44 gift-wrap over a NostrSigner seam, NIP-99 30402 listing build/parse with op declarations. nostr 0.44; compiles native AND wasm32 (verified from source in the nix devshell); 30 tests. Built via rb-lite (8-round claude,codex implement/review loop, panel clean) + final codex xhigh review GO (P2 outer-wrap verification + 2 P3s fixed). Commits 23ca4b1/bf8ac4a.

title: Injectable clock for deterministic time
description:
## Outcome
An injectable time source (a Clock trait with now() returning unix seconds) used everywhere deadlines/timestamps are computed, so every time-based transition is deterministic under test.

## Why
The reconcile-loop state machine, invoice expiry, paid_through/soft_date, and downtime credit all key on wall-clock time. Without an injectable clock none of those paths are unit-testable.

## Scope
A Clock trait + a real SystemClock + a TestClock (settable/advanceable). Wire store and reconcile to take a Clock.

## Non-goals
Monotonic-vs-wall subtleties beyond the NTP assumption (a §16 minor risk).

## Dependencies
None (foundational root). Unblocks: store (lnrent-7fp.1), reconcile, and every time-based test.

## Verification
Unit: TestClock advance drives a fake deadline transition without sleeping.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.5; eng-review T11.

## Acceptance Criteria
- TestClock can set and advance time; a reconcile deadline fires under TestClock with no real sleep.
- SystemClock returns wall-clock unix seconds.
- store and reconcile take a Clock by injection (no direct std time calls in those paths).

title: Buyer management-op dispatch (op.request -> recipe hook -> op.result)
description:
## Outcome
Operator-side dispatch of buyer-facing management operations (SPEC.md §7.4, ADR-0013). The daemon receives an `op.request` NIP-17 DM `{id, subscription_id, op, params}`, authorizes it, runs the recipe's declared `request`-kind operation hook, and returns `op.result {request_id, status, data|error}`. This is the operator half of the unified buyer `ops` interface; the buyer half is buyer-core (lnrent-7fp.13).

## Why
A buyer must manage the service they rented (WireGuard get-config, Fedimint admin-url/dkg, Hermes config/exec, and stop/restart/status everywhere) through ONE interface, without a per-service client and without arbitrary host shell. Recipes declare a narrow management surface; the daemon dispatches it deterministically. AI-free: every op runs a recipe hook, never an LLM (ADR-0013, §4.1).

## Scope
- Inbound `op.request` handling on top of the Nostr engine's DM primitives (lnrent-7fp.5), decoded via the wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19).
- **Authorization**: load the subscription via the store actor (lnrent-7fp.1); accept only if the DM sender pubkey == the subscription's `buyer_pubkey`, the op is declared by the subscription's recipe, and the subscription state permits it (M1a: ACTIVE only; suspended/terminated refuse — a per-op "available-when-suspended" manifest flag is a post-M1a refinement, not built here).
- **Durable idempotency (CRITICAL, codex)**: before running, upsert an `op_invocation` row keyed unique on `(sender_pubkey, request_id)` (the op.request `id`) via the store actor (lnrent-7fp.1). A declared op may be NON-idempotent (e.g. `restart`), so a duplicate `op.request` — NIP-17 redelivery, buyer retry after a dropped `op.result`, or daemon restart mid-op — MUST resend the cached (DONE/ERROR) or in-flight (RUNNING) result and MUST NOT re-run the hook. `op.result.request_id` correlates the reply.
- **Dispatch (request-kind only)**: resolve `Recipe::operation(op)`, run its `ops/<hook>` via the recipe runner (lnrent-7fp.6) with the lifecycle-hook I/O contract (JSON stdin: subscription + instance + validated op params + host facts; JSON stdout = `op.result.data`), run OFF the reconcile loop. The `hook` is a bare name resolved to `ops/<hook>`; reject any non-bare hook (traversal) — `Operation::hook_is_safe` / `Recipe::validate` (lnrent-7fp.6) is the gate.
- **Structured errors**: on failure return `op.result {status:"error", error:{code, message, retryable}}` with no `data`. `code` ∈ `unauthorized | unknown_op | invalid_params | not_active | timeout | hook_failed`; cap/redact hook stderr; an `unauthorized` reply must NOT reveal whether the `subscription_id` exists (someone else's sub and a nonexistent sub are indistinguishable to a non-buyer).
- Emit `op.result` back over NIP-17; the `op_invocation` row IS the durable audit record (op, subscription, sender, status, cached result/error).
- M1a proves this with the trivial recipe's minimal op(s) (`status` idempotent, `restart` non-idempotent — exercises the dedupe path).

## Non-goals
`interactive`-kind ops (shell / console / `logs -f` / file copy over the Iroh Native-connect session, §9.2) — those arrive with reachability (M1b+). Rich per-service ops (WireGuard get-config, Fedimint dkg, Hermes exec) ship with their recipes (M1b/M2). The buyer-side ops surface is lnrent-7fp.13.

## Constraints / failure handling
Op-param validation mirrors order-param validation (reject unknown/missing). A hook timeout/nonzero exit -> `op.result {status:error}`, never a daemon wedge (reuse the recipe runner's timeout + output-cap). Unauthorized or unknown-op requests get `op.result {status:error}` WITHOUT running any hook, and are logged. Op idempotency is keyed on `(sender_pubkey, op.request.id)` in `op_invocation` (NOT transport message-id dedupe): DONE/ERROR resends the cached result, a live RUNNING attaches to the in-flight task. **Startup recovery:** an `op_invocation` left `RUNNING` by a crash mid-op has no live task, so on daemon start it is converted to a terminal `error {code:"interrupted", retryable:false}` (effect unknown — neither re-run nor reported success); a later duplicate resends that, and the buyer may reissue under a NEW `id`.

## Dependencies
Depends on: wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19, for op.request `id` / op.result `request_id`), Nostr engine (lnrent-7fp.5, inbound DM + reply primitives), recipe runner (lnrent-7fp.6, runs the op hook + validates operation declarations incl. hook-name safety), store actor (lnrent-7fp.1, `op_invocation` idempotency + subscription lookup for auth). Unblocks: the e2e op proof in lnrent-7fp.15.

## Verification
Integration over a local relay + the trivial recipe: an authorized `op.request` runs the hook and returns `op.result`; a DUPLICATE op.request (same sender+id) resends the cached result and the hook runs exactly once (proven with non-idempotent `restart`); an `op.request` from a non-buyer pubkey is refused without running the hook and without leaking subscription existence; an undeclared op name is refused; a hook that times out / exits nonzero returns a structured `op.result` error.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1 (op.request/op.result, durable idempotency), §7.4, §11 (op_invocation); ADR-0013; ADR-0001 (daemon sole writer), ADR-0002 (recipes trusted code).

## Acceptance Criteria
- An authorized `op.request {id, subscription_id, op:"status", params:{}}` runs the trivial recipe's `ops/status` and returns `op.result {request_id, status:"ok", data:...}`.
- A duplicate `op.request` (same sender_pubkey + `id`) returns the SAME cached `op.result` and the hook runs exactly once — verified with non-idempotent `restart`.
- An invocation left `RUNNING` by a crash mid-op is recovered at startup to a terminal `error {code:"interrupted", retryable:false}` WITHOUT re-running; a duplicate then resends that cached error (tested).
- An `op.request` whose DM sender != the subscription's `buyer_pubkey` is refused with `op.result {status:"error", error.code:"unauthorized"}`, runs NO hook, and does not reveal whether the subscription exists.
- An `op.request` naming an op the recipe does not declare is refused (`unknown_op`, no hook run); a hook with a non-bare (traversal) path is rejected by validation, never executed.
- A management hook that exceeds its timeout or exits nonzero yields a structured `op.result` error (`timeout` / `hook_failed`) and does not wedge the reconcile loop.
- Every invocation is recorded durably in `op_invocation` (op, subscription, sender pubkey, state, cached result/error).
- M1a scope: only `request`-kind ops, ACTIVE subscriptions only; interactive/Iroh ops and the suspended-op `allowed_states` flag are explicitly out of scope here.
close_reason: Implemented in daemon/src/op_dispatch.rs (master, OpDispatch impl OpHandler): durable idempotency on (sender_pubkey, request_id) via op_invocation — claim RUNNING before the recipe hook, hook OUTSIDE the txn, commit DONE/ERROR after; duplicate resends the cached op.result (labeled with the STORED sub/op) and never re-runs the hook (proven with non-idempotent restart); concurrent RUNNING defers (no in-flight registry); recover_interrupted_ops flips orphaned RUNNING->interrupted at startup (for .21). Auth: sender==buyer_pubkey + ACTIVE + recipe-declared request-kind op; unauthorized/not_active/unknown_op/invalid_params return a structured op.result error, no hook, no existence leak. Hook stdin mirrors the lifecycle-hook contract (subscription+instance+params+host). Hook failures cached as op.result errors with redacted buyer messages, never a wedge. 8 tests; full suite+clippy green. Built via watched rb-lite (loop codex was blind to the untracked file; claude verified sound) + 2 independent codex gates (1st found 3: instance-context P1 + cached-metadata P2 fixed, is_timeout P3 + test; 2nd: no remaining P0/P1). Deferred: lnrent-c95 (in-flight RUNNING-attach, blocked by .21), lnrent-xjn (op_invocation/inbound_request retention/GC). Production wiring (run_inbound injection + startup recovery) is .21.

title: Daemon runtime wiring + task supervisor
description:
## Outcome
The production `lnrentd` main(): construct every subsystem and spawn the supervised concurrent tasks that make the money path actually run. Today daemon/src/main.rs only opens the store, loads recipes, and serves operator IPC — nothing wires the Nostr engine, payment watch, capture, provision, outbox, reconcile, refund, or op-dispatch together, so there is no runnable end-to-end daemon (codex next-steps review gap).

## Why
Each integrator bead (.5/.8/.10/.9/.11/.17/.20) owns one piece but not the assembly. The e2e ship gate (.15) needs a single runnable process that boots them all with one config + one operator key and keeps the long-lived loops alive. Without this bead .15 would secretly absorb a large, untested integration deliverable.

## Scope
- Load operator config + account-0 key (.16); open the store (.1); select the payment backend (MockPayment for M1a, .4 later).
- Construct the Nostr engine (.5) with the real signer + relays, injecting the order handler (.17) and op handler (.20) and the Outbound reply primitive.
- Spawn + SUPERVISE the long-lived tasks (restart on failure, structured shutdown): Nostr inbound routing (.5), payment watch -> capture (.8), provision + outbox sender (.10), reconcile loop (.9), refund executor (.11). On startup run the durable recovery each loop needs (re-drive PROVISIONING, drain unsent outbox, retry PENDING refunds, ERROR orphaned RUNNING op_invocations).
- Graceful shutdown: stop accepting, let in-flight commit, flush.

## Non-goals
The business logic inside each subsystem (their own beads). The e2e test itself (.15).

## Dependencies
Depends on the integrators it assembles: .16, .17, .10, .9, .11, .20 (and the already-done .5/.8/.1/.12). Unblocks: .15 ship gate.

## Verification
A smoke test: boot the wired daemon against an in-process relay + MockPayment + the dummy recipe, confirm it publishes a listing and a full order.request -> ... -> provision.ready completes; kill + restart mid-flight and confirm recovery (the durable loops re-drive). The exhaustive money-path assertions live in .15.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `lnrentd` boots from one config + the operator account-0 key (.16), selects MockPayment for M1a, and serves operator IPC AND the Nostr engine concurrently.
- The supervised loops (Nostr inbound, payment-watch->capture, provision+outbox, reconcile, refund) are all spawned; a panic/error in one restarts it without taking down the process.
- On startup the durable recovery runs: PROVISIONING re-driven, unsent outbox drained, PENDING refunds retried, orphaned RUNNING op_invocations marked ERROR.
- Smoke test: against an in-process relay + MockPayment + the dummy recipe, the daemon publishes its listing and a full order.request -> order.invoice -> (pay) -> provision.ready completes; killing the daemon mid-handshake and restarting recovers (no lost/duplicated effect).
- Graceful shutdown drains in-flight work and flushes; `cargo test` + `cargo clippy` clean.
close_reason: Implemented daemon/src/supervisor.rs + main.rs wiring (master ca38be2): constructs every subsystem from .16 config + account-0 key, selects MockPayment (fails on fedimint), runs the money path as a supervised daemon. Boot recovery (recover_interrupted_ops -> provision recover -> settlement catch-up [scans OPEN order+renewal, lookup, capture-if-Paid, conservative settle-time capped at the binding boundary incl. renewal paid_through+retention_s] -> refunder.drive -> reconcile catch-up -> outbox drain). Supervised loops (IPC, Nostr inbound, settlement watch->capture, reconcile, maintenance) under a restart supervisor (capped-backoff restart that resets after a healthy run; shared shutdown; draining IPC+handler wrappers). MockPayment.set_now seeded+synced to the daemon clock. Listing published+upserted. Graceful shutdown drains+flushes.
