Smoke test boots the wired daemon over an in-process relay + MockPayment + dummy recipe, proving order->invoice->settle->provision.ready + crash/restart recovery. Full suite (192 unit + supervisor smoke) + clippy green. Built via watched rb-lite (recovered from a double-run mishap to a clean base) + 1 codex gate that caught a renewal retention-boundary settle-cap P1 (fixed) + no remaining P0/P1. Deferred: complete nostr-inbound shutdown drain -> lnrent-k82 (needs a nostr_engine API; durability covers it for M1a).

title: Downtime credit on restart (heartbeat + outage-window deadline shift)
description:
## Outcome
On restart, CREDIT operator downtime so a buyer is never suspended for the operator's outage
(ADR-0005 revision; home-lab boxes reboot). Persist a heartbeat (the `daemon_state` row) on the
reconcile tick. On restart, compute the outage window `[last_heartbeat, now]`; for any subscription
whose suspend/destroy deadline (`next_deadline`) fell INSIDE that window, SHIFT it forward by the
outage duration and re-send its reminder. A deadline OUTSIDE the window is NOT shifted.

## Why
Operator boxes reboot; without downtime credit a buyer gets suspended for the OPERATOR's outage, not
their own non-payment. Split out of the core reconcile bead (.9) because it is a distinct
restart-resilience feature, not part of the mock money-path demo — and it was where the un-bounded
.9 attempt accumulated much of its complexity.

## Scope
heartbeat persist on the tick; the restart outage-window computation + deadline shift + reminder
re-send. Re-key safely: shifting a deadline must NOT orphan an already-issued renewal invoice keyed
on the old paid_through and re-issue a DUPLICATE auto-renewal invoice (a real bug the .9 review
found, esp. for renew_lead_s == 0, where the reminder marker keys on paid_through) — one cycle still
yields exactly one invoice (§6.6).

## Non-goals
The core reconcile tick + deadline transitions (.9); settlement (.8); refund (.11).

## Dependencies
Depends on: the core reconcile (.9). Does NOT block the e2e ship gate (.15) — downtime credit is
M1a-resilience, not the mock money-path demo (like .14/.18).

## Acceptance Criteria (unit, TestClock)
- A suspend deadline that fell in the recorded outage window is SHIFTED by the outage; one OUTSIDE
  the window is NOT shifted; missed deadlines fire on restart.
- The deadline shift does NOT re-issue a duplicate renewal invoice (the renew_lead_s==0 double-issue
  guard): one cycle, one invoice.

## Spec refs
ADR-0005 (+ revision); SPEC §6.5.
close_reason: Downtime credit on restart DONE (master, daemon/src/{reconcile,capture,order_intake,supervisor,store}.rs + docs): a nullable subscription.suspend_not_before floor set on restart for ACTIVE subs whose renewal window overlapped the outage [last_heartbeat,now], giving the buyer their full renew_lead window of operator-availability before suspension. paid_through NEVER moved (prepaid + renew:auto invoice anchor) -> renewal math + dup-invoice guard trivial; floor self-expires + cleared on renewal. The credited boundary B = max(paid_through,suspend_not_before)+retention_s is honored uniformly by reconcile (suspend/destroy/auto-invoice-expiry), capture (refund gate), order_intake (renew.request/load_renewable), and the .21 settlement catch-up. Boot recovery credits BEFORE catch-up; heartbeat monotonic; credit path mints no invoices (backend-down-at-boot can't lose credit). Comprehensively unit-tested (interior/repeat/long outage, soft-date-only, zero-lead, no-op, boot order, cross-module consistency); full suite + clippy green; codex gate = no P0/P1. Designed with the user after the first cut revealed the cascade (capture+order_intake+catch-up all consume the boundary). Deferred: SUSPENDED-subs' destroy credit (lnrent-d6n); polish nits (post-reminder auto-invoice expiry refresh, migration hygiene) (lnrent-ux7) — no money/correctness bugs. ADR-0005 + SPEC §6.5/§11 updated to the floor design.

title: sqlite migrations (user_version)
description:
## Outcome
A minimal schema-version/migration mechanism (sqlite user_version) so the schema can evolve past M0 without a painful first column change.

## Why
store.rs currently applies CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with no versioning; the first real migration would be ad hoc. Codex flagged this.

## Scope
A migrations runner keyed on PRAGMA user_version; M1a is version 1 (the current §11 schema).

## Non-goals
Down-migrations.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: any later schema change.

## Verification
Unit: opening an old (v0) db migrates to v1; opening a current db is a no-op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §11; eng-review T12; codex finding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opening a fresh db sets user_version = 1 and applies the §11 schema.
- Opening a current (v1) db is a no-op.
- Unit test: a simulated v0 db migrates to v1 without data loss.

title: Fedimint PaymentBackend (ecash via existing federation + gateway)
description:
## Outcome
A Fedimint-backed PaymentBackend — the PRIMARY receive path (ADR-0012). The operator's fedimint client (member of an EXISTING federation, routing through an EXISTING gatewayd) issues a gateway bolt11 bound to an external_id, receives into ecash, and exposes the §6.1 trait: create_invoice(amount, memo, expiry, external_id) (idempotent on external_id), lookup, pay(dest, amount, idempotency_key) (ecash-out refund via the gateway, idempotent on the key, returns a payment id), payment_status / payment_status_by_key (returning the OUTBOUND `PayStatus`, distinct from invoice `PaymentStatus`), and watch() — a settlement stream emitting ecash-received for an invoice's external_id.

## Why
Fedimint ecash is the economic fit for low-value rentals: NO per-payment inbound-liquidity cost (the gateway eats the LN side), unlike phoenixd whose first payment triggers a costly channel open (ADR-0012). phoenixd + LND are later secondary backends (M3). Fedimint cannot hold invoices either, so capture-then-refund applies (ADR-0003).

## Dependencies + DB backend (ADR-0015)
Adds **`fedimint-client 0.11.1` + `fedimint-rocksdb`** to the DAEMON crate (pinned 0.11.1 = the operator's federation version). The fedimint client's own state lives in **RocksDB** (its only published persistent backend) — a SECOND DB engine alongside our sqlite store (§11), which the client owns and we never touch directly. The daemon ships **glibc-dynamic** (musl-static dropped — RocksDB's C++ on musl is painful; a custom sqlite IRawDatabase is a documented later option). `fedimint-client` is DAEMON-ONLY — never in the wasm buyer-core (payment out of scope for the client, ADR-0014). Heavy C++ build (rocksdb).

## Scope
fedimint client integration (existing federation + gateway) on fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb; gateway-issued invoice carrying external_id (idempotent on external_id, §6.6); ecash-received settlement stream -> Settlement{invoice_id, external_id, amount_sat}; ecash-out refund via pay() (idempotent on idempotency_key); satisfies the §6.1 PaymentBackend trait stubbed in backends.rs (FedimintPayment becomes the primary impl; the M0 stub `bail!`s are replaced; phoenixd is M3).

## Non-goals
phoenixd + LND backends (M3); running a guardian (the operator JOINS an existing federation); a custom sqlite IRawDatabase (deferred, ADR-0015); musl-static packaging (M4 at most).

## Constraints / failure handling
settlement = ecash received for the requested invoice; the client tracks which invoice -> which order (external_id, UNIQUE); refunds pay out via the gateway to the buyer's BOLT12 offer / LN address, idempotent on the durable key (no double-refund, §6.6); gateway/federation unavailability surfaces as a backend error; settlements can be redelivered on client reconnect (capture stays idempotent, §6.6). The fedimint RocksDB dir + federation invite/config must be backed up alongside our sqlite (lnrent-7fp.14, ADR-0004/0015).

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane B). Unblocks: capture (lnrent-7fp.8), refund (lnrent-7fp.11).

## Verification
Integration against a test federation + gateway (or a mock PaymentBackend for the e2e).

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.1, §6.4, §6.6, §12; ADR-0003, ADR-0009, ADR-0012, ADR-0015.

## Acceptance Criteria
- create_invoice(.., external_id) returns a gateway bolt11 (idempotent on external_id — a re-call returns the same invoice); on payment, watch() emits Settlement{invoice_id, external_id, amount_sat} when ecash is received.
- A redelivered settlement (client reconnect) is replayable so capture stays idempotent.
- pay(dest, amount, key) pays a refund out via the gateway, idempotent on the key (a retry never double-pays); payment_status_by_key(key) returns the OUTBOUND `PayStatus`.
- The §6.1 PaymentBackend trait is satisfied by the Fedimint impl (fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb, ADR-0015); the daemon builds glibc-dynamic; the e2e (lnrent-7fp.15) runs it against a mock/test federation.

## M1a DECISION (2026-06-24): mock first
The operator chose a MOCK over a live federation for the M1a money path. Delivered: **MockPayment** (daemon/src/backends.rs) — deterministic in-memory PaymentBackend implementing the full §6.1 trait (create_invoice idempotent on external_id, pay idempotent on key, lookup with injectable-clock absolute expiry, watch()/settle()), + 5 tests. capture(.8) is built+proven on it. **This bead (the REAL fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb backend) is DEFERRED** — it is no longer on the M1a money-path critical path; it lands when wiring a real federation (target TBD). The trait + Settlement model already exist.
close_reason: Fedimint PaymentBackend DONE + proven live. fedimint-client 0.11.1 + fedimint-rocksdb behind a 'fedimint' feature; FedimintPayment satisfies the full async PaymentBackend trait (create_invoice idempotent-on-external_id via an lnrent-owned sqlite index + gateway bolt11 + extra_meta; watch() live/recovery settlement fan-in; lookup; pay() idempotent-on-key via gateway pay_bolt11_invoice, Lightning+Internal; payment_status[_by_key]; oplog crash-window backfill). Commits f20f578(.4a async trait)/5344269(.4b)/d4d3657(.4c.2 receive)/7486dcd(.4c.3 pay)/3c6acee(codex P1/P2 fixes)/a9110fc(.4d). PROVEN GREEN against a real fedimint 0.11.1 devimint regtest (daemon/tests/fedimint_live.rs): received 1000 sat ecash + paid 500 sat out, idempotent. main.rs still rejects payment_backend=fedimint — production enablement for real buyers is gated on a separate LNURL/BOLT12 refund-dest resolver bead (refund_dest is BOLT12/LN-addr, pay()=bolt11) + the main.rs wiring.

title: Nostr engine: NIP-99 listings + NIP-17 DM protocol
description:
## Outcome
A Nostr engine that (a) publishes NIP-99 classified listings (kind 30402, with an operator tag AND the recipe's declared management operations) for the operator's offers, and (b) speaks the lnrent DM protocol over NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs: order.request, order.invoice, order.error, provision.ready, delivery.resend.request, billing.invoice, billing.notice, billing.refund, renew.request, sub.cancel. Buyer->operator request DMs (order.request, renew.request) carry a client `id`; the operator's responses carry the matching `request_id` (correlation + idempotency, §5.1). (The op.request/op.result DISPATCH is the operator op-dispatch bead lnrent-7fp.20, built on this engine's inbound-DM + reply primitives.)

## Why
The marketplace is Nostr-native (NIP-99 discovery + NIP-17 transaction); NIP-90 was rejected as ill-fitting. NIP-17 over public relays has no ordering/ack/delivery guarantee, so the protocol needs message IDs, idempotency, and buyer resync (codex/outside-voice P1).

## Scope
rust-nostr (nostr-sdk) integration; publish/replace 30402 listings (carrying the operator tag + the recipe's `[[operation]]` declarations, so buyers can discover the ops surface before ordering, §7.4); gift-wrap (NIP-59/44) encode/decode of the DM JSON; per-message id + idempotent handling of duplicates; publish/subscribe across several default relays; account-0 key signing (single key in M1a).

## Non-goals
Operator manifest / per-box key verification (M5); the web buyer shell (lnrent-7fp.18, also M1a). The durable outbox SENDER lives in the provision+outbox bead (lnrent-7fp.10); this bead provides the publish/subscribe primitives it uses. The op.request -> op.result DISPATCH (auth + run hook + reply) is lnrent-7fp.20; this bead only provides the inbound-DM + reply primitives it consumes.

## Constraints / failure handling
Relays drop events: publish to multiple relays, dedupe inbound by message id, support buyer resync (delivery.resend.request prompts provision.ready redelivery; renew.request is renewal-invoice only). Do not trust gift-wrap created_at for expiry (use payload fields).

## Dependencies
Depends on: the wire codec (lnrent-7fp.19) — built ON it, does not own it. Unblocks: provision+outbox (lnrent-7fp.10), operator op-dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20). (The CLI buyer lnrent-7fp.13 depends on the codec lnrent-7fp.19, NOT on this operator engine.)

## Verification
Integration over a local relay.

## Spec refs
SPEC §5.1, §5.2, §5.3, §5.4 (publishes op declarations in the 30402 listing), §7.4; eng-review T5; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A NIP-99 (30402) listing publishes and is fetchable from a local relay, carrying the operator tag AND the recipe's declared management operations.
- order.request -> order.invoice -> provision.ready round-trips over NIP-17 on a local relay.
- A duplicate inbound DM (same message id) is processed exactly once.
- A dropped order.invoice is recovered by the buyer re-sending order.request (idempotent by order/request id); renew.request covers only renewal resync.

## Note (codex)
This bead owns the NIP-17/NIP-99 TRANSPORT primitives (gift-wrap encode/decode, publish/subscribe across relays, message-id dedupe). The order/invoice business orchestration is owned by lnrent-7fp.17; the op.request/op.result management dispatch is owned by lnrent-7fp.20.

## Boundary (polish)
The wire codec (DM message types + NIP-17 gift-wrap + 30402 schema) is the shared lib lnrent-7fp.19. This bead is the OPERATOR-side engine — publish listings, handle inbound order + op DMs (decode/dedupe at the transport level, then hand order DMs to lnrent-7fp.17 and op DMs to lnrent-7fp.20), the outbox publish primitive, multi-relay publish/subscribe — built ON that codec with an injected signer (operator key from lnrent-7fp.16). It no longer owns the codec.
close_reason: Operator Nostr transport engine implemented (daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs, ~1300 lines): multi-relay publish/subscribe, 30402 listing publish (operator tag + op declarations), inbound kind-1059 gift-wrap decode via lnrent-wire + outer-event-id dedup (seen_message migration 2), injected OrderHandler/OpHandler/Outbound seams, paged lag/restart backfill, semaphore DoS backpressure. Built via rb-lite (10-round claude,codex loop) + a codex-found P1 (unpaged backfill) fix round; final codex xhigh GO. 9 local-relay integration tests; 105 workspace tests green. Commits 5b40c7a/3be67d1.

title: Recipe runner + trivial M1a recipe
description:
## Outcome
A hook runner that executes a recipe's lifecycle hooks (provision/suspend/resume/destroy/healthcheck) AND its buyer-facing management-operation hooks (`ops/<hook>`, §7.4) as child processes with: a timeout (kill on expiry), bounded stdout/stderr capture, JSON on stdin (subscription+instance+validated params+host facts), JSON result on stdout, run OFF the reconcile loop. Plus ONE trivial instant recipe for M1a (a WireGuard peer or a dummy 'echo creds' service) that also declares a minimal request-kind management op (`status` / `restart`) so the handshake AND the ops interface have something to exercise.

## Why
A hung/runaway provision must not wedge the reconcile loop (codex/eng-review). Recipes are trusted code (ADR-0002) but still fallible. M1a needs a fast, reliable recipe to prove the loop without VM complexity. Management ops (ADR-0013) run on the same hook-runner machinery, so the runner owns both.

## Scope
process spawn with timeout + output cap + JSON stdin/stdout (shared by lifecycle hooks and management-op hooks); Recipe::validate() (hooks exist+executable, backend known, os supported, AND `[[operation]]` declarations valid — kind in {request, interactive}; `hook` is a SAFE BARE NAME (no path separators, no `..`, no absolute) resolved as `ops/<hook>` — `Operation::hook_is_safe` (already in recipe.rs); for request-kind the resolved `ops/<hook>` exists+executable; op names unique; AND, as defense-in-depth, the canonicalized resolved path stays inside the recipe's `ops/` dir so a stray symlink cannot escape — recipes are trusted (ADR-0002) but this catches author mistakes); the trivial recipe dir + lifecycle hooks + a minimal `ops/` hook.

## Non-goals
Incus/VM recipe (M1b); third-party recipe install (never, ADR-0002). The operator-side op DISPATCH (inbound op.request -> auth -> run hook -> op.result) is lnrent-7fp.20; this bead only provides the runner that executes an op hook and the validation of operation declarations.

## Constraints / failure handling
timeout -> kill + treat as provision/op failure -> refund path (provision) or op.result error (op); secrets via stdin JSON, not argv/env; idempotent lifecycle hooks (re-run safe) for crash recovery (management-op hooks are not assumed idempotent — that is handled at dispatch, lnrent-7fp.20).

## Dependencies
None (parallel root, lane E). Unblocks: provision+outbox (lnrent-7fp.10), operator op dispatch (lnrent-7fp.20).

## Verification
Unit + a smoke provision + a smoke management-op run.

## Spec refs
SPEC §7, §7.2, §7.4, §8.1; ADR-0002, ADR-0013; eng-review T8, T10.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A hook exceeding its timeout is killed and surfaces as failure (unit), for both lifecycle and management-op hooks.
- Oversized stdout/stderr is truncated to the cap, not unbounded.
- Nonzero exit surfaces as failure; Recipe::validate() rejects a recipe with a missing/non-executable hook, unknown backend, or unsupported OS.
- Recipe::validate() rejects an `[[operation]]` with an unknown `kind`, a non-bare/traversal `hook` (e.g. `../x`, `a/b`, absolute), a duplicate op name, or (for request-kind) a missing/non-executable `ops/<hook>`; it also rejects a hook whose canonicalized path escapes the recipe `ops/` dir (symlink-escape test).
- The trivial recipe provisions and returns a delivery-payload JSON on stdout, AND its minimal request-kind op hook runs and returns JSON on stdout.

## Tier validation (codex)
- Recipe::validate() rejects a missing or invalid security tier (one of 0/1/1.5/2); the Listing carries an honest tier (ADR-0007, §9.1).

title: Pre-flight + capacity reservation
description:
## Outcome
Order-time capacity reservation plus order-param validation. At pre-flight (before the invoice is issued) the daemon validates the order's params against the recipe [[params]], checks available >= requested, and atomically (via the store actor) creates a reservation HELD for the order with TTL = invoice expiry. available = host budget - (active Instances + live reservations). Reservation released on expiry/refund, stays HELD through PROVISIONING, becomes CONSUMED at ACTIVE.

## Why
Pre-flight without reservation lets two concurrent orders race the last slot into capture-then-refund — on a one-box host the scarce resource (RAM/disk/ports) is the second sale, not an edge case (outside-voice P1). Params must be validated before money moves.

## Scope
operator-configured host budget; param validation against the recipe manifest; the reservation table lifecycle (HELD/CONSUMED/RELEASED); pre-flight check + reserve in one store transaction.

## Non-goals
Runtime per-Instance cgroup limits (the compute backend enforces those in M1b).

## Constraints / failure handling
reserve is atomic with the availability check (no TOCTOU); reservation stays HELD across the whole PROVISIONING window so a concurrent order can't reuse the slot mid-provision.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1). Unblocks: e2e.

## Verification
Unit (concurrent reservation) + integration.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.4, §9.3, §7.1; eng-review T9; outside-voice/codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two concurrent orders for the last slot: exactly one reservation is HELD, the other rejected before any invoice (no TOCTOU).
- Order params are validated against the recipe [[params]] at pre-flight; invalid params reject before invoice.
- Reservation is RELEASED on invoice expiry, stays HELD through PROVISIONING, and becomes CONSUMED at ACTIVE.

title: Idempotent settlement capture
description:
## Outcome
Idempotent, **invoice-status-first** handling of ALL incoming settlements. On a Settlement, look up the invoice by external_id (UNIQUE, NOT NULL), then in ONE transaction `UPDATE invoice SET status='PAID', settled_at=?, applied_at=? WHERE id=? AND status='OPEN'` together with the resulting subscription move, decided by the SETTLED INVOICE, not the sub state alone:
- already-PAID/applied invoice -> **no-op** (backend redelivery / replay);
- OPEN **order** invoice (sub PENDING) -> PROVISIONING (the capture);
- OPEN **renewal** invoice (sub ACTIVE/SUSPENDED) -> extend/resume, setting `paid_through = max(paid_through, settled_at) + period`;
- **EXPIRED / terminal / unmatched** invoice -> exactly **one** refund_attempt (settled-but-terminal), keyed by a deterministic refund key (`refund:<external_id>`, §6.6) so a redelivered settlement cannot create a second refund.
A replayed settlement affects 0 rows (status guard) and is a no-op, so paid_through can never double-extend.

## Why
the payment backend can redeliver a settlement (e.g. a Fedimint client / phoenixd ws reconnect); the event_log alone prevents double-running a hook, not double-counting a payment. The idempotency guard is the invoice status (OPEN->PAID CAS), and the outcome is chosen by invoice class — NOT by subscription state alone, so a renewal settlement in ACTIVE is never mis-routed to a refund (codex #3/#5; convergence fix). Renewal and first payment share the same settlement machinery.

## Scope
settlement -> invoice (external_id) -> subscription mapping; the atomic status-guard transaction for first capture, renewal extension (max formula), resume-on-late-renewal, and settled-but-terminal refund routing with a deterministic refund key.

## Non-goals
Running provision (lnrent-7fp.10); refund EXECUTION (lnrent-7fp.11); deadline firing (lnrent-7fp.9 reconcile).

## Constraints / failure handling
external_id UNIQUE + NOT NULL per invoice; one transaction per settlement; already-PAID invoice -> no-op; EXPIRED or terminal-sub settlement -> exactly one refund_attempt (deterministic refund key prevents duplicates on redelivery), not provisioning.

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (lnrent-7fp.1), the payment backend (Fedimint, lnrent-7fp.4 — its `watch()` settlement stream). Unblocks: provision+outbox, refund, e2e.

## Verification
Unit + integration with duplicate and renewal settlements.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.2, §6.3, §6.6; ADR-0009; eng-review T3; codex.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A duplicate Settlement for the same invoice extends paid_through exactly once (idempotent; unit + integration).
- A first-invoice settlement moves PENDING -> PROVISIONING; a renewal settlement sets `paid_through = max(paid_through, settled_at) + period`; a renewal while SUSPENDED (in retention) resumes + extends.
- A Settlement for an EXPIRED invoice OR on a terminal subscription produces exactly ONE refund_attempt — enforced by `UNIQUE(refund_attempt.idempotency_key)` (`refund:<external_id>`) + `INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` in the settlement txn, so a redelivered settlement reuses the one row, never a second — and never a provision.
- UNIQUE(external_id) holds: a second insert with the same external_id fails.

## Settled-but-terminal ownership (codex)
This bead only DETECTS a settlement on an EXPIRED/terminal invoice and ROUTES it: it creates a **detached** refund_attempt (PENDING, deterministic key) and leaves the subscription terminal (it does NOT enter REFUND_DUE — that state is reserved for a captured order whose provision failed, lnrent-7fp.10/.11). The refund EXECUTION (pay/retry/status/alert) is owned by lnrent-7fp.11.
close_reason: Capture implemented (daemon/src/capture.rs): invoice-status-first idempotent settlement state machine + 11 tests, built on the PaymentBackend trait + MockPayment (mock chosen for M1a; real Fedimint .4 deferred). Codex xhigh GO: sound base for .11/.10. Dropped the stale .8->.4 edge (capture needs the trait, which exists, not the real backend).

title: Reconcile loop + total state machine (core deadline transitions)
description:
## Outcome
A single periodic reconcile loop that advances subscriptions by their DEADLINES: scan rows whose
`next_deadline <= now` and fire the due transition, recomputing `next_deadline`. Implements the
TOTAL SubState machine (§6.3) via a compare-and-swap UPDATE on `(state, next_deadline)` — the
subscription row IS the guard. **Downtime credit (heartbeat + outage-window deadline shift) is SPLIT
OUT to its own bead (depends on this) — keep this bead to the core tick + deadline transitions.**

## Scope (the core deadline transitions + the CAS guard + totality — nothing more)
- **soft_date reminder** (ACTIVE, now >= soft_date, not yet reminded): issue the soft-date renewal
  invoice (REUSE order_intake renewal issuance, deterministic `renew:auto:<sub>:<paid_through>`
  external_id) and ENQUEUE billing.invoice + billing.notice to the `outbox` (.10's sender delivers —
  no direct publish). Advance next_deadline to paid_through.
- **suspend** (ACTIVE, now >= paid_through): ACTIVE -> SUSPENDED; fire the recipe `suspend` hook
  (runner .6, idempotent); enqueue billing.notice. next_deadline = paid_through + retention_s.
- **destroy / terminate** (SUSPENDED or CANCELLED, now >= retention end): -> TERMINATED; fire the
  recipe `destroy` hook (best-effort); RELEASE the reservation (reservation::release_txn, .7);
  next_deadline cleared. TERMINATED is the single finalized terminal both expiry and cancel reach.
- **PENDING order expiry** (now >= next_deadline = the order invoice expiry set by .17): sub ->
  EXPIRED, OPEN order invoice -> EXPIRED.
- **renewal invoice expiring unpaid**: a no-op on SUBSCRIPTION state — only that invoice -> EXPIRED.

## Totality
Any NON-settlement `(state, event)` with no defined transition above is a LOGGED no-op (not a
panic/error). Do NOT implement settlement handling — invoice-status-first, owned by capture (.8).
The renewal `paid_through = max(paid_through, settled_at) + period` formula is CAPTURE's, applied on
SETTLEMENT; reconcile fires only the soft_date REMINDER (it does NOT extend paid_through itself).

## Constraints / failure handling
Absolute wall-clock deadlines; each transition is a CAS on `(state, next_deadline)` so a
replayed/stale/concurrent fire affects 0 rows (no double-run); idempotent + journaled to event_log.
Buyer DMs are ENQUEUED to the outbox, never published directly. Side effects: suspend/destroy hooks
via runner (.6); reservation release on TERMINATED (.7); soft-date renewal issuance via order-intake
(.17). KEEP IT BOUNDED: do NOT modify capture/order_intake/ipc/store beyond adding a thin
txn-composable helper if strictly required; the machine is finite (9 states) — no speculative
states/events/abstractions/config/scheduling framework (a `next_deadline <= now` scan IS the
scheduler).

## Non-goals
Downtime credit (split bead); settlement/capture (.8); refund execution + settled-but-terminal
auto-refund (.8/.11); provision/deliver (.10 — reconcile only ENQUEUES); the daemon supervisor that
calls the tick on an interval (.21 — expose `reconcile_tick(now)` it drives).

## Dependencies
Depends on: store (.1), clock (.2), recipe runner (.6), reservation (.7), order-intake issuance
(.17). Unblocks: refund (.11), e2e (.15).

## Acceptance Criteria (unit, TestClock)
- Each transition fires at its deadline: soft_date -> reminder + renewal invoice enqueued + cursor
  advanced; paid_through -> SUSPENDED + suspend hook + billing.notice enqueued; retention end ->
  TERMINATED + destroy hook + reservation RELEASED; PENDING order-expiry -> EXPIRED.
- A renewal invoice expiring unpaid changes ONLY the invoice (-> EXPIRED), not the sub state.
- CANCELLED -> TERMINATED after the retention destroy completes.
- A transition is a CAS on `(state, next_deadline)`: a replayed/stale fire affects 0 rows. A
  NON-settlement `(state, event)` with no defined transition is a logged no-op.

## Spec refs
SPEC §6.2, §6.3, §6.5; ADR-0005; eng-review T6; codex (total state machine).
close_reason: Core reconcile loop + finite 9-state deadline transitions + CAS guard implemented (daemon/src/reconcile.rs, ~800 impl lines): reconcile_tick scans next_deadline<=now, fires the due transition as a CAS on (state,next_deadline) (replay=0 rows), journaled, buyer DMs enqueued to outbox (.10 delivers): PENDING order expiry; soft_date reminder (self-contained renew:auto invoice, expiry sized to the renewal window) + billing DMs; suspend+suspend-hook; destroy+destroy-hook+reservation release_txn; renewal-invoice-unpaid expiry; totality=logged no-op. Renewal-side transitions guard against backend-paid-but-capture-pending invoices (renewal_settlement_pending mirrors order_invoice_may_expire). Built via HARD-BOUNDED rb-lite (--max-rounds 6, --min-severity P1, file-locked) after an un-bounded attempt blew up + was discarded + the bead re-scoped (downtime credit split to .22). Manually fixed 2 codex P1s (renewal-invoice expiry sizing; backend-paid-renewal defer); final codex xhigh GO. 169 tests green. Deferred: single-shot reminders (repeated = post-M1a). Commits on master.

title: Full-daemon fedimint REFUND-path staging dogfood (o6p acceptance residual)
description:
o6p's code is complete + happy-path proven live (real buyer -> ecash -> DO droplet -> SSH -> cancel, + the 3-seller/5-buyer marketplace sim). NOT yet exercised live: the REFUND leg through the full daemon. Run lnrentd with payment_backend=fedimint + the REAL refund Resolver (wired in F1, 8e411eb) against a signet/mutinynet/devimint federation and drive: (a) a late-order / failed-provision REFUND_DUE -> refund paid out; (b) an LN-address AND an LNURL refund_dest resolved to a fresh bolt11 at refund time (proves F1's Resolver end-to-end, not just the receive/pay primitive that fedimint_live.rs covers). scripts/live-fed-e2e.sh proves the backend primitive; this proves the daemon money path's refund arm. Mainnet enablement itself stays the OPERATOR's action per docs/go-live.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- With `payment_backend=fedimint` + the real refund Resolver against a signet/mutinynet/devimint federation, drive and prove: (a) a late-order / failed-provision REFUND_DUE pays out a refund; (b) BOTH an LN-address AND an LNURL `refund_dest` resolve to a fresh bolt11 at refund time (proves the Resolver end-to-end, beyond the receive/pay primitive fedimint_live.rs covers).
- Evidence captured: daemon logs + ledger showing each `refund_attempt` SENT and the buyer-side receipt for (a) and (b).
- This is the release gate for the daemon money path's refund arm; mainnet enablement stays the operator's action (docs/go-live.md).
- Outcome: the staging run is green end-to-end; if anything fails, file follow-up beads rather than closing.

title: op-dispatch: serve in-flight RUNNING duplicates immediately (not defer-and-retry)
description:
## Outcome
When a buyer retries an op.request whose first attempt is still RUNNING, serve the in-flight invocation's result immediately instead of the current defer-and-retry (returning Err so the wrap is reprocessed on the next relay redelivery). The .20 contract (SPEC §5.1) wants a duplicate to get the cached OR in-flight result.

## Why
.20 deliberately scoped OUT an in-flight task registry for M1a (overengineering risk) — a concurrent-RUNNING duplicate returns Err (op_dispatch.rs), so it is not marked seen and is reprocessed later, by which time the first attempt has committed DONE/ERROR and the cached result is resent. Correct + safe (hook runs at most once), but a buyer retrying after a lost op.result gets silence until the next redelivery. Once .21 wires the daemon supervisor (which owns op task lifecycle), it can hold in-flight handles and serve a concurrent RUNNING duplicate immediately.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concurrent duplicate (same sender+request_id) arriving while the first hook is RUNNING receives the in-flight invocation's result (await/attach), not an Err-defer; the hook still runs exactly once.
- No regression to crash-recovery (orphaned RUNNING -> interrupted) or the DONE/ERROR cached-resend path.

## M1a-hardening review decision (2026-07-03): DEFER — correctly parked on .21
Reviewed as part of the M1a hardening set (with h18/k82/xjn). Confirmed NOT a hardening gap: current defer-and-retry is money-safe (hook runs at most once; a concurrent-RUNNING duplicate is Err-deferred, not marked seen, and served the cached DONE/ERROR result on the next redelivery). Serving in-flight duplicates immediately requires the in-flight task registry that .20 deliberately scoped OUT as overengineering; the supervisor that can hold in-flight handles arrives in .21. So this stays OPEN and blocked by .21 as a latency/UX enhancement, not reopened now.

title: downtime credit: also credit SUSPENDED subs' retention/destroy deadline
description:
## Outcome
Extend downtime credit (lnrent-7fp.22) to SUBSCRIBED-then-SUSPENDED subs: a sub already SUSPENDED before an operator outage, whose retention/destroy deadline falls inside the outage window, must NOT be destroyed on restart — the buyer could not pay-to-resume while the operator was down. Shift its destroy cursor forward by the outage credit and keep capture's + order_intake's resumable boundary consistent with that shift.

## Why
.22 credits the ACTIVE renew_lead/suspend window (ADR-0005 revision's stated scope: 'renewal/suspend deadlines'). codex flagged (P1, data-loss) that a SUSPENDED sub destroyed for the operator's outage is the same class of buyer-harm — the buyer in retention grace couldn't pay-to-resume while down. Deferred from .22 because it is additional behavior beyond the ADR's stated scope and adds state-dependence to capture's refund gate (ACTIVE: max(pt,suspend_not_before)+retention_s; SUSPENDED: the credited destroy cursor). The .22 framework (suspend_not_before floor + credited-boundary honored in reconcile/capture/order_intake) is the foundation to build on.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A SUSPENDED sub whose destroy deadline fell in the outage is NOT destroyed on restart; its destroy is shifted by the outage credit; a pay-to-resume in the credited window resumes (capture + order_intake honor the shifted boundary), not refund/reject.
- An outage outside its retention window does not shift it.
close_reason: DONE + reviewer-verified: apply_restart_downtime_credit now also credits SUSPENDED subs' retention window lost to an operator outage (monotonic suspend_not_before floor, anti-resurrection B_old>last gate, gives back exactly the downtime overlap, aligns with capture's resumable cutoff). Spec docs/specs/downtime-credit-suspended.md (3-pass codex-review-loop); 8 new tests; rb-lite clean 1 round (both reviewers verified, not diff-blind). ACTIVE credit unchanged; RESUMING/CANCELLED skipped.

title: capture: enforce invoice expiry in the settlement txn (don't rely on the reconcile race)
description:
Codex (codex-review-loop on the .17 branch) flagged that invoice expiry is not enforced at SETTLEMENT. Enforce it in capture (lnrent-7fp.8): when capturing an OPEN ORDER invoice, if settled_at >= invoice.expires_at, route to refund (settled-but-terminal) not PROVISIONING — symmetric with the inclusive retention gate already in capture for renewals. This is the SINGLE correct place for expiry enforcement: an issuance-time check in order_intake.rs was tried and REVERTED because comparing the backend's absolute invoice.expires_at to the daemon clock is fragile across clock sources (it broke default mock-mode, where MockPayment's clock defaults to 0 — codex pass 5->6). At settlement both settled_at and invoice.expires_at are authoritative. Also when wiring the daemon (lnrent-7fp.21), sync MockPayment.set_now to the system clock so mock invoices have a sane absolute expiry. Add a capture test: settle at/after expiry -> refund; before -> provision.

## Related capture-settlement hardening (from the .9 review)
Second capture (.8) money-strand to fix here: a persistently-failing recipe `resume` hook on a RENEWAL settlement strands funds. capture runs `resume` BEFORE the OPEN->PAID CAS; if `resume` keeps failing, `?` propagates so capture_txn never runs — the invoice never reaches PAID and never hits the expiry->refund gate, and reconcile can't rescue it (it sees backend status Paid). Unlike provision's capture-then-refund, there is no bounded-retry -> REFUND_DUE escape, so a settled buyer is stranded with no service and no refund (violates ADR-0003 'funds never vanish'). Fix: bound the resume-hook retries and, on permanent failure, route to REFUND_DUE (refund executed by .11), like provision does.
close_reason: Fix 1 (settlement-expiry gate) DONE in capture.rs (master d2d1f1f): apply_paid threads the order invoice expires_at; the (order,PENDING) arm refunds instead of provisioning when settled_at >= expires_at (inclusive, symmetric to the retention gate; NULL = no expiry). 16 capture tests + full workspace suite green, clippy clean. Built via watched rb-lite (stopped at round 1: code sound, only P3 comment nits which were already tightened). Fix 2 (resume-hook strand) SPLIT to lnrent-18v — capture_txn is a pure sqlite txn that cannot run the async resume hook; it belongs at daemon wiring (.21). codex review's P2 (release the held reservation in-txn) REJECTED with evidence: reconcile's fire_pending_expiry releases it on the next pass (a PAID/non-OPEN invoice makes order_invoice_may_expire return true, reconcile.rs:260-262/444/464) — same timing as the normal unpaid-expiry path, no leak.

title: GATE-0 abuse resistance (before unattended/scaled public exposure)
description:
Umbrella for GATE-0 (PR-1..PR-4): bounded anti-griefing so an unauthenticated stranger with a free keypair cannot strand or amplify at zero cost. Every business handler is reachable via gift_unwrap by any sender; there is no rate limit/PoW/deposit today. Source: docs/specs/gate0-abuse-resistance.md. Non-goal: no reputation/deposit/attestation (ADR-0011, M-later). Attended-dogfood carve-out per go-live.md.

## Success Criteria
A single free keypair can no longer strand host capacity or amplify daemon cost at zero price: per-pubkey live-HELD cap (PR-1) + inbound token bucket (PR-2) + auth-before-durable-claim (PR-3) + unpaid-load/params/message-size caps (PR-4) all land with tests that prove a bounded attacker; the money-path suite stays green; NO reputation/deposit/attestation system introduced (M-later).
close_reason: All 4 GATE-0 beads landed on master: gdu.1 hold cap (#6), gdu.2 inbound token bucket (#8), gdu.3 auth-before-claim (#10), gdu.4 input caps (#13) + mi9.2 id-validator parity (#12). Abuse-resistance gate complete.

title: Per-pubkey cap on outstanding live HELD reservations
description:
# PR-1 (GATE-0) — Per-pubkey cap on outstanding live HELD reservations

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-1; focused spec: docs/specs/gate0-abuse-resistance.md §A.

## Problem / evidence
lnrent is built to seed an ecosystem of independent third-party operators. The operator who runs
this daemon is a stranger who inherits its attack surface and cannot patch around it, so a
zero-cost capacity-denial griefing path is a duty we owe them, not a self-use nicety.

The operator `#p` recipient tag is public and `gift_unwrap` authenticates *any* seal sender (an
attacker signs with a free throwaway key), so `order.request` is reachable by an unauthenticated
stranger. `reserve()` counts every live HELD reservation against the host budget with **no
per-pubkey cap**. The live-HELD counting predicate is `live_usage`:

- `daemon/src/reservation.rs:242-278` — `live_usage()` sums CONSUMED + live-HELD holds; a HELD row
  counts if `expires_at > now` OR its order invoice is PAID OR its subscription is PROVISIONING,
  `AND r.order_id <> ?2` (line 274) — it already excludes the order being reserved.
- `daemon/src/reservation.rs:107` — `reserve()` calls `live_usage(tx, now, &oid)` to budget-check,
  excluding this order's own hold.
- `daemon/src/order_intake.rs:105` — the order id embeds the sender: `ord:{sender_hex}:{req.id}`.
- `daemon/src/order_intake.rs:40` — `const INVOICE_EXPIRY_S: u32 = 3600;` the unpaid-hold TTL.
- `daemon/src/order_intake.rs:157-158` — `expires_at = now + INVOICE_EXPIRY_S`, passed into `reserve()`.
- `daemon/src/supervisor.rs:98-102` — `M1A_FALLBACK_BUDGET { cpu: 2, ports: 16, .. }`: a small host.

Attack: one free keypair orders → holds the last slot → lets the 1h hold expire → re-orders,
keeping a small host unsellable indefinitely at zero cost. The oversell-prevention mechanism
becomes a capacity-denial weapon.

## Design / fix
In `reserve()`, before the budget check, count the sender's LIVE holds and refuse above a cap,
returning the existing `capacity_full` order error (retryable) — NOT a new code (leaks nothing
about the cap, buyer contract unchanged; see `order_intake.rs:169-172`).

- Count keys on the sender prefix of `order_id`: `WHERE order_id LIKE 'ord:' || ?sender || ':%'`.
- Use the **exact same live-HELD predicate** `live_usage`/`reserve()` already uses (a paid or
  PROVISIONING hold stays live PAST its `expires_at`). Do NOT filter on `expires_at > now` alone —
  that would drop a paid-but-provisioning hold and let one pubkey exceed the cap with paid
  in-flight orders. Stale/crash-left expired-never-paid rows fall outside the predicate, so they do
  not eat the cap.
- **Exclude the order being reserved** (`AND order_id <> ?this_order_id`), exactly as `live_usage`
  excludes its own `oid` at reservation.rs:274. Required for idempotent re-reserve: a crash after
  `reserve()` but before the cached invoice commit leaves a live HELD row; the buyer's retry of the
  SAME request must continue that order, not be counted against itself and rejected `capacity_full`.
- `sender_hex` is lowercase hex (no LIKE wildcards); assert/normalize before building the pattern.

**Config knob:** `max_live_holds_per_buyer`, default **2**, operator-tunable, plumbed the same way
existing operator config reaches the reservation path. The name is "live", not "unpaid": a paid
order's hold stays HELD through PROVISIONING and still consumes the cap, and the knob name must not
mislead a tuning operator (2026-07-05 rename note). `0` disables ordering for everyone; do not
special-case it — document that.

**Invariant to preserve (review-verified):** the hold TTL stays COUPLED to (>=) the order-invoice
expiry. Capture gates only on `invoice.expires_at`, so a hold released while its invoice is still
payable lets a settle-after-release land on a slot another order has reserved (oversell). Do NOT
shorten the hold TTL alone. (Shortening the unpaid order-invoice expiry itself — which shortens
both together — is allowed but out of scope; keep 1h.)

## Acceptance criteria
- A sender at `max_live_holds_per_buyer` live HELD orders gets `capacity_full` on the next order; a
  different sender is unaffected; an expired-and-never-paid hold frees the cap slot; a paid order
  (HELD through PROVISIONING) still counts against the cap.
- Unit tests: cap boundary (Nth from one pubkey rejected, N-1 accepted); a second pubkey reserves
  freely at the same time; expired-unpaid hold no longer counts; **paid/PROVISIONING hold still
  counts** (the counting-rule regression); **idempotent re-reserve of the SAME order_id continues
  and is NOT rejected** when the sender is already at cap (the self-exclusion regression);
  `max_live_holds_per_buyer=0` refuses all orders. Existing reservation tests stay green.
- Preserved-invariant regression: a test asserting the hold `expires_at` still equals (>=) the
  order invoice `expires_at` after this change (no hold-TTL shortening slipped in).
- e2e / integration: drive the real Supervisor through order intake — submit `cap+1` distinct
  `order.request`s from one buyer key, assert the extra returns `order.error{capacity_full}` and
  that a second buyer key still reserves; poll the store for HELD reservation state. The in-process
  test relay rate-limits at 60 notes/min (excess drops as OK:false, not Err) and clamps absent
  filter limits to 500 — keep the burst under 60/min and set explicit filter limits, or the test is
  vacuous.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
Bounded anti-griefing only. No reputation, deposit, or attestation system (that is ADR-0011,
M-later). No change to the capacity budget model, capture, or invoice-expiry values. No new wire
message types or error codes. Overengineering is the top project risk — this is a `WHERE` clause
and one config knob, nothing more.

## Dependencies
Within GATE-0: independent and first to land (PR-1's fix is nearly free — the sender is already in
`order_id`). PR-1 covers the `order.request` capacity path that PR-3's note references (there is no
"owner" for a first order, so `order.request` has no pre-auth reject; PR-1 + PR-2 bound it instead).
No hard code dependency on the other GATE-0 beads.
close_reason: Implemented on impl/pr1-hold-cap: per-pubkey live-HELD cap in reserve() (same live predicate as live_usage, self-excluded, capacity_full result) + max_live_holds_per_buyer config knob (default 2, env LNRENT_MAX_LIVE_HOLDS_PER_BUYER) plumbed main->build->OrderIntake->reserve. 8 new tests (boundary, 2nd-buyer, cap=0, self-exclusion, expired-frees, paid/PROVISIONING-still-counts, oversell-invariant, order-flow integration -> capacity_full). 332 lib + integration green both feature configs; clippy clean.

title: Per-pubkey inbound token bucket in process_inbound
description:
# PR-2 (GATE-0) — Per-pubkey inbound token bucket in process_inbound

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-2; focused spec: docs/specs/gate0-abuse-resistance.md §B.

## Problem / evidence
lnrent seeds an ecosystem of independent third-party operators. The operator is a stranger who
inherits this inbound surface and cannot patch around it, so bounding a zero-cost flood is a duty
owed to them.

The only inbound backpressure today is a concurrency cap, not a volume cap, and the one real
rate-limiter is test-only:

- `daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs:104` — `const MAX_INBOUND_CONCURRENCY: usize = 32;` caps instantaneous
  work, not cumulative cost.
- `daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs:1589` — `use nostr_relay_builder::builder::{... RateLimit};` inside
  `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`; the `RateLimit` type is used only to configure the *test relay* at
  `nostr_engine.rs:1673` and `:1706`. It is **not wired to the daemon** inbound path.
- `daemon/src/order_intake.rs:181` — every fresh `(sender, request_id)` drives a signature verify +
  NIP-44 decrypt + several DB writes + a real backend `create_invoice` **before any payment**.

A free keypair can mint distinct-`request_id` wraps forever; replay dedup only stops *duplicate*
work, not *flood* work.

## Design / fix
A small in-memory per-pubkey token bucket in `process_inbound`, applied AFTER `gift_unwrap` reveals
the authenticated seal sender and BEFORE the business handler dispatch. The verify/decrypt cost is
unavoidable (the sender is only known after decrypt); the bucket bounds the expensive part —
handlers, DB writes, invoice creation, signed replies.

Placement (verified anchors):
- `daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs:661` — `gift_unwrap` returns `Unwrapped { sender, msg }`; `sender` is
  the authenticated seal sender.
- `daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs:672-687` — the handler dispatch (`order.handle` / `op.handle`). The
  bucket check goes between these two.
- `daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs:694` — `mark_seen(...)`, the durable `seen_message` write on success.

Behavior:
- Shape: `capacity` tokens, refill `refill_per_min`. One token per accepted wrap. Config knobs:
  `inbound_rate_capacity` default **10**, `inbound_rate_refill_per_min` default **6**.
- In-memory only: `HashMap<PublicKey, Bucket>` behind the existing engine state, bounded LRU to the
  ~4096 most-recently-seen senders (match `NEGATIVE_CACHE_CAPACITY` at `nostr_engine.rs:110`). A
  restart resets buckets — fine.
- On an empty bucket: **drop the wrap WITHOUT writing `seen_message`** and without a reply, log at
  `debug` with the sender. Not-seen means relay redelivery/backfill retries it later — polite
  backpressure, no permanent loss for a bursty-but-honest buyer. Do NOT route a rate-dropped wrap
  to the bounded negative cache (`note_non_routable`): it is a valid message, not garbage.
- The operator's own pubkey (self-DMs; PR-5 alerts later) is exempt.
- Rate-limit ONLY by the seal sender, never the outer wrap identity (ephemeral keys make outer
  rotation free).
- **Flat by design (operator-ratified 2026-07-04):** paying/ACTIVE-sub senders share the same
  bucket as strangers. No ownership tier — it would cost a hot-path DB lookup and let a 1-sat
  purchase buy a bigger flood budget. Knobs are the only tuning surface; do not add tiers without
  dogfood evidence of real agent friction.

## Acceptance criteria
- A burst of `capacity + k` wraps from one sender: exactly `capacity` reach handlers, `k` dropped
  unseen; a second sender is unaffected; refill admits later wraps; the operator's own pubkey
  bypasses.
- Unit tests: token accounting (accept up to capacity, then drop); refill-over-time admits again
  (boundary: exactly-at-capacity accepted, capacity+1 dropped); operator-pubkey exempt; **assert no
  `seen_message` row is written for a rate-dropped event id** (the redelivery-safety regression) and
  that it is NOT added to the negative cache; second-sender isolation.
- e2e / integration: drive the real engine's `process_inbound` (or the Supervisor) with a burst
  from one buyer key and confirm only `capacity` orders produce store effects while a concurrent
  second key is served. The in-process test relay itself rate-limits at 60 notes/min (excess drops
  as OK:false, not Err) and clamps absent filter limits to 500 — set `inbound_rate_capacity` and
  the burst well under 60/min and set explicit filter limits so the daemon bucket (not the relay) is
  what drops, or the test is vacuous. Prefer calling `process_inbound` directly with crafted wraps
  to sidestep the relay entirely.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
Bounded anti-griefing only. No reputation/deposit/attestation (ADR-0011, M-later). No persistence
for rate buckets. No owner/ACTIVE tiers. No rate limiting of outbound/reply traffic beyond what the
inbound bucket implies. Keep it a HashMap + LRU + two knobs — overengineering is the top project
risk.

## Dependencies
Within GATE-0: independent to land, but PR-3 composes ON TOP of it — PR-3's note says "tighten once
PR-2's rate limit lands so the two compose" (the bucket bounds the amplification of PR-3's row-free
auth-reject replies). PR-1 covers the order-path capacity griefing that this bucket also throttles.
No hard code dependency on PR-1.
close_reason: Implemented via rb-lite (round-1 impl + round-2 O(1) LRU fix), finished + verified manually. Per-pubkey in-memory token bucket in process_inbound: bounds cumulative inbound cost after gift_unwrap; rate-dropped wraps are NOT seen-written (relay redelivery retries) and NOT negative-cached; operator self-DMs bypass; flat per-sender (no tiers); O(1) intrusive-LRU bounded to 4096 senders; inbound_rate_capacity=10 / inbound_rate_refill_per_min=6 env knobs. 5 tests incl. the redelivery-safety regression, driving process_inbound directly (no vacuous relay). clippy --workspace + test --workspace green, --no-default-features builds.

title: Authorize op.request reject path before the durable claim
description:
# PR-3 (GATE-0) — Authorize op.request reject path before the durable claim

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-3; focused spec: docs/specs/gate0-abuse-resistance.md §C.

## Problem / evidence
lnrent seeds an ecosystem of independent third-party operators; the operator is a stranger who
cannot patch around this amplifier themselves. Today an unauthenticated stranger's `op.request`
becomes a durable write plus a signed outbound reply, with no legitimate artifact.

- `daemon/src/op_dispatch.rs:96` — `dispatch` calls `self.claim(...)` FIRST, inserting the durable
  `RUNNING` `op_invocation` row before any authorization.
- `daemon/src/op_dispatch.rs:250-265` — `claim()` does `INSERT INTO op_invocation ... VALUES (...,
  'RUNNING', ...) ON CONFLICT(sender_pubkey, request_id) DO NOTHING`, then classifies.
- `daemon/src/op_dispatch.rs:152-160` — the owner/ACTIVE auth gate (`load_subscription` → owner
  match → `state == "ACTIVE"`) runs AFTER the claim, each reject calling `self.fail(...)` which
  commits a terminal row and replies.

So a stranger with no subscription persists a 120-day-retained `op_invocation` row AND receives a
signed `op.result{unauthorized}` reply: one unauthenticated inbound → durable write + signed
outbound amplifier.

## Design / fix
The durable-idempotency-first ordering only needs to precede the *hook*, not the *auth reject*.
Split `claim()`'s insert-then-classify into validate-id → lookup → auth → claim:

0. **Validate the id** (`validate_buyer_request_id_tail`, owned by PR-4/DRIFT-3): a malformed
   `op.request.id` is a structural pre-lookup reject → `invalid_request_id` with NO row (you cannot
   form the `op_invocation` PK from it). Distinct from the authorized-op `invalid_params` below.
1. **Lookup existing** (read-only) for `(sender, request_id)`: if a row exists, classify
   Done/Errored/Running and resend/defer exactly as today (`op_dispatch.rs:266-300`). This preserves
   cached-resend semantics even if the sub's state has since changed — a previously authorized op
   whose sub later left ACTIVE must still get its cached `op.result`, not a fresh `not_active`.
2. **No row** → run the AUTH gate only (`load_subscription` → owner → ACTIVE) WITHOUT inserting.
   Unknown-sub / not-owner / not-ACTIVE gets the same `unauthorized` / `not_active` reply as today
   but persists **nothing**. The reply itself stays (silence would break a legitimate buyer with a
   stale sub id and leak nothing more; PR-2's bucket bounds the reply amplification).
3. **Authorized** → insert the RUNNING claim. **The auth read and the claim must be one serialized
   store transaction** (or re-run the ACTIVE check INSIDE the claim txn): fold `load_subscription` +
   owner/ACTIVE gate into the same `store.transaction` that does the `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO
   NOTHING` + classify, so authorization and the durable claim commit atomically. Reject paths
   return from inside that txn WITHOUT inserting. This preserves the current single-txn TOCTOU
   safety: without it, a sub can pass the ACTIVE read at step 2 then be suspended before the claim
   inserts, letting the hook run on a no-longer-ACTIVE sub. The concurrent-duplicate race (two fresh
   authorized requests) is still handled by the conflict-classify — the loser sees Running/Done and
   follows today's paths. The hook still runs OUTSIDE any txn, after the claim commits (unchanged;
   `op_dispatch.rs:185-208`).

**Row-free applies ONLY to the three auth rejects** (unknown sub / not-owner / not-ACTIVE). Once a
sender is authorized, `unknown_op` (`op_dispatch.rs:165-170`) and `invalid_params`
(`op_dispatch.rs:173-183`) STILL commit a terminal ERROR row as today — they are past auth,
deterministic, and need the cached-resend so a retry gets the same error. Do not make those
row-free. Document that a buyer whose sub goes ACTIVE between retries then gets a fresh claim —
correct behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- An `op.request` from a stranger (no sub / non-owner / non-ACTIVE sub) → the same `op.result` error
  reply as today AND **zero** `op_invocation` rows. An authorized op still claims, runs the hook at
  most once, and commits DONE/ERROR as today.
- Unit tests: each of the three auth rejects writes no row (assert an empty `op_invocation` for that
  sender/request); an authorized `unknown_op` / `invalid_params` STILL commits a terminal ERROR row
  (the past-auth regression); a retry of a previously-DONE op whose sub has since been suspended
  still resends the cached DONE result (the cached-resend regression); the auth+claim TOCTOU — a sub
  that leaves ACTIVE between the read and the insert must not get a claimed/hooked run (drive the
  concurrent suspend, or assert the atomic-txn contract directly).
- e2e / integration: drive the real Supervisor's op-dispatch — a stranger key sends `op.request`,
  assert an error reply and no persisted invocation row; an owner with an ACTIVE sub gets a normal
  result. The in-process test relay rate-limits at 60 notes/min (excess drops as OK:false) and
  clamps absent filter limits to 500 — keep the message rate under 60/min and set explicit filter
  limits, or the test is vacuous. Existing op-dispatch tests stay green.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
Bounded control only — reorder existing checks and make the auth-reject path row-free; no new
mechanism. No reputation/deposit/attestation (ADR-0011, M-later). No new wire message types; the
only new error code is `invalid_request_id` for a malformed op id (introduced by PR-4/DRIFT-3, used
here at step 0). Reuse existing codes everywhere else. Do not touch the money core.

## Dependencies
- **coordinate with PR-2 (related link, NOT a hard block — PR-3's reordering can land independently and is then tightened to compose with PR-2):** PR-3's roadmap note says "tighten once PR-2's rate limit lands so the two
  compose" — the row-free auth rejects still emit a signed reply, and PR-2's per-pubkey bucket is
  what bounds that reply amplification. Land PR-2 first so the composition holds.
- Step 0's id validator is the same `validate_buyer_request_id_tail` extension delivered by
  PR-4/DRIFT-3 (a sibling bead). Coordinate the `invalid_request_id` reason with it; DRIFT-3's
  request-id validation is owned by that separate bead, not this one.
close_reason: Implemented via rb-lite (claude,codex; converged clean in 1 round, panel found only 2 P3 nits). op_dispatch claim() reordered lookup->auth->claim: the 3 auth rejects (unknown-sub/not-owner/not-ACTIVE) reply the same error but persist NO op_invocation row; authorized path folds the owner/ACTIVE read + RUNNING insert into ONE store txn (TOCTOU-safe); past-auth unknown_op/invalid_params still commit ERROR rows; cached-resend preserved across sub state change; hook still outside the txn. Step 0 op-id validation deferred to mi9.2/DRIFT-3 per spec. 3 new tests + existing green. clippy --workspace + test --workspace green, --no-default-features builds. Verified the landed diff independently (panel was 2/3).

title: Cap unpaid invoice load + params size + engine message size
description:
# PR-4 (GATE-0) — Cap unpaid invoice load + params size + engine message-size bound

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-4 (HARDEN that folds into the GATE-0 story); focused spec:
docs/specs/gate0-abuse-resistance.md §D.

## Problem / evidence
lnrent seeds an ecosystem of independent third-party operators. The operator is a stranger who
inherits these unbounded-input paths and cannot patch around them, so closing the downstream-
exhaustion residue is a duty owed to them.

- `daemon/src/order_intake.rs:181` — every fresh order calls the backend `create_invoice` BEFORE
  payment, a downstream-exhaustion vector against the real fedimint mint / gateway. Partially
  mitigated because the invoice is created AFTER `reserve` (PR-1 short-circuits it once capacity is
  full), but wide on a multi-slot host.
- `daemon/src/reservation.rs:43-65` — `validate_params` checks required/type only; **no size or
  key-count cap**. Params are stored verbatim in the subscription row
  (`daemon/src/order_intake.rs:751-761`, `self.params_json`).
- `wire/src/wrap.rs:68` — `let msg: Msg = serde_json::from_str(&gift.rumor.content)?;` inside
  `gift_unwrap`: the rumor content is JSON-decoded with **no engine-level message-size bound**
  before decode.
- `daemon/src/order_intake.rs:983` — `validate_buyer_request_id_tail` (`[A-Za-z0-9_-]`, len 1..=128)
  is applied only to the order path (`order_intake.rs:99`); `renew.request.id` and `op.request.id`
  flow unvalidated (see DRIFT-3, a SEPARATE sibling bead — not in scope here).

## Design / fix
Three bounded caps, each reusing existing errors/dispositions:

- **Engine message-size bound.** The cap must sit where the raw bytes still exist. By the time
  `process_inbound` has a decoded `Msg`, `gift_unwrap` has already JSON-decoded the rumor content —
  so enforce the bound INSIDE the unwrap path: a pre-check on `gift.rumor.content` length before the
  `serde_json::from_str` at `wire/src/wrap.rs:68`, rejecting content larger than
  `MAX_INBOUND_CONTENT_BYTES = 64 * 1024` (matches the resolver body cap `MAX_BODY_BYTES` at
  `daemon/src/refund_resolver.rs:39`; generous for every legitimate message type). **An over-cap
  wrap mirrors the existing undecodable-wrap path exactly:** `gift_unwrap` returning `Err` in
  `process_inbound` (`daemon/src/nostr_engine.rs:661-667`) routes to the bounded NEGATIVE CACHE
  (`note_non_routable`), NOT a `seen_message` write. So an over-cap wrap goes to the negative cache
  and is not written to `seen_message`. (Add a distinct `Error` variant on `wire::Error` for the
  size reject; it flows through the same `Err` arm.)
- **params caps** in `reservation::validate_params`: reject params whose serialized JSON exceeds
  **8 KiB** or with more than **32 top-level keys**, using the existing `params_invalid` error.
  Applied at order intake — the only path that stores params.
- The unpaid `create_invoice` load is mostly mitigated by PR-1/PR-2 (invoice creation is post-
  `reserve`, so the per-pubkey hold cap short-circuits it, and the token bucket throttles the
  arrival rate). This bead closes the residue via the two input caps above; no new invoice-rate
  mechanism is added.

Caps SHOULD be operator-tunable with these sane defaults where a knob fits the existing config
plumbing; the message-size and resolver-matched byte bound may stay a constant.

## Acceptance criteria
- Oversized rumor content (>64 KiB) is rejected in the unwrap path and disposed of like any
  undecodable wrap (negative cache, **no `seen_message` row**); oversized (>8 KiB) or too-many-keys
  (>32 top-level) params are rejected at order intake with `params_invalid`.
- Unit tests: boundary sizes accepted — exactly 64 KiB rumor content, exactly 8 KiB params, exactly
  32 keys all pass; one byte / one key over each is rejected. Assert the over-cap wrap writes no
  `seen_message` row and IS placed in the negative cache (mirrors the undecodable-wrap disposition —
  the regression that the earlier draft got wrong).
- e2e / integration: drive the real engine `process_inbound` with an over-cap wrap and assert it is
  dropped without a `seen_message` row while a normal-size wrap from the same/another sender is
  handled. The in-process test relay rate-limits at 60 notes/min (excess drops as OK:false) and
  clamps absent filter limits to 500 — a >64 KiB event may also trip relay-side limits, so prefer
  calling `process_inbound`/`gift_unwrap` directly with a crafted oversized rumor to keep the test
  non-vacuous. Existing order/reservation tests stay green.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
Bounded input caps only. No reputation/deposit/attestation (ADR-0011, M-later). No new invoice-rate
limiter (PR-1/PR-2 carry that). **DRIFT-3 (applying `validate_buyer_request_id_tail` to
`renew.request.id` / `op.request.id`, including the `invalid_request_id` reason PR-3 step 0
consumes) is a SEPARATE bead owned elsewhere — not included here.** No new error codes beyond the
size/param rejects reusing `params_invalid` and the wire size-reject variant. Overengineering is the
top project risk — three constants/knobs and two guard clauses.

## Dependencies
Within GATE-0: independent to land (smallest, per the spec's suggested order). Complements PR-1
(the reserve-before-invoice short-circuit) and PR-2 (arrival-rate throttle) on the unpaid-invoice
load story. Sibling bead: DRIFT-3 request-id validation, which supplies the `invalid_request_id`
reason PR-3 references — coordinate the reason string across the two, but no hard code dependency.
close_reason: Merged as PR #13 (d29cd22): 64KiB inbound content bound in gift_unwrap (ContentTooLarge -> undecodable disposition), 8KiB/32-key params caps in validate_params; NIP-59 transport ceiling measured at 40,572B so unwrap guard is defense-in-depth; 458 tests + clippy green

title: Refund-dest resolver hardening: transient-failure escalation + bounded-concurrency drive
description:
## Outcome
Residual P2 robustness for the lnrent-ug8 refund-dest resolver (merged 2277274). Per the codex next-steps review (2026-06-29), item 1 below is ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in the merged code — so this bead is now mostly the bounded-concurrency item + a doc nit.

## Items
1. (LARGELY DONE) Transient-failure escalation: the merged code already has a per-row resolution deadline + a stuck-PENDING operator alert (refund.rs:47/54/739). VERIFY it covers the permanently-transient case (NXDOMAIN / always-5xx) end to end (alert fires, no terminalize) and close this item if so.
2. (REMAINING, P2) BOUNDED-CONCURRENCY DRIVE: Refunder::drive() still processes refunds SERIALLY (refund.rs:185); each LNURL resolution can take ~120s. One slow/hostile buyer endpoint head-of-line-blocks every other pending refund. This is THROUGHPUT/DoS hardening, NOT money correctness — only needed if broad hostile public traffic is expected. Add bounded concurrency or keep the per-row deadline as sufficient.
3. (DOC NIT) SPEC §11 schema appendix (~SPEC.md:1145) still shows the old refund_attempt idempotency_key claim without the resolver columns/gen-bound key; sync to store.rs (the §6.4/§6.6 narrative is already correct).

## Non-goals
The gen-bound idempotency, SSRF envelope, LUD-06 verification (done in ug8). The in-flight-Unknown stranding (o6p's oplog recovery / gen-0=bare-key).
close_reason: DONE: item 1 (transient-failure escalation) verified already-implemented (RESOLUTION_DEADLINE per-row + stuck-PENDING operator-loud alert, no terminalize on a permanently-transient endpoint); item 3 (SPEC §11 refund_attempt schema doc) synced to the real ug8 resolver columns + gen-bound key. Item 2 (bounded-concurrency drive) is throughput/DoS hardening only needed pre-public — split to a new P3 bead (not on the M1a/pre-real-money path).

title: nostr-engine: drain in-flight inbound handlers on shutdown (complete graceful drain)
description:
## Outcome
Give NostrEngine a shutdown/drain API so the daemon supervisor (.21) can AWAIT all in-flight inbound work before graceful shutdown returns — not just the handler calls it can count via the Draining* wrappers.

## Why
.21's graceful shutdown wraps the order/op handlers in Draining* counters and wait_idle()s on them, but NostrEngine::run_inbound spawns DETACHED tasks INSIDE the engine — the startup backlog fetch, per-relay CLOSED-resubscribe loops, and the pre-handler decrypt/dedupe/fetch that happen BEFORE the handler wrapper's enter(). So wait_idle() can observe active==0 while such a task is about to enter a handler, mutate the store, and send a reply AFTER shutdown() returned and the final outbox flush ran (codex+claude flagged on .21). It is NOT a lost effect today — durability recovers a cut-off task (op_invocation RUNNING -> interrupted on reboot, the outbox, capture's OPEN->PAID CAS) — so it was deferred from .21 because the COMPLETE fix needs an engine API and .21 was file-locked out of nostr_engine.rs.

## Scope
A NostrEngine method to (a) stop accepting new inbound wraps and (b) await/cancel its own spawned tasks (backlog fetch, resubscribe loops, in-flight per-wrap handler tasks) within a bounded window; the supervisor calls it during shutdown before the final outbox flush.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On graceful shutdown, no inbound handler mutates the store or sends a reply after the engine's drain returns (deterministic test: signal shutdown with an in-flight wrap mid-decrypt -> it either completes before drain returns or is cancelled, never lands after).
- Existing nostr_engine + supervisor smoke tests stay green.
close_reason: Engine-owned inbound graceful-shutdown drain landed (02917b5): per-wrap TaskTracker is the authoritative drain, supervisor drains before final outbox flush, InboundDrain diagnostic-only. Two shutdown races caught by the independent codex gate fixed (stopped-loop drain + await-aborted-loop-before-snapshot); re-gate clean. 6 drain tests + suites green, clippy clean, --no-default-features green.

title: Pay-retry crash window: recovery skips the new op behind a stale FAILED row — operator double-pay
description:
# Crash between pay start and index upsert can double-pay via a stale FAILED row

**Discovered during lnrent-y4m.16** via the adversarial codex pass (its P0 finding #1). PRE-EXISTING — the upsert-after-pay ordering and the recovery skip predate y4m.16.

## Problem
`start_new_pay` commits the fedimint operation BEFORE the index upsert (fedimint_backend.rs: pay_bolt11_invoice then pay_idx_upsert). Scenario:
1. Operation A fails definitively -> key K marked FAILED.
2. A same-key retry starts operation B; the daemon CRASHES after B commits but before the upsert replaces A's row.
3. `recover_pay_from_oplog` skips ANY already-indexed key (`pay_idx_status_by_key(..).is_some()` guard), so B stays invisible behind A's stale FAILED row.
4. A later drive sees Failed; if the persisted invoice is now EXPIRED the refunder re-resolves a NEW payment hash and starts operation C while B is still in flight.
Fedimint's same-payment-hash dedup cannot protect C (fresh hash). B and C can BOTH settle -> the OPERATOR pays twice (a real INV-1-adjacent loss: each op is capped individually, not jointly).

## Scope
In `recover_pay_from_oplog`: compare operation ids — when the indexed row is FAILED and the oplog holds a DIFFERENT (newer) operation for the same idempotency key, replace the stale row with the oplog op as PENDING (the y4m.16 ambiguous/CAS machinery then re-awaits it; if it failed definitively too the retry proceeds as before). Never touch SUCCEEDED rows; PENDING rows cannot be stale (a new op only starts from FAILED/absent). The oplog scan is paginate_operations_rev (newest first) — first non-indexed-op wins per key.

## Non-goals
No change to the pay start ordering (op id is unknowable before starting); no oplog recovery of AMBIGUOUS ops (lnrent-4gt PART 2). Related: [[lnrent-4gt-part1-gen0-bare-key]].

title: Roadmap cleanup: dead-code cuts + doc-drift fixes (do first)
description:
Umbrella for the production-readiness.md cleanup workstream (CUT-1..4, DRIFT-2/3/4). Pure deletion + comment/doc fixes, no behavior change — done EARLY to shrink the surface GATE-0/GATE-1/HARDEN build on. Source of truth: docs/specs/cleanup-cuts.md + docs/specs/production-readiness.md §CUT/§DRIFT. Ecosystem framing: operator is a stranger; overengineering is the top project risk.

## Success Criteria
All four CUTs deleted (grep confirms zero remaining call sites), DRIFT-2/4 comment fixes landed, DRIFT-3 owned by lnrent-mi9.2 under GATE-0; `cargo test -p lnrentd` + `clippy` green; zero behavior change; completed BEFORE GATE-0/GATE-1 work so those build on the reduced surface.

title: Dead-code cuts + comment drift (CUT-1..4, DRIFT-2/4)
description:
# CLEANUP — Dead-code cuts + comment drift (CUT-1..4, DRIFT-2/4)

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md CUT-1, CUT-2, CUT-3, CUT-4, DRIFT-2, DRIFT-4 (and §"Suggested sequencing" item 3, "a single low-risk cleanup bead … do it early to shrink the surface the above are built on"); focused spec: docs/specs/cleanup-cuts.md.

## Problem / evidence

lnrent exists to seed an ecosystem of independent third-party operators — the operator is a stranger who has never read this code (SPEC §1). Overengineering is the standing top project risk, so verified-dead mechanism and stale docs are pure liability: they enlarge the surface every later gate builds on and mislead the next operator/implementer. Every item below is verified dead or verified stale; nothing changes runtime behavior.

- **CUT-1 — dead backend traits + impls (~90 LOC).** `daemon/src/backends.rs`: `ComputeBackend` (`backends.rs:16-23`) + `HostCompute` (`backends.rs:172-190`), `NetworkBackend` (`backends.rs:26-31`) + `WireguardNetwork` (`backends.rs:193-208`), `StorageBackend` (`backends.rs:422-426`), `Observability` (`backends.rs:429-432`) — every method `bail!("… not implemented (M0 stub)")`, zero call sites outside the file. Provisioning is 100% hook-driven; `recipe.provisioning.backend` is a JSON string, never a trait. The header comment `backends.rs:1-3` still claims "v1 implements Compute (host) + Network (WireGuard)" — false.
- **CUT-2 — duplicate `FedimintPayment` stub.** `backends.rs:212-243` is an all-`bail!("… M0 stub")` `PaymentBackend` impl shadowing the real `fedimint_backend::FedimintPayment`. Its ONE consumer is `daemon/tests/supervisor.rs:594` (`dev_settle_default_is_unsupported_for_non_mock_backend`), which does `FedimintPayment.dev_settle("external", START)` and asserts `err.to_string() == DEV_SETTLE_UNSUPPORTED` (supervisor.rs:595-599) — i.e. it asserts the TRAIT DEFAULT error.
- **CUT-3 — dead `compute_backend` operator knob.** Parsed (`config.rs:140` `RawConfig.compute_backend`), resolved (`config.rs:113` `Config.compute_backend`), validated (`config.rs:599-611`, `is_known_compute_backend`), persisted (`store.rs:68` `compute_backend TEXT`), threaded through the reconcile-on-boot update (`config.rs:1652-1783`), fed by CLI arg (`main.rs:68` field, `main.rs:369` `compute_backend: args.compute_backend`) and env (`config.rs:371` `compute_backend: get(ENV_COMPUTE_BACKEND)`) — but **never read to drive behavior**; runtime dispatch uses the recipe's `provisioning.backend` (validated live at `recipe.rs:199`). go-live.md §3 even sets `LNRENT_COMPUTE_BACKEND=cloud-do` (`go-live.md:61`) — a value `is_known_compute_backend` would currently *reject*, so the knob is both dead and mis-documented.
- **CUT-4 — vestigial `domain::SubState` / `domain::Subscription`.** `domain.rs:10-30` (`SubState`, `Serialize/Deserialize`-derived) + `domain.rs:53-71` (`Subscription`) + their test `domain.rs:80-90` (`substate_serializes_to_spec_values`). Never constructed from a DB row or parsed anywhere (every module reads/writes `state` as SQL text). Already wrong: the enum has **no `Resuming` variant** (`domain.rs:10-30`) though the live machine uses `RESUMING` everywhere — a latent landmine (a future `state='RESUMING'` parse would serde-fail on exactly the in-flight paid state).
- **DRIFT-2 — SPEC destroy-failure "alerted" wording (VERIFY-ONLY; already fixed 2026-07-05).** `SPEC.md:583` now reads "a destroy failure is logged (alert once production-readiness PR-5 lands) and does not…"; the other four phantom-alert anchors read "push alert once PR-5 lands" (`SPEC.md:590,674,765,858`). Grep for `alerted`/`logged + alert` in SPEC.md returns no stale wording. This item is a confirmation, not an edit.
- **DRIFT-4 — stale comments.** `domain.rs:60` `/// BOLT12 offer or Lightning address for refunds (ADR-0003).` (dies with CUT-4's `Subscription` deletion). `store.rs:86` `refund_dest          TEXT,    -- BOLT12 offer or Lightning address` (SPEC §6.4 F3/F6 *reject* BOLT12/raw bolt11 at intake — LN-address/HTTPS-LNURL only). `daemon/Cargo.toml:75` `# --- Real Fedimint backend … (default OFF) …` sits 20 lines above `daemon/Cargo.toml:95` `default = ["fedimint"]` — the feature is DEFAULT ON.

## Design / fix

Minimal, behavior-identical: pure deletion + comment/doc corrections. No new abstractions, no schema migration, no behavior change (CUT-3's residual ignore+WARN is the only runtime-visible delta, and it only replaces a validation error with a warning for a knob nothing reads).

- [ ] **CUT-1:** delete `ComputeBackend`, `HostCompute`, `NetworkBackend`, `WireguardNetwork`, `StorageBackend`, `Observability` from `backends.rs`. Rewrite the `backends.rs:1-3` header to describe what the file actually holds (the `PaymentBackend` seam + DTOs + `MockPayment`; provisioning is hook-driven).
- [ ] **CUT-2:** delete the `FedimintPayment` stub (`backends.rs:212-243`). In `daemon/tests/supervisor.rs`, replace the fixture with a minimal local `struct NonMockBackend;` implementing only the required `PaymentBackend` methods (`create_invoice`/`lookup`/`lookup_settlement`/`pay`/`payment_status`/`payment_status_by_key`/`watch`) as `unimplemented!()`/`bail!`, and NOT overriding `dev_settle` — so the test still exercises the trait's default `DEV_SETTLE_UNSUPPORTED` error. `MockPayment` cannot stand in (it overrides `dev_settle` to succeed, `backends.rs:403-413`).
- [ ] **CUT-3:** remove the operator-facing knob, keep storage + compatibility. Delete the CLI arg (`main.rs:68`, `main.rs:369`) and stop threading the knob through the reconcile-on-boot update (`config.rs:1652-1783` — keep the `relays` handling; the compute column is written once as the fixed default `'host'`, `DEFAULT_COMPUTE_BACKEND` at `config.rs:47`, for new operators). **KEEP the `ENV_COMPUTE_BACKEND` probe** (`config.rs:371`) and **KEEP `RawConfig.compute_backend` parseable** (`config.rs:140`) — `RawConfig` is `#[serde(default, deny_unknown_fields)]` (`config.rs:135`), so a deployed file/stdin config still carrying `compute_backend` must NOT fail to start. Stop threading either value into the resolved config; emit ONE "ignored" WARN whenever a non-empty value is supplied by file, stdin, OR env. **KEEP** the `store.rs:68` sqlite column (migrations are append-only) — annotate the schema comment as reserved/unused. **KEEP** `is_known_compute_backend` (`recipe.rs:15,199` — it validates the RECIPE's live `provisioning.backend`).
- [ ] **CUT-4:** delete `SubState`, `Subscription`, and the `substate_serializes_to_spec_values` test from `domain.rs`. Per the overengineering rule, deletion over repair: a typed state enum gets wired (generated from SPEC §6.3 with a completeness test) only when a live consumer actually needs it.
- [ ] **DRIFT-2 (verify-only):** confirm `SPEC.md:583,590,674,765,858` all read the "alert once PR-5 lands" form and grep shows no residual "logged + alerted"/bare-"alerted" phantom-alert wording. No edit expected; if any anchor still overstates, reword to match.
- [ ] **DRIFT-4:** fix `store.rs:86` comment → `-- LN address or HTTPS LNURL (§6.4 F3/F6; BOLT12/raw bolt11 rejected at intake)`. Fix `daemon/Cargo.toml:75` → "(DEFAULT ON; --no-default-features for the mock-only build)". `domain.rs:60`'s stale doc dies with CUT-4; if any surviving type keeps a `refund_dest` doc, align it to §6.4 (LN-address/HTTPS-LNURL only).

## Acceptance criteria

- Grep proves no references remain to the deleted symbols: `ComputeBackend|NetworkBackend|StorageBackend|Observability|HostCompute|WireguardNetwork|SubState` and `domain::Subscription` return only incidental/comment hits, zero live call sites.
- The CUT-2 test `dev_settle_default_is_unsupported_for_non_mock_backend` still asserts `DEV_SETTLE_UNSUPPORTED`, now via the local `NonMockBackend` fixture.
