- Bootstrap with `LNRENT_COMPUTE_BACKEND` set succeeds with a one-time "ignored" WARN (no error); without it, unchanged; the operator row still round-trips (the `store.rs:68` column is intact). A config file/stdin carrying `compute_backend` still parses and boots (deny_unknown_fields not violated).
- go-live.md §3 no longer sets the knob; SPEC §6.3 destroy-failure wording matches reality (verified above).
- Unit tests: n/a — this is deletion + comment/doc fixes. The one CUT-2 fixture is replaced (not deleted); the existing suite must stay green as-is. Do not add speculative tests.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green (default AND `--no-default-features`); `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean; do NOT run cargo fmt on order_intake.rs (known local rustfmt drift — and note the sibling hazard: never run a blanket `cargo fmt`; format only the specific leaf files you edited, since rustfmt on lib.rs/main.rs cascades into every declared module).

## Non-goals / scope discipline

- Do NOT touch `PaymentBackend`, its DTOs (`Invoice`/`PaymentStatus`/`PayStatus`/`Settlement`), or `MockPayment` — that is the one genuinely polymorphic, load-bearing money seam (`Arc<dyn PaymentBackend>` everywhere).
- Do NOT drop the `store.rs:68` `compute_backend` column (no schema migration) and do NOT delete `RawConfig.compute_backend` or the `ENV_COMPUTE_BACKEND` probe — both are the config-compatibility promise for existing operator scripts.
- Do NOT touch `is_known_compute_backend` (it guards the live recipe backend).
- Do NOT change any runtime behavior beyond the ignored-knob WARN. This is NOT DRIFT-3 (renew/op id validation) — that is a separate behavior-changing CODE bead.
- Keep the load-bearing machinery the roadmap explicitly refused to cut: SSRF envelope, oplog crash-recovery, INV-1 fee math, generation-bound refund keys, CAS-guarded transitions, WAL, migrations, backup hardening, and the inbound backfill/negentropy + three-layer dedupe.

## Coupled edits

- go-live.md §3: drop `LNRENT_COMPUTE_BACKEND=cloud-do` from the bootstrap example (`go-live.md:61`) in the SAME change that lands CUT-3. Bootstrap with the var still set must NOT error (ignore + one-time WARN), so existing operator scripts don't break.
close_reason: Implemented on impl/cleanup-cuts: CUT-1..4 deleted (backends stub traits/impls + FedimintPayment stub + domain SubState/Subscription + compute_backend knob), DRIFT-4 comments fixed, DRIFT-2 verified. 325 lib tests + integration green (default + --no-default-features), clippy clean. Behavior-identical except the dead compute_backend knob now warns+ignores instead of validating.

title: Apply buyer request-id validator to renew/op id paths
description:
# DRIFT3 — Apply buyer request-id validator to renew/op id paths

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md DRIFT-3 (and §"Suggested sequencing" item 3: "a small CODE change … schedule it as its own tiny bead beside PR-4, not in the cleanup bead"); focused spec: docs/specs/gate0-abuse-resistance.md §D (specifies the EXACT malformed-id reply behavior) + a cross-doc annotation to docs/specs/refund-provisioning-hardening.md F4.

## Problem / evidence

The operator serves a stranger: every business handler is reachable by an unauthenticated sender with a free keypair (SPEC §1 / GATE-0). The order path already length/charset-bounds the buyer-chosen id; two sibling paths do not, so the F4 request-id hardening is inconsistent across message types.

- The validator exists and is applied to the order path only: `order_intake.rs:983` `fn validate_buyer_request_id_tail(id: &str)` enforces `!id.is_empty() && id.len() <= 128 && all bytes in [A-Za-z0-9_-]` (`order_intake.rs:984-988`), and is called at `order_intake.rs:99` (`if let Err(error) = validate_buyer_request_id_tail(&req.id)`) before any order id is built.
- **`renew.request` is unvalidated:** `req.id` flows straight into the external id at `order_intake.rs:346` (`let external_id = format!("renew:req:{}:{}", sender.to_hex(), req.id);`) and thence into the `inbound_request.request_id` column, with no length/charset bound.
- **`op.request` is unvalidated:** `op_dispatch.rs:250` `claim()` inserts `req.id` into `op_invocation.request_id` (`op_dispatch.rs:261-263`, `ON CONFLICT(sender_pubkey, request_id) DO NOTHING`) and it is echoed back in the signed `op.result` (`op_dispatch.rs:112,126`), all with no bound.
- Neither reaches a hook or a DO tag (so no injection), but the missing length bound is a real inconsistency: an unauthenticated stranger can drive an unbounded-length id into a durable, retained column. F4 (`docs/specs/refund-provisioning-hardening.md:151-157`) explicitly scoped renew/op ids OUT ("acceptable if it is a tiny reuse, but it is not required"; and "Do **not** add a blanket … validator for … `OpRequest` as part of F4"), so the two docs currently point opposite ways.

## Design / fix

Reuse the *exact* existing validator — do not invent a second alphabet or a broader shape check (F4 point 2 deliberately refuses a blanket subscription-id shape validator; this bead only adds the same 1..=128 / `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` tail bound the order path already has).

- [ ] **renew path:** call `validate_buyer_request_id_tail(&req.id)` at the top of the renew handler, before `order_intake.rs:346` constructs the `renew:req:<sender>:<id>` external id. Per gate0-abuse-resistance.md §D a malformed renew id is **DROPPED + LOGGED with NO reply** — `wire::Msg` has no renew error variant (a renew is answered only by `billing.invoice`; renew's other rejects already drop-and-log, wire/src/dm.rs), so do NOT send a reply. No renewal, no invoice, and NO `inbound_request` row is written on a bad id.
- [ ] **op path:** validate `req.id` before the durable claim in `op_dispatch.rs` (before `claim()` at `op_dispatch.rs:96`/`:250`), so a malformed id never persists an `op_invocation` row; reply `op.result.error.code = "invalid_request_id"` — a DISTINCT structural pre-lookup reject per gate0-abuse-resistance.md §D, NOT the post-auth `params_invalid`/`invalid_params` code. To reuse the helper across modules, expose it as `pub(crate) fn validate_buyer_request_id_tail` in order_intake.rs (or lift it to a shared module) rather than duplicating the byte check.
- [ ] **preserve the invariant:** keep the alphabet and bounds byte-identical to `order_intake.rs:984-988` so all three paths agree; do not tighten or loosen it.
- [ ] **cross-doc annotation (F4):** in `docs/specs/refund-provisioning-hardening.md` F4, annotate the renew/op carve-out (`:151-157`) with a note that production-readiness.md DRIFT-3 now applies the same length/charset validator to `RenewRequest.id` / `OpRequest.id` for consistency (a bounded length check, NOT the blanket subscription-id shape validator F4 refused), so the two docs no longer point opposite ways.

## Acceptance criteria

- A `renew.request` with an id that is empty, >128 chars, or containing a byte outside `[A-Za-z0-9_-]` is DROPPED + LOGGED before any external id / invoice / `inbound_request` row is created, with NO reply (no renew error variant exists); a valid id behaves exactly as today.
- An `op.request` with such an id is rejected before the `op_invocation` `RUNNING` claim (no durable row written), with an `op.result` `invalid_request_id` reject (DISTINCT from `params_invalid`); a valid id behaves exactly as today.
- Grep confirms all three call sites (order at `order_intake.rs:99`, renew, op) route through the one `validate_buyer_request_id_tail`; no second copy of the byte check exists.
- refund-provisioning-hardening.md F4 carries the DRIFT-3 annotation.
- Unit tests: renew bad id → no invoice, no `inbound_request` row, NO reply; op bad id → no `op_invocation` row + an `op.result` `invalid_request_id` reject; plus a valid-id happy-path on each; keep them minimal.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean, do NOT run cargo fmt on order_intake.rs (known local rustfmt drift; format only the specific leaf files you edit — never a blanket cargo fmt, which cascades through lib.rs/main.rs).

## Non-goals / scope discipline

- Do NOT add a blanket subscription-id / message shape validator for `RenewRequest`, `SubCancel`, `DeliveryResendRequest`, or `OpRequest` (F4 point 2 refuses this — malformed subscription references stay handled by the existing unknown-sub / non-owner / `op.result` paths, so legacy/manual-liability rows are not blocked).
- Do NOT touch the DO tag URL-encoding or the order path (both landed under F4).
- Do NOT re-order the `op.request` durable-claim-vs-auth logic — that is PR-3's job; this bead only adds the id bound ahead of the claim.
- Do NOT change the alphabet/bounds; reuse the existing constant behavior verbatim.

## Coupled edits

- docs/specs/refund-provisioning-hardening.md F4 (§`:134-171`, specifically the renew/op carve-out at `:151-157`) MUST be annotated in the SAME change, so the hardening doc and production-readiness.md DRIFT-3 don't point opposite ways on whether renew/op ids are validated.
close_reason: Merged as PR #12 (0582d12): renew drop+log gate, op pre-claim invalid_request_id reject, F4 annotation, tests; workspace suite + clippy green

title: refund: gate FAILED-at-cap on non-Pending status (real backend)
description:
## Outcome
When the real Fedimint PaymentBackend (lnrent-7fp.4) can return PayStatus::Pending/Unknown, refund.rs commit_failure must NOT park a refund FAILED (and DM the buyer "failed") on the final attempt if the outbound payment is actually in-flight (Pending) and may still settle. Gate the FAILED transition on a definitively non-Pending status — keep retrying, or park to a distinct needs-operator state, while Pending.

## Why
The crash-safety argument rests on 'an ambiguous pay outcome is recovered by the next drive fast-skip', but FAILED is terminal and pending_refunds() only lists PENDING, so there is no next drive. A Pending-but-later-settled refund parked FAILED tells the buyer 'failed' while the money went out; an operator acting on the loud FAILED alert could manually re-refund outside the idempotency key -> double-pay. Surfaced by the watched rb-lite review of .11; deferred because the M0 MockPayment only returns Succeeded/Unknown (never Pending), so it cannot be exercised or tested until .4 lands.

## Acceptance Criteria
- With a backend that returns PayStatus::Pending, a refund whose final attempt pay() is ambiguous-but-in-flight is NOT parked FAILED; it stays recoverable until the status resolves.
- Integration test with a backend double that reports Pending then Succeeded: the refund ends SENT and the buyer is never told 'failed' for a paid refund.
close_reason: Already implemented in merged .11 (codex-verified): on pay() error the post-error status is consulted and Pending/Unknown routes to commit_pay_failure(allow_terminal=false), which stays PENDING/recoverable at the cap rather than parking FAILED (refund.rs:144,352); covered by test ambiguous_pay_failure_stays_recoverable_at_cap. Real-backend Pending->Succeeded resolution test falls under .4/.15.

title: Enable payment_backend=fedimint for real buyers (main.rs wiring + pre-switch gaps)
description:
## Outcome
Enable payment_backend=fedimint for real buyers: wire FedimintPayment + the production refund Resolver, behind the SAFETY GATES below. After lnrent-7fp.4 (DONE+proven), lnrent-ug8 (DONE+merged 2277274), lnrent-zwk (catch-up settled_at). NOT a one-liner. (codex reviews 2026-06-28/29.)

## A. SAFETY CORE — do BEFORE main.rs accepts fedimint (split from the wiring)
1. fedimint_pay OPLOG RECOVERY. NUANCE (codex 2026-06-29): the generation gate already PREVENTS double-pay safely (it never re-resolves Unknown — reuses the persisted invoice; refund.rs:397/430). The recovery is needed to stop a STRANDED refund, not a double-pay: FedimintPayment.payment_status_by_key() only reads the lnrent index (fedimint_backend.rs:465), and a crash AFTER pay_bolt11_invoice commits but BEFORE pay_idx_upsert (fedimint_backend.rs:448) leaves the key with no record -> Unknown -> reuse -> if the bolt11 expired meanwhile, it spins PENDING and the buyer is never refunded. On open, recover fedimint_pay from the fedimint operation log (extra_meta lnrent_idempotency_key) so Unknown -> a real status.
2. gen-0 = BARE KEY (codex: 'correct and cleaner', NOT yet implemented). gen_key() currently suffixes EVERY gen incl 0 (refund.rs:147); bolt11 pass-through picks gen 0 (refund.rs:360). Change gen-0 to the bare `refund:<external_id>` (only gen>=1/resolved LNURL keeps `:g<gen>`), update schema/SPEC comments, and re-enter pay() on the bare key for a legacy Pending bolt11. This closes the legacy in-flight-Unknown DOUBLE-PAY (old binary only ever paid bolt11 under the bare key -> fedimint dedups) and removes ug8's legacy bare-key fall-through.

## B. WIRING
- main.rs REJECTS fedimint twice (~115/137) + always builds MockPayment (~149). When payment_backend==fedimint, construct FedimintPayment::join_or_open(config.fedimint.invite, data_dir, identity.fedimint_root_secret(), clock) as Arc<dyn PaymentBackend>; keep MockPayment for mock; gate the mock-only set_now clock-sync seam. The real backend is feature-gated (lib.rs:8) so the `fedimint` path must COMPILE BOTH with AND without the feature (cfg the construction).
- WIRE the real Resolver: replace refund::PassThroughResolver via Refunder::with_resolver(the LNURL Resolver). Inert until done.
- GATEWAY CONFIG (real blocker): config has fedimint.gateway (config.rs:86) but FedimintPayment selects ANY gateway via get_gateway(None,false) for BOTH invoice (fedimint_backend.rs:305) and refund (fedimint_backend.rs:428). Honor the configured gateway (pass its pubkey) or REMOVE the field — don't silently ignore.
- ecash-FUNDING check (startup/periodic balance warning when too low for pending refunds); OBSERVABILITY (pending refunds, failed generations, gateway availability, LNURL failures).

## C. REAL-MONEY GATES (codex 'fail as a complete ship graph until these are explicit')
- DEPENDS ON lnrent-7fp.14 (backup/restore) for real-money release: the seed ALONE can't restore the ecash position (need the federation invite + the RocksDB, SPEC §4.6/1217). .14 is a real-money BLOCKER.
- FINALIZE the Fedimint secret scheme BEFORE funds: identity.rs:36 still marks the HKDF derivation PROVISIONAL; the .4 client uses RootSecret::StandardDoubleDerive(new_root(seed,salt)). Decide it is FINAL + update docs/tests, or do NOT ship funds (changing it after funds = lost ecash).
- STAGING DOGFOOD (acceptance gate): a FULL-DAEMON run with payment_backend=fedimint + the real Resolver against a mutinynet/signet (or devimint) federation, exercising order->settle->provision + late-order REFUND + LN-address/LNURL refund. The current fedimint_live test only proves the backend receive/pay PRIMITIVE (fedimint_live.rs:48), not the daemon money path. Mainnet only after staging is green.
- Operator RUNBOOK: ecash balance, gateway selection, backup/restore, refund-stuck handling.

## Depends on
lnrent-7fp.4 (DONE), lnrent-ug8 (DONE), lnrent-zwk, lnrent-7fp.14 (real-money). h18 is non-blocking polish.
close_reason: Code-complete + proven live this session (2026-07-01/02): fedimint is the default build, main.rs builds FedimintPayment (build_fedimint_backend), the real refund Resolver is wired (F1 8e411eb), gateway honored (506a864), funding/observability logs (79d6bf3), HKDF secret FINALIZED, backup/restore (.14) done, runbook docs/go-live.md written; real payments proven live (DO-droplet e2e + marketplace sim). Residual is operator-side: the refund-path staging dogfood (tracked in the new follow-up bead) + the mainnet flip (docs/go-live.md).

title: Refund-dest resolver: LN-address/LNURL -> bolt11 (enable real-buyer Fedimint refunds)
description:
## Outcome
A backend-agnostic resolver that turns a stored refund_dest (BOLT12 offer or Lightning address/LNURL, set at order time, copied to refund_attempt.dest) into a concrete bolt11 AT REFUND TIME, so refunds go out for real buyers. Plugs into refund.rs (the Refunder) BEFORE payment.pay(), not into any backend (phoenixd needs the identical step). Gates enabling payment_backend=fedimint (lnrent-enable-fedimint).

## Why
refund_dest is BOLT12/LN-address (SPEC §6.6, store.rs:37) because the buyer is OFFLINE when a refund fires (days later) and a bolt11 is single-use + expires. pay() takes a bolt11, so today a real refund_dest parks FAILED. .4 is PROVEN LIVE (a9110fc) but bolt11-only; this is the last mile.

## Decisions (with the user 2026-06-28)
- FORMATS: Lightning address (user@domain) + LNURL bech32 (lnurl1...) via LNURL-pay; bolt11 pass-through. BOLT12 (lno1...) REJECTED (unsupported) -> structural park. Defer BOLT12.
- EXPIRY: AUTO RE-RESOLVE on expiry, made safe by GENERATION-BOUND idempotency below.

## Resolution flow
Detect form (bolt11 / user@domain / lnurl1 / lno1). LN-address/LNURL: GET lnurlp URL (user@domain -> https://domain/.well-known/lnurlp/user; lnurl1 -> bech32-decode) -> {callback,minSendable,maxSendable,metadata}; assert owed_msat in [min,max]; GET callback?amount=<owed_msat> -> {pr}; verify pr amount == owed_msat AND pr description_hash == sha256(the EXACT raw metadata bytes, NOT reserialized JSON).

## Idempotency model — GENERATION-BOUND (codex P0 fix; the prior key-bound design double-paid)
Columns on refund_attempt: resolved_bolt11 TEXT, resolved_expiry INTEGER, resolution_gen INTEGER (starts 1). The Refunder passes pay() a GENERATION-BOUND idempotency key `refund:<external_id>:g<gen>` (NOT the stable ledger key) so each generation has its OWN fedimint_pay row + status. INVARIANT: persist (resolved_bolt11, resolved_expiry, resolution_gen) in a COMMITTED txn BEFORE pay(); pay() always pays the persisted invoice with the persisted gen's key. Per drive, after the already_paid fast-skip (which checks the CURRENT gen key):
 1. resolved_bolt11 unset -> gen=1, resolve, persist, pay(key g1).
 2. set: check CURRENT-gen status = payment_status_by_key(refund:<ext>:g<gen>):
    - Succeeded -> done.
    - Pending OR Unknown -> re-pay the SAME persisted invoice (key g<gen>); fedimint dedups on the stable payment_hash. (Unknown = the pay-commit/upsert crash window -> REUSE, never re-resolve.)
    - Failed AND resolved_expiry passed -> RE-RESOLVE: gen+=1, resolve fresh, persist, pay(key g<gen>).
    - Failed AND not expired -> re-pay the SAME persisted invoice (key g<gen>); the prior attempt's funds returned, fedimint allows a retry on the unexpired invoice.
WHY SAFE: status is bound to the CURRENT generation, so a stale OLDER-gen FAILED can never trigger a re-resolution while a newer gen is in flight (closes the codex P0 interleaving). A new payment_hash appears only once the CURRENT gen is BOTH expired AND terminally Failed (can never settle). This genuinely subsumes pay-oplog-recovery: the persisted invoice + gen-bound key + fedimint per-hash dedup cover every crash window. Re-validate the amount on EVERY (re-)resolution.

## Failure handling (codex: structural must park IMMEDIATELY, not via the retry cap)
- STRUCTURAL (BOLT12; malformed address; amount out of [min,max]; description-hash mismatch; HTTPS/SSRF violation) -> park FAILED IMMEDIATELY. NOTE: refund.rs today only immediate-parks a MISSING dest (refund.rs:124); a backend Failed status burns the MAX_REFUND_ATTEMPTS cap. So add a structural-park path the resolver can invoke directly (mirror commit_structural_failure), NOT the pay-Failed retry path.
- TRANSIENT (DNS/HTTP/TLS error, 5xx, timeout) -> leave PENDING, retry next drive (bounded by MAX_REFUND_ATTEMPTS).

## LNURL security (codex)
- description_hash over the EXACT raw metadata string (not reserialized).
- SSRF guard: reject private/link-local/loopback/localhost targets + DNS-rebinding; HTTPS-only on the lnurlp URL AND the callback AND the final resolved URL; reject http redirects.
- Cap response size + per-request timeout; require enough remaining bolt11 TTL before paying (don't pay an about-to-expire invoice).

## Order-time validation (codex)
Validate refund_dest FORMAT in OrderIntake BEFORE the reservation/invoice/subscription write (it currently copies req.refund_dest straight in at order_intake.rs:222). DECIDE: require a supported non-empty refund_dest for real buyers, vs keep it optional (validate-if-present). Format check only; HTTP resolution stays at refund time.

## Scope / sequencing
1. resolver module + refund.rs wiring + the 3 refund_attempt columns (migration) + the structural-park path.
2. order-time format validation in OrderIntake.
3. Tests: format detection; MOCK LNURL server (happy path + each structural failure + SSRF reject); the generation gate (gen-A failed+expired -> gen-B; gen-B crash-before-upsert -> Unknown -> REUSE gen-B, NO re-resolve, NO double-pay; stale gen-A Failed never re-resolves a live gen-B); bolt11 pass-through; immediate structural park (no retry-cap burn). Optionally extend fedimint_live with an LNURL leg.
4. Hand off to lnrent-enable-fedimint (main.rs wiring etc.).

## Non-goals
BOLT12 resolution (later bead — gateway onion-message support); Tor for LNURL (follow-up; clearnet HTTPS v1); LNURL comment/payerData.
close_reason: Refund-dest resolver DONE + merged (master 2277274). Backend-agnostic LN-address/LNURL->bolt11 resolver (LNURL-pay + LUD-06 verification + thorough SSRF envelope; BOLT12 deferred) wired into refund.rs ahead of pay() with GENERATION-BOUND idempotency (pay key refund:<ext>:g<gen>, ledger key stable; persist-before-pay; re-resolve only on current-gen Failed+expired; Unknown reuses; lookup-errors leave PENDING), an M4 migration (3 cols, per-column crash self-heal), order-time format validation, SPEC updates. Built via watched rb-lite (7 codex+claude rounds: gen-bound idempotency P0 + legacy-key upgrade P1 + attempts/escalation/SSRF P2s all addressed) + an independent codex merge gate (verdict MERGE, no must-fix P0/P1). 53 resolver/refund tests; cargo test + clippy (default + --features fedimint) green. NOT wired live: PassThroughResolver is the default + main.rs rejects fedimint until lnrent-o6p (which is GATED on the fedimint_pay oplog recovery — the one residual latent upgrade-only in-flight-Unknown double-pay ug8 defers). Deferred resolver P2s -> new hardening bead.

title: GATE-1 operability & money-safety (before unattended mainnet)
description:
Umbrella for GATE-1 (PR-5,6,8,9,16 + new PR-21,22): the operator-facing product surface a stranger onboards through — real alerts, teardown dead-letter, sweep/payout, liveness+refund+relay actuators, holdings-floor warning, plus the suspend/destroy race fix and single-instance lock. Ledger-authoritative (ADR-0016): ledger authorizes/warns, balance read only by the explicit reconcile command. Sources: gate1-alerting-operability.md, gate1-operator-sweep.md, operator-money-cli.md.

## Success Criteria
An unattended operator can SEE and ACT on every money-critical condition. Delivered on a ledger-authoritative money core (PR-INV2: `available_balance_msat` read in exactly one place — the reconcile command): real push alerts (PR-5), teardown dead-letter (PR-6), operator sweep (PR-8), federation-liveness + refund-retry + relay-status actuators (PR-9a/b/c), holdings-floor warning (PR-16), the suspend/destroy renewal race closed (PR-21), and a single-instance lock (PR-22). Each ships with unit + e2e tests and an operator-visible surface; no monitoring framework, no automatic balance reads, no refund-cancel verb.

title: Real alert sink: thin dispatcher + self-NIP-17-DM
description:
# PR-5 (GATE-1) — Real alert sink: thin dispatcher + self-NIP-17-DM

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-5; focused spec: gate1-alerting-operability.md §A.

## Problem / evidence
lnrent seeds an ecosystem of independent third-party operators — the operator is a stranger
onboarding cold, and the alert path is part of the product surface they onboard through, not
internal tooling. Today that surface does not exist: every "alert" is a log line an operator can
only see by tailing the daemon 24/7.

- `refund.rs:866` (`tracing::error!` "refund parked FAILED — manual handling required") and
  `refund.rs:941` (retries-exhausted parked-FAILED) are pure logs. `refund.rs:985` (stuck-PENDING)
  is a log. `supervisor.rs:1264-1271` (the readiness ALARM) is a log.
- No push transport exists: `Cargo.toml` carries no axum/hyper/prometheus/lettre, and go-live's
  operating posture is "watch the WARN/ERROR logs."
- The outbox drain already funnels everything through one wire codec:
  `provision.rs:1001` deserializes every outbox `payload_json` as `lnrent_wire::Msg` and
  `provision.rs:1005-1006` drops any non-`Msg` row to FAILED. So an `msg_type='alert'` row with a
  non-`Msg` payload would be silently quarantined — the alert must be a real wire variant.
- `wire/src/dm.rs:298` `enum Msg` has no alert variant today.

## Design / fix
Overengineering is THE top risk: this is a thin dispatcher with a self-Nostr-DM first sink, NOT a
monitoring framework. No HTTP server, no metrics lib, no webhook/email sinks in this cut.

- One small `alerts` module owned by the supervisor: `Alert { kind: AlertKind, subject, detail }`.
- **`AlertKind` is a CLOSED enum:** `RefundParked`, `RefundStuck`, `TeardownFailed`,
  `RelayBlackout`, `HoldingsLow`. No free-form kinds. The only sanctioned extension is PR-8
  appending `SweepFailed`. There is deliberately NO `BalanceQueryFailed` — the ledger-authoritative
  revision (ADR-0016 / §E) retires the automatic balance query, so there is nothing left to fail.
- **Sink = a NIP-17 gift-wrapped DM via the existing outbox** to a configurable recipient — reuses
  the durable drain/retry/FAILED machinery, zero new infra. Add a real wire variant
  `Msg::OperatorAlert { kind, subject, detail }` to `wire/src/dm.rs` and set the outbox row's
  `payload_json` to that (an operator→operator-chosen-peer DM; buyers never decode it, so no
  buyer-facing decode path changes).
- **Config (dispatcher/sink ONLY):** `alerts_enabled` (default **true** when the payment backend is
  fedimint, false for mock) + `alert_npub` (the operator's PERSONAL Nostr identity, distinct from
  the daemon's operator key, so alerts land in the DM client they already carry; unset ⇒ fall back
  to self-DM). These two are ALL the sink config — §D's `min_holdings_warn_msat` is a
  warning-condition knob owned by PR-16, not a sink knob.
- **Edge-triggered with a per-(kind,subject) cooldown** (`ALERT_COOLDOWN_S = 6h`), in-memory
  last-sent map; restart resets it (worst case: one duplicate per condition per restart — do NOT
  persist). Each wired call site keeps its existing log line; the alert is additive.
- Wire the two conditions PR-5 already owns: refund parked-FAILED at BOTH sites (`refund.rs:866`
  AND `refund.rs:941`) and refund stuck-PENDING (`refund.rs:985`, retuned by PR-9a). The other
  kinds are wired by their owning beads (TeardownFailed→PR-6, RelayBlackout→PR-9c, HoldingsLow→PR-16).
- Ledger-authoritative constraint: the dispatcher must never read the federation balance. It fires
  on ledger/log conditions the daemon already detects. Document honestly in the module doc that a
  `RelayBlackout` alert is precisely the one that cannot be delivered while the pool is down (it
  queues in the outbox; §C's relay status query is the out-of-band read).

## Acceptance criteria
- Each `AlertKind` fires exactly one outbox DM to the resolved recipient on condition onset, is
  cooldown-suppressed on repeats within 6h, and re-fires after the cooldown.
- `alerts_enabled=false` → no outbox rows written, log lines unchanged.
- `alert_npub` set → DM addressed to that npub; unset → self-DM to the operator key; both land
  durably in the outbox and drain via the existing sender.
- Unit tests: cooldown map suppresses a repeat (kind,subject) and re-fires past 6h; disabled config
  writes nothing; `Msg::OperatorAlert` round-trips through the wire codec and is NOT dropped by
  `drain_once`'s non-`Msg` quarantine; the two refund parked-FAILED sites each enqueue exactly one
  alert row.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor with a MockPayment, force a refund to park FAILED, and
  poll the outbox/store for the `OperatorAlert` row with detailed logging (do NOT assert relay
  delivery of the DM — the test relay rate-limits at 60 notes/min and clamps absent filter limits
  to 500, which can make relay-side DM assertions silently vacuous; assert on the persisted outbox
  row instead).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No monitoring framework, no HTTP server, no metrics/webhook/email sinks, no persisted cooldown
state. The `AlertKind` enum is CLOSED (only PR-8's `SweepFailed` may extend it). No
`BalanceQueryFailed` and no automatic balance read anywhere. Thin dispatcher only.

## Dependencies
None (this is the base bead). It UNBLOCKS the "surface it" half of PR-6, PR-9a, PR-9c, PR-16, and
supplies the enum PR-8 extends with `SweepFailed`.
close_reason: Merged as PR #14 (29b0d22): alert dispatcher (closed AlertKind enum, 6h cooldown, self-DM/alert_npub), Msg::OperatorAlert riding the outbox, 3 refund conditions wired; addressed codex+coderabbit review (in-txn atomic parked alerts, pay-stuck coverage, cooldown-after-commit); 465 tests + clippy green

title: Ledger-authoritative money core: expected_msat + readiness + reconcile
description:
# PR-INV2 (GATE-1) — Ledger-authoritative money core: expected_msat + readiness ledger revision + `lnrent reconcile`

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md "Revision note (2026-07-04, ledger-authoritative)" + the PR-16
dependency; focused specs: gate1-alerting-operability.md §D/§E/§F + operator-money-cli.md; principle:
ADR-0016.

## Why this is its own foundational bead
The alerting spec's title lists "**the INV-2 ledger revision**" as a first-class deliverable
alongside PR-5/6/9/16, and three GATE-1 beads build on it: PR-16 (holdings floor) consumes
`expected_msat`; PR-8 (sweep) computes `surplus_msat` from the same ledger reads; and ADR-0016's
principle — "the ledger authorizes and warns; the balance is read ONLY by the explicit reconcile
command" — is realized here. Without this bead the ledger-read machinery would be implemented 2–3×
(once per consumer) and the "balance is read in exactly one place" invariant could be violated by a
partial landing. This bead is the single owner of that invariant. It is well-specified already
(§D/§E/§F + operator-money-cli.md) so it needs no separate design spec, unlike PR-21.

## Problem / evidence (verified against the tree)
The daemon reads `available_balance_msat()` on the AUTOMATIC path today:
- Fedimint start calls `log_readiness()` → `available_balance_msat()` (fedimint_backend.rs:371,380-401).
- Refund readiness reads it on every maintenance tick (supervisor.rs:1194) with a `BalanceQueryFailed`
  ALARM (supervisor.rs ~1264-1272).
- `lnrent money` reports the live balance (ipc.rs:211-218).
The balance is an eventually-consistent aggregate of the same history on another clock; authorizing or
warning off it is an unbounded race surface (ADR-0016). There is no `expected_msat` ledger read and no
`lnrent reconcile` command (bin/lnrent.rs exposes Status/Recipes/Money/Subs/Sub/Suspend/Resume/Dev only).

## Design / fix — three tightly-coupled parts, one invariant
Source of truth: gate1-alerting-operability.md §D (expected_msat), §E (readiness revision), §F
(reconcile); operator-money-cli.md (the `lnrent money` ledger-only view). Kept as ONE bead because
splitting the "balance is read in exactly one place" invariant across beads would let a partial
implementation violate it, and all three parts share the same `expected_msat` helper.

1. **§D — `expected_msat` ledger helper (pure sqlite + the local `fedimint_pay` index; NO federation
   call).** `expected_msat = Σ gross of all captured receipts (BOTH INV-3 provenance classes,
   de-duped per external payment id) − Σ gross of refund_attempt rows that are SENT or have durable
   STARTED evidence in the local pay index − Σ max_outlay_msat of SENT/PENDING sweep rows`. A
   conservative LOWER BOUND on the spendable wallet (refunds/sweeps subtracted at gross/cap ≥ the real
   outlay by INV-1). **This is the single shared ledger read; PR-8 and PR-16 REUSE it — do not
   reimplement "sum the ledger" in those beads.**
2. **§E — INV-2 readiness goes ledger-derived (LANDED-CODE change).** Readiness compare becomes
   `expected_msat >= required_msat` (a pure sqlite read). Remove `available_balance_msat` from every
   boot/maintenance path; retire `BalanceQueryFailed` (with §E there is no automatic balance query left
   to fail — this AlertKind must not appear in PR-5's enum). `lnrent money` reports ledger figures
   (`expected_msat`, earned/reserved/paid-out per operator-money-cli.md), network-free apart from an
   explicit gateway/liveness field. Keep the gateway-liveness half of the old `log_readiness` (liveness
   ≠ balance, and it stays allowed).
3. **§F — `lnrent reconcile [--json]` (IPC `Request::Reconcile`).** Operator-invoked, on demand, NEVER
   on a timer. Queries `available_balance_msat()` exactly ONCE, computes `expected_msat` from the
   ledger, and REPORTS `{ wallet_msat, expected_msat, verdict }` (verdict OK / drift). Report-only
   ALWAYS: never mutates state, never gates a payment, never auto-refuses. This is the SOLE
   `available_balance_msat` call site after this bead lands.

## Acceptance criteria
- **The invariant, grep-level (primary gate):** after this lands, `available_balance_msat` has exactly
  ONE non-test call site — the `reconcile` handler. Add a test (or CI grep) asserting it, so a future
  auto-path re-introduction fails loudly.
- Readiness verdict (READY / NOT-READY) reproduces the existing INV-2 test matrix using
  `expected_msat >= required_msat`, with NO backend balance call — assert via a backend double that
  PANICS on `available_balance_msat`.
- `lnrent reconcile` reports wallet vs expected and a correct verdict on both agreement and injected
  drift; it makes exactly one balance call and mutates nothing (assert store unchanged after).
- `lnrent money` is network-free apart from the gateway/liveness field; shows `expected_msat` +
  earned/reserved/paid-out.
- `BalanceQueryFailed` is absent from the AlertKind set and the tree.
- Unit tests: `expected_msat` over both provenance classes with per-external-id de-dup; the
  started-but-not-SENT refund subtraction; sweep-cap subtraction; the readiness compare matrix;
  reconcile verdict OK vs drift.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor with MockPayment + a backend double that panics on
  `available_balance_msat`; confirm boot + a full maintenance cycle never call it; then run `reconcile`
  and confirm exactly one call. Detailed logging on each ledger figure.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No new money math beyond re-sourcing readiness from the ledger; no changes to capture/refund
transitions or reconcile-loop walker logic; no funding/receiving (`fund`) command; reconcile is
report-only and NEVER authorizes or refuses a payment. `expected_msat` stays a pure local read — never
a federation call.

## Dependencies / coupled edits
Foundational — no blocks-deps. **BLOCKS the ledger consumers:** PR-16 (holdings floor consumes
`expected_msat`) and PR-8 (sweep `surplus_msat` reuses the same ledger reads) depend on this. Annotate
docs/specs/refund-money-path-hardening.md INV-2 and docs/specs/operator-money-cli.md that the ledger
revision landed here (the roadmap's DRIFT notes already reserve those pointers). Independent of PR-5
(PR-5 is the alert transport; HoldingsLow is FIRED by PR-16, which composes this bead + PR-5).

title: Orphaned-droplet teardown dead-letter + lnrent teardowns
description:
# PR-6 (GATE-1) — Orphaned-droplet teardown dead-letter + `lnrent teardowns`

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-6; focused spec: gate1-alerting-operability.md §B.

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator onboarding cold cannot see when their infra keeps billing them for a droplet
the daemon failed to delete — the failure is swallowed and invisible in every operator view.

- `reconcile.rs:620-631` `run_lifecycle_hook`: a failed hook is logged as
  `tracing::warn!` ("reconcile lifecycle hook failed (non-fatal)") and returns `Ok(())`.
- `reconcile.rs:902` then runs the `destroy` hook best-effort, and the CAS at
  `reconcile.rs:908-911` unconditionally transitions `-> TERMINATED`, sets `next_deadline=NULL`,
  and `reservation::release_txn` frees capacity — **no retry, no dead-letter, no record**.
- The orphan is invisible: not in `lnrent money`, not in `subs`/`sub` (reads a clean
  `TERMINATED`), only a WARN. A DO droplet that failed to delete bills the operator forever.
- The do-vps `destroy` hook is by-tag idempotent, so re-running is safe — but nothing surfaces that
  it is needed.

## Design / fix
Purely additive — the state transition and reservation handling are UNCHANGED. Surface the orphan,
do not touch the money core.

- New append-only migration table `teardown_failure (id, subscription_id, hook, handles_json,
  attempts, last_error [capped like MAX_ERROR_MESSAGE_CHARS], first_failed_at, last_attempt_at,
  resolved_at NULL=open)`.
- **Scope: reconcile `fire_destroy` (the retention/cancel destroy) ONLY.** Suspend-hook failures
  stay WARN-only (no money burn). The **provision-failure** best-effort destroy is DELIBERATELY
  EXCLUDED — it already has its own durable retry ledger `provision_cleanup_pending`/`_done`
  (`provision.rs:38-39`) + `recover_failed_cleanups()` (`provision.rs:253`). A second ledger over
  the same destroy would create two out-of-sync retry loops. Instead **surface** that existing
  backlog: add a read that counts open `provision_cleanup_pending` (minus matching `_done`) rows
  and fold the count into the same `lnrent teardowns` / `open_teardowns` view, and fire the
  `TeardownFailed` alert from the existing `recover_failed_cleanups` retry site past a threshold.
  ONE retry ledger per path; the `teardown_failure` table is exclusively `fire_destroy`'s.
- On a `fire_destroy` hook failure: insert-or-update the open row for (subscription_id, hook) —
  attempts+1, last_error, last_attempt_at — and call the PR-5 dispatcher on EVERY failed attempt.
  The dispatcher's per-(kind,subject) cooldown is what suppresses repeats (firing only on the first
  failure would mean the dispatcher is never called again and the 6h cooldown could never re-fire).
- **Preserve BOTH existing behaviors exactly:** (1) the reconcile retention `fire_destroy` releases
  the reservation in the same txn as `-> TERMINATED` (fully-paid-out rental; capacity frees);
  (2) the provision-failure `-> REFUND_DUE` path KEEPS the reservation held until the refund is
  actually SENT (SPEC §9.3). The dead-letter records that provider-side cleanup is owed; it must
  NOT change which path releases the hold, and must NOT block the `TERMINATED` transition.
- **Retry:** the maintenance loop retries open rows with the SAME hook + persisted `handles_json`,
  capped backoff (`2^min(attempts,6) * 60s` after `last_attempt_at`). Success → `resolved_at=now`.
  No max-attempts park (a monthly-billing orphan is exactly the thing to keep retrying; the alert
  cooldown bounds the noise). Idempotent hook contract (SPEC §7.2) makes re-runs safe.
- **Surface:** IPC `Request::Teardowns` → open rows (id, sub, hook, attempts, last_error, ages);
  CLI `lnrent teardowns [--json]`; `lnrent status` gains an `open_teardowns` count.
- Ledger-authoritative note: no balance read anywhere here; this is a provider-side (infra) record,
  not a money record.

## Acceptance criteria
- A failing `destroy` hook → sub still reaches TERMINATED + reservation released (unchanged) + one
  open `teardown_failure` row + one `TeardownFailed` alert.
- Maintenance retries the open row with backoff using the persisted `handles_json`; a later hook
  success sets `resolved_at`; `lnrent teardowns` lists it open, then resolved.
- The provision-failure destroy path writes NO `teardown_failure` row (its own journal owns it),
  yet its open backlog appears in the same `teardowns` view and alerts from `recover_failed_cleanups`.
- Unit tests: insert-or-update dedups on (sub,hook) and bumps attempts; every failed attempt calls
  the dispatcher (cooldown does the suppression); backoff schedule computed from `attempts`;
  resolved rows drop out of the open view; `open_teardowns` count folds both sources.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor with a recipe whose `destroy` hook fails, advance the
  clock to fire retention destroy, and poll the store for the dead-letter row + the outbox alert
  row with detailed logging; then make the hook succeed and confirm resolution. Assert on persisted
  rows, not relay delivery (test relay 60/min + 500-clamp caveat).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No second retry ledger for the provision-failure destroy (surface the existing one only). No change
to the TERMINATED transition or reservation-release semantics. No balance read. No suspend-hook
dead-letter. Thin query + subcommand only.

## Dependencies
depends_on PR-5 (the `TeardownFailed` alert kind and the dispatcher it fires through).
close_reason: Merged as PR #17 (squash): teardown_failure dead-letter + retry + provision-backlog surfacing + lnrent teardowns; codex P1 fixed (record atomic with terminate CAS, retry skips live subs); 482 tests + clippy green

title: Operator sweep: lnrent sweep <bolt11> from ledger surplus
description:
# PR-8 (GATE-1) — Operator sweep: `lnrent sweep <bolt11>` from ledger surplus

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-8; focused spec: gate1-operator-sweep.md (whole doc);
principle: ADR-0016.

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator onboarding cold literally cannot realize revenue: there is no daemon-safe exit
for accumulated ecash. This is borderline GATE-0-important — without it the operator cannot get
paid.

- The CLI is read-only + admin: `bin/lnrent.rs:27-47` exposes `Status/Recipes/Money/Subs/Sub/
  Suspend/Resume/Dev` — no payout verb.
- The only outbound money path is the Refunder's `pay_refund_capped`, INV-1-capped to a specific
  RECEIVED amount; operator profit (sales − refunds) has no route out.
- The workaround — a second fedimint client against the daemon-owned RocksDB — risks
  lock/corruption of the money DB (ADR-0015). Funds are recoverable from seed+invite but not
  withdrawable in operation.

## Design / fix

> **Ledger foundation:** the `expected_msat` / ledger-read machinery this bead relies on is OWNED and implemented by PR-INV2 (lnrent-urw.10, the INV-2 ledger revision). This bead CONSUMES it — do not reimplement the ledger sums here.
**Ledger-authoritative (ADR-0016): authorization is computed from the LEDGER ONLY; the federation
balance is never read in this path.** No catch-up-first / balance-before-reserve / quiescence
patches — all inputs are in one sqlite DB under one writer (ADR-0001).

- Authorize from a single ledger-derived quantity:
  `surplus_msat = receipts_msat − reserved_msat − paid_out_msat`, `ALLOW iff surplus >= outlay`.
  - `receipts_msat` = Σ gross of ALL captured receipts across BOTH INV-3 provenance classes
    (settled invoice rows AND settle-refund event_log entries), de-duped by external payment id.
  - `reserved_msat` = Σ gross (once per external_id) of at-risk receipts
    (sub PENDING/PROVISIONING/RESUMING/REFUND_DUE) + every non-terminal `refund_attempt`
    (including unpriceable ones — gross always bounds the INV-1-capped outlay).
  - `paid_out_msat` = Σ gross of SENT refunds + Σ `max_outlay_msat` of SENT/PENDING sweep rows.
  - A receipt is FINAL (full gross, no offsetting reserve) exactly when no state-machine path can
    still route it to a refund (order receipt final at ACTIVE; renewal final once applied to ACTIVE
    or after a RESUMING resume succeeds). **Fail closed: when in doubt, reserved, never sweepable.**
  - `outlay_msat(sweep) = refund_required_outlay_msat(amount_sat, Some(amount_sat))` — a gateway
    QUOTE for execution (a pricing call, NOT a balance read); failure → `sweep_unpriceable`.
- **Amount is the invoice's own** — no amount CLI arg, no amount/dest mismatch class. Zero-amount
  bolt11 rejected (`sweep_invalid`). Operator mints the invoice in their own wallet.
- **Serialized through the same store/backend actor the Refunder uses** so the surplus computation
  cannot interleave with a capture or refund commit (single-writer discipline, ADR-0001 — not a
  race patch).
- Idempotency: pay key `sweep:<payment_hash>`; new table `sweep_attempt` (id=`sweep:<hash>`,
  bolt11, amount_sat, max_outlay_msat, status PENDING|SENT|FAILED, attempts, timestamps,
  last_error). Durable intent BEFORE pay (mirrors §6.6). A PENDING/SENT row subtracts its
  `max_outlay_msat` from surplus the moment it exists. Send with an outlay cap
  (`pay_capped(bolt11, amount_sat, max_outlay_msat, key)`): a fee rise between quote and send must
  REFUSE, not overspend. One in-flight sweep at a time (`WHERE status='PENDING'` count 0 to accept;
  else `sweep_busy`).
- **Crash recovery re-gates unstarted intents:** for each PENDING sweep, `payment_started_by_key` →
  started ⇒ re-await by key (funds committed, finishing cannot double-pay); not-started ⇒ RE-RUN
  the surplus gate against the current ledger, **excluding the row being recovered from its own
  `paid_out_msat`** (its cap is already subtracted). Gate now fails ⇒ FAILED
  `superseded_by_liability` + alert, never send.
- Command surface: IPC `Request::SweepQuote { bolt11 }` (dry-run: earned/reserved/paid-out/surplus
  breakdown + verdict) and `Request::Sweep { bolt11 }`; CLI `lnrent sweep <bolt11> [--json]` quotes
  by default, pays only on `--yes` (mirrors lnd-payments). Do NOT reuse `refund_attempt` — a sweep
  must never enter refund liability/readiness math (INV-3 would correctly reject it).
- A FAILED sweep fires the PR-5 `SweepFailed` alert (this bead APPENDS `SweepFailed` to PR-5's
  closed enum); if PR-5 is absent a WARN suffices — do not hard-couple the two specs.
- Observability: `lnrent money` gains `last_sweep` + the surplus breakdown (all ledger reads, no
  network); sweep attempts appear in `event_log` (`kind='sweep'`).

## Acceptance criteria
- Happy path: with only FINAL receipts, quote → `--yes` pays the operator invoice, ledger
  PENDING→SENT, `lnrent money` shows it. Funded regtest: a sweep test alongside the existing
  fedimint_live pay test.
- Surplus gate unit tests (pure ledger, no backend): at-risk receipts reserve at gross;
  non-terminal refunds (incl. an unpriceable one) reserve at gross with per-external_id de-dup; SENT
  refunds and PENDING/SENT sweeps subtract; a receipt becomes sweepable exactly when its sub reaches
  ACTIVE (and a suspended-renewal receipt at RESUMING→ACTIVE); one ACTIVE 100k + one PROVISIONING
  100k leaves 100k sweepable, not 0.
- An uncaptured settlement is inert: settle at the mock backend WITHOUT running capture → surplus
  unchanged, sweep refused; after capture it appears at-risk, sweepable only at ACTIVE.
- Fee-rise safety: quote at fee F, raise the gateway fee before send → capped send refuses, nothing
  paid, row FAILED with the cap error.
- Idempotency/crash: kill between ledger-PENDING and pay-confirm → restart re-drives by key, sent
  exactly once; not-started branch re-gates and refuses (`superseded_by_liability`) when a new
  liability consumed the surplus; re-submitting the same bolt11 after success returns cached
  success, no second payment.
- Refusals are structured: zero-amount/expired bolt11 (`sweep_invalid`), `sweep_unpriceable`,
  `sweep_busy`; nothing written to `refund_attempt`; `required_msat` unchanged; but `expected_msat`
  DOES drop by the SENT/PENDING sweep cap (per alerting §D).
- Works identically on MockPayment (the gate never asks for a balance).
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor through SweepQuote→Sweep on a MockPayment, poll the
  `sweep_attempt` ledger + `event_log` with detailed logging; assert on persisted rows (no relay
  assertions; test relay 60/min + 500-clamp caveat if a `SweepFailed` DM is asserted — assert the
  outbox row, not delivery).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
NO balance read anywhere in this path (authorization, pre-checks, or acceptance) — the pay itself
is the fail-safe for book-vs-wallet shortfall; drift detection is the explicit `reconcile` command
(§F), never here. No scheduled/automatic sweeps, no LN-address/LNURL sweep (bolt11 only), no
partial/split sweeps, no fee-limit knob (the quote is the fee; the cap enforces it), no change to
refund paths or INV-1/INV-3.

## Dependencies
depends_on PR-5 (appends `SweepFailed` to the closed AlertKind enum; soft-coupled — WARN fallback
if PR-5 lands later). Borderline GATE-0-important: without it an operator cannot realize revenue.

title: Federation-liveness probe + retune 7-day stuck alert
description:
# PR-9a (GATE-1) — Federation-liveness probe + retune the 7-day stuck alert

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-9 (liveness bullet); focused spec:
gate1-alerting-operability.md §C.

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator cannot tell a sick federation from a sick gateway or an empty wallet, and
stranded money stays silent for a WEEK.

- An offline/no-consensus federation surfaces only as failing per-op `Err`s and slowly-accumulating
  PENDING refunds. `available_balance_msat` reads LOCAL state (`fedimint_backend.rs:830`), so it is
  NOT a liveness probe.
- The loud stuck-refund alert does not fire for 7 days: `refund.rs:63`
  `const RESOLUTION_STUCK_ALERT_S: i64 = 7 * 24 * 3600;`, and today it only logs
  (`refund.rs:985`), it does not push.
- The readiness report cannot distinguish "federation down" from "gateway down" — there is one
  `gateway_ok` field and no federation field.

## Design / fix
Add a distinct federation-reachability probe and make the stuck condition alert on a human
timescale. Ledger-authoritative: the probe is a LIVENESS check, not a balance read.

- Extend the backend trait: `PaymentBackend::backend_ready() -> Result<bool>` (default `Ok(true)`;
  Fedimint impl does ONE cheap authenticated API round-trip that actually hits guardians — the
  lightest fedimint-client call that reaches consensus, NOT a local-DB read).
- Report it as its own `federation_ok` field in `lnrent money`, alongside `gateway_ok`, and fold it
  into the readiness warning set as a DISTINCT variant so "federation down" ≠ "gateway down" ≠
  "balance/holdings low".
- **Retune the stuck alert:** `RESOLUTION_STUCK_ALERT_S` 7d → **6h** (`refund.rs:63`), keep it a
  const (config knob only if dogfood shows the need). It now also fires a PR-5 `RefundStuck` alert,
  not just the log at `refund.rs:985`.
- Extend stuck detection to the in-flight-forever pay shape (the P3 the refund audit found:
  `commit_pay_failure` bumps attempts without ever reaching the stuck alert): ANY non-terminal
  refund whose `created_at` is older than the threshold alerts, regardless of which loop last
  touched it.

## Acceptance criteria
- `lnrent money` gains `federation_ok`; a mock backend reports `true` (default trait impl); the
  readiness warning distinguishes federation-down from gateway-down.
- A stuck refund alerts at 6h (was 7d), including the pay-in-flight-forever shape (a row whose
  `created_at` is past threshold but which the pay loop, not the resolution loop, last touched).
- Unit tests: a backend stub returning `backend_ready()=false` yields the federation-down warning
  variant distinct from a `refund_gateway_ready()=false` gateway-down variant; the retuned 6h
  threshold triggers the `RefundStuck` alert while the log line is preserved; the in-flight-forever
  row also alerts.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor with a backend stub whose `backend_ready` flips false,
  poll `Request::Money`/readiness for `federation_ok=false`; advance the clock past 6h on a stuck
  PENDING refund and poll the outbox for the `RefundStuck` alert row with detailed logging (assert
  the persisted row, not relay delivery — test relay 60/min + 500-clamp caveat).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No balance read for liveness (the probe hits guardians, it does not read holdings). No gateway
failover (PR-13). No refund list/actuator here (PR-9b) and no relay status here (PR-9c). The 6h
threshold stays a const, not a config knob, absent dogfood evidence.

## Dependencies
depends_on PR-5 (the `RefundStuck` alert kind and dispatcher).
close_reason: Merged as PR #20 (squash): PaymentBackend::backend_ready (fedimint session_count guardian probe) + federation_ok field + distinct FederationDown warning (fires even at zero liabilities, both IPC + maintenance alarm); RESOLUTION_STUCK_ALERT_S 7d->6h; codex P2 + coderabbit Major fixed; 488 tests + clippy green

title: lnrent refunds list + retry actuator (no cancel verb)
description:
# PR-9b (GATE-1) — `lnrent refunds` list + retry actuator (no cancel verb)

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-9 (refund actuator bullet); focused spec:
gate1-alerting-operability.md §C.

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator can see a count but cannot inspect or act — and a permanently-parked refund
pins host capacity.

- `lnrent money` shows a `parked_count` but there is no per-item list and no actuator: `ipc.rs` has
  no `Refunds` request (the `Request` enum at `ipc.rs:24` has no such variant) and `bin/lnrent.rs`
  has no `refunds`/`refund-retry` verb (Cmd enum, `bin/lnrent.rs:27-47`).
- The operator sees "3 parked" and cannot inspect destination / amount / attempts, nor re-drive a
  recovered one.
- This also gates CAPACITY: REFUND_DUE deliberately holds its reservation until REFUNDED (§9.3), so
  a permanently-parked refund pins host capacity until the operator resolves it.

## Design / fix
A read view + a single re-drive actuator that reuses the existing money path end-to-end. No new
money math; ledger-authoritative (no balance read).

- IPC `Request::Refunds` → all non-terminal + parked-FAILED `refund_attempt` rows (id,
  subscription_id, dest form, amount_sat, status, attempts, ages); CLI `lnrent refunds [--json]`.
- **`last_error_class` (optional):** the `refund_attempt` schema has no error column today (the last
  failure reason is only logged). To include a `last_error_class` in the response, add ONE nullable
  `last_error_class TEXT` column (append-only migration) written by the refunder wherever it already
  bumps `attempts`/parks a row (a short bounded enum-ish class string, NOT free text). If that write
  is deemed out of scope for this bead, DROP `last_error_class` from the response contract rather
  than returning an unpersisted field.
- **Actuator:** `lnrent refund-retry <id>` → for a parked-FAILED row, reset it to PENDING with
  attempts=0 and nudge the refunder. This is the ONLY mutation, and it reuses the existing money
  path end-to-end (resolver → INV-1-capped pay → ledger). Retry of a non-parked id is a structured
  error.
- **No cancel/abandon verb — DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED** (alerting §C): abandoning a refund liability is
  a policy action that stays manual/deliberate. Document the exclusion; revisit only with a real
  need.

## Acceptance criteria
- `lnrent refunds` lists a parked refund with its persisted fields (id, sub, dest form, amount,
  status, attempts, ages — plus `last_error_class` ONLY if the nullable column was added).
- `refund-retry <id>` on a parked-FAILED row re-drives it through the real resolver + capped-pay
  path (reaching SENT when the endpoint recovers); retry of a non-parked id returns a structured
  error, mutating nothing.
- There is NO cancel/abandon verb.
- Unit tests: the list projects a parked row's fields; retry resets status→PENDING/attempts→0 for a
  parked row and no-ops with a structured error otherwise; if `last_error_class` is added, the
  refunder writes it on park and the list surfaces it (else the field is absent from the contract).
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor, park a refund FAILED, `Request::Refunds` shows it,
  `refund-retry` re-drives it to SENT once the resolver/pay double succeeds; poll the store with
  detailed logging (assert persisted refund_attempt state, not relay delivery — test relay 60/min +
  500-clamp caveat).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No cancel/abandon verb (manual, deliberate). No new refund money math — retry reuses the existing
path. No balance read. No unpersisted response fields (drop `last_error_class` rather than fake it).

## Dependencies
None. (Sibling of PR-9a/PR-9c; independent of PR-5 — the list + retry actuator surface persisted
ledger rows and need no alert dispatcher.)
close_reason: Merged as PR #19 (squash): lnrent refunds list + refund-retry actuator (no cancel verb); codex P2 fixed (supersede stale failed-refund DM via refund_outbox_id); 486 tests + clippy green

title: Relay-pool status query + zero-connectivity alert
description:
# PR-9c (GATE-1) — Relay-pool status query + zero-connectivity alert

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-9 (relay-pool bullet); focused spec:
gate1-alerting-operability.md §C.

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator whose relays all drop silently stops receiving orders and refund/billing DMs —
a full marketplace blackout with nothing surfaced.

- Relay churn is logged but not queryable and not alerted; if all relays disconnect, the operator
  silently stops receiving inbound orders and outbound refund/billing DMs.
- There is no `Request::Relays` IPC path and no relay row in `lnrent status`.

## Design / fix
A thin status read over the existing nostr-sdk pool + a single zero-connectivity alert. No new
transport, no reconnection logic.

- IPC `Request::Relays` → per-relay `{ url, connected, last_connected_at }` pulled from the
  nostr-sdk pool; surfaced in `lnrent status`.
- On each maintenance tick, if ALL relays have been disconnected for
  `> RELAY_BLACKOUT_ALERT_S = 15min` → fire the PR-5 `RelayBlackout` alert.
- **Honest deliverability note (from §A):** the `RelayBlackout` alert is precisely the one that
  cannot be delivered while the pool is down — it queues in the outbox and drains when connectivity
  returns, which is self-limiting. The `Request::Relays` status query is the out-of-band read an
  operator uses when the DM cannot arrive. Document this in the module doc.
- Ledger-authoritative: no balance read; this is a transport-liveness surface only.

## Acceptance criteria
- `lnrent status` / `Request::Relays` show per-relay connected state and `last_connected_at`.
- All relays disconnected past `RELAY_BLACKOUT_ALERT_S` → one `RelayBlackout` alert queued; the
  status query shows the disconnected pool; reconnect clears the condition and the next onset
  re-alerts.
- Unit tests: the status projection maps pool state to `{url, connected, last_connected_at}`; the
  all-disconnected-past-threshold predicate fires exactly once per onset (edge-triggered) and
  re-arms after reconnect.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor whose relay pool reports all-disconnected past the
  threshold, poll `Request::Relays` for the disconnected state and the outbox for the queued
  `RelayBlackout` alert row with detailed logging. Assert on the persisted status/outbox row, NOT on
  relay delivery — the alert cannot be delivered during a blackout, and the test relay's 60/min
  rate limit + 500 filter clamp make relay-side assertions vacuous anyway.
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No reconnection/failover logic, no relay-set management verb, no balance read. Thin status read +
one edge-triggered alert only.

## Dependencies
depends_on PR-5 (the `RelayBlackout` alert kind and dispatcher).

title: Draining-holdings warning from ledger-expected holdings
description:
# PR-16 (GATE-1) — Draining-holdings warning from ledger-expected holdings

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-16; focused spec: gate1-alerting-operability.md §D;
principle: ADR-0016.

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator whose float is draining toward zero — with nothing CURRENTLY owed — is warned by
nobody.

- `log_refund_readiness` early-returns at zero liabilities: `supervisor.rs:1259-1261`
  (`if report.liability_count == 0 { return; }`), and readiness is liability-gated. A wallet
  draining to zero with no open liability warns no one.
- Per ADR-0016 the fix must NOT read `available_balance_msat` — no automatic balance read exists
  anymore; wallet-vs-books drift is the explicit `lnrent reconcile`'s job.

## Design / fix

> **Ledger foundation:** the `expected_msat` / ledger-read machinery this bead relies on is OWNED and implemented by PR-INV2 (lnrent-urw.10, the INV-2 ledger revision). This bead CONSUMES it — do not reimplement the ledger sums here.
A liability-independent float warning computed from a PURE LOCAL ledger read — no network, no
balance query.

- Define **ledger-expected holdings** `expected_msat` (sqlite ledger + the local `fedimint_pay`
  index; NO federation call — reused verbatim by §E's readiness compare and §F's reconcile):
  `expected_msat = Σ gross of all captured receipts (BOTH INV-3 provenance classes, de-duped by
  external payment id) − Σ gross of refund_attempt rows that are SENT or whose pay has durable
  STARTED evidence in the local pay index − Σ max_outlay_msat of SENT/PENDING sweep rows`.
  Started-but-not-yet-SENT refunds MUST be subtracted (once the backend op starts, the outgoing
  contract locks those funds out of the spendable wallet). Because refunds/sweeps are subtracted at
  gross/cap while real outlays are ≤ that (INV-1), `expected_msat` is a conservative LOWER BOUND on
  the spendable wallet.
- **`expected_msat` is NOT the sweep's authorization quantity:** the sweep's `surplus_msat`
  additionally subtracts `reserved_msat` (at-risk receipts + open refund liabilities).
  `expected_msat` answers "what should the wallet HOLD"; surplus answers "what may the operator
  KEEP". NEVER authorize a payout from `expected_msat`.
- Config `min_holdings_warn_msat` (default **0 = disabled**; the operator opts in with a floor that
  matches their float). This is a WARNING-CONDITION knob owned here, NOT a PR-5 sink knob. On each
  maintenance tick, if `expected_msat < floor` → fire the PR-5 `HoldingsLow` alert (cooldown-bounded
  by the dispatcher). No backend call.
- **Independence note:** this is an operator-FLOAT warning, NOT a refund-readiness warning — it is
  computed regardless of whether any liability exists (readiness stays liability-gated, §E). Annotate
  docs/specs/refund-money-path-hardening.md accordingly (the roadmap's PR-16 cross-doc note reserves
  this).

## Acceptance criteria
- `expected_msat` below the configured `min_holdings_warn_msat` → one `HoldingsLow` alert; floor=0
  never alerts.
- The floor check makes NO backend call: assert via a backend stub that PANICS on
  `available_balance_msat` — the tick must not touch it.
- A started-but-PENDING refund reduces `expected_msat` by its gross: assert with a `refund_attempt`
  row that has started-evidence in the local pay index.
- Unit tests: `expected_msat` computation (receipts − SENT/started refunds − sweep caps) over both
  provenance classes with per-external_id de-dup; the started-evidence subtraction; floor=0 disables;
  a non-zero floor above `expected_msat` fires exactly once per onset (dispatcher cooldown suppresses
  repeats); the check runs even with zero liabilities.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor with a MockPayment and a backend double that panics on
  `available_balance_msat`, set a non-zero floor, drain the ledger below it, and poll the outbox for
  the `HoldingsLow` alert row with detailed logging (assert persisted row, not relay delivery — test
  relay 60/min + 500-clamp caveat).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
NO automatic balance read (`available_balance_msat` is retired from every automatic path; only the
explicit `reconcile` command reads it — §F). Not a refund-readiness warning (float-independent). Not
the sweep authorization quantity. No network call in the floor check.

## Dependencies
depends_on PR-5 (the `HoldingsLow` alert kind and dispatcher). ALSO needs the INV-2 ledger revision
(§E) that introduces `expected_msat` and retires `BalanceQueryFailed` — schedule §E ahead of this
bead (it is the second implementation step in the alerting spec, right after PR-5 core).

title: Suspend/destroy hooks race a concurrently-captured renewal
description:
# PR-21 (GATE-1) — Suspend/destroy hooks race a concurrently-captured renewal

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-21; focused spec: **TO BE WRITTEN by this bead** (money-core
adjacent — the first deliverable is the spec, then the compensating-action implementation).

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator's buyer can pay on time and still get a powered-off box (or a destroyed one)
because the lifecycle hook runs OUTSIDE the CAS that guards the state row.

- `fire_suspend` (`reconcile.rs:853-869`): the pre-check `renewal_settlement_pending` runs at
  `reconcile.rs:850`, then the suspend hook runs at `reconcile.rs:853-854` OUTSIDE the CAS. If the
  buyer's renewal settles during the hook's up-to-120s window, capture commits first (sub stays
  ACTIVE with a fresh deadline), the post-hook CAS at `reconcile.rs:865-868`
  (`WHERE ... state='ACTIVE' AND next_deadline=?4`) matches 0 rows and no-ops — leaving the instance
  powered OFF while the row reads ACTIVE. The resume driver only drives RESUMING, so nothing repowers
  it; the buyer pays for a down service until the next suspend/renew cycle.
- `fire_destroy` (`reconcile.rs:899-911`) has the same shape: pre-check at
  `reconcile.rs:899`, destroy hook at `reconcile.rs:902-903` OUTSIDE the CAS at
  `reconcile.rs:908-911`. A renewal captured mid-destroy-hook (inside the credited resumable window)
  flips SUSPENDED→RESUMING while the droplet is being irreversibly deleted — money-safe (ends in a
  refund) but a timely-paid service is destroyed and surfaces only as a confusing refund.

## Design / fix
**This bead's FIRST deliverable is a focused spec** (money-core adjacent; get it reviewed before
code). The spec must specify the compensating actions and their acceptance tests. Sketch to refine:

- **Suspend:** on a lost suspend-CAS (post-hook CAS matched 0 rows), re-read the sub; if it is now
  ACTIVE (the renewal won), run the `resume` hook as a best-effort COMPENSATING action to repower the
  instance, logged loudly. Mirror the lost-CAS cleanup pattern the provisioner already uses
  (`provision.rs` lost-CAS cleanup).
- **Destroy:** re-check `renewal_settlement_pending` AFTER the hook but BEFORE the CAS (today the
  check exists only pre-hook at `reconcile.rs:899`), and fire a PR-5 alert on a lost CAS to RESUMING
  so the operator learns a paid service was destroyed and a refund is now in flight.
- Preserve every existing invariant: all transitions stay CAS-guarded; the money core (capture,
  refund math, reservation semantics) is untouched — this only adds compensating actions on the lost
  race and an alert. The `resume`/`destroy` hooks are already idempotent (SPEC §7.2), so a
  best-effort compensating re-run is safe.
- No config knobs (behavior-only fix). Ledger-authoritative: no balance read introduced.

## Acceptance criteria
- A renewal that settles+captures during the suspend hook leaves the instance POWERED ON: after the
  lost suspend-CAS, the compensating `resume` hook runs and the sub row + instance agree (ACTIVE +
  powered on), logged loudly.
- A renewal captured during the destroy hook: the post-hook `renewal_settlement_pending` re-check
  aborts the terminate CAS where possible; on a genuine lost-CAS-to-RESUMING, a PR-5 alert fires so
  the destroyed-but-paid case is visible (not just a silent refund).
- Unit tests: simulate capture committing between the pre-check and the CAS for BOTH `fire_suspend`
  and `fire_destroy`; assert the compensating `resume` runs on suspend-loss and the alert fires on
  destroy-loss; assert no double-suspend / no double-destroy and every transition stays CAS-guarded.
- e2e / integration: drive a real Supervisor with a controllable clock and a hook double that blocks
  long enough to let a renewal capture land mid-hook; poll the store for the reconciled ACTIVE state
  and (destroy case) the outbox alert row, with detailed logging (assert persisted state/outbox rows,
  not relay delivery — test relay 60/min + 500-clamp caveat).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No change to capture, refund math, or reservation semantics — compensating actions + one alert only.
No move of the hook inside the CAS (a 120s network hook cannot run inside a DB txn). No new config.
Spec-first: do not write implementation code before the focused spec is reviewed.

## Dependencies
None hard. The destroy-side compensating alert fires through PR-5's dispatcher when present (soft —
falls back to a loud log otherwise); the spec deliverable and the suspend-side compensating resume
proceed independently. Money-core adjacent — sequence with care relative to the reconcile path.
close_reason: done

title: Single-instance flock on the data dir (lnrentd.lock)
description:
# PR-22 (GATE-1) — Single-instance flock on {data_dir}/lnrentd.lock

**Roadmap:** production-readiness.md PR-22; focused spec: production-readiness.md §PR-22 (this item
is small and self-contained — no separate focused spec needed).

## Problem / evidence
A stranger-operator running under systemd can end up with TWO daemons on one data dir — duplicate
maintenance loops driving the same subscription into double droplets — with nothing preventing it.

- `run_daemon` (`main.rs:159`) takes NO lock at startup — verified: no `flock`/`lnrentd.lock`/
  `try_lock` anywhere in `main.rs` or `lib.rs`.
- `ipc.rs` SILENTLY rebinds the socket: `ipc.rs:109` (`let _ = std::fs::remove_file(path);`) removes
  any existing socket before `ipc.rs:111` `UnixListener::bind(path)` — so a second daemon steals the
  first's IPC socket with no error.
- Two maintenance loops then drive the same PROVISIONING sub — exactly the duplicate-concurrent-drive
  hazard the code itself documents at `supervisor.rs:1041-1044` ("duplicate concurrent drives could
  run recipe hooks before one loses the CAS") — yielding two real droplets, one orphaned. Mock mode
  has no accidental RocksDB-lock protection at all.

## Design / fix
An exclusive advisory lock taken once at daemon startup, before any maintenance loop or IPC bind.

- Acquire an exclusive `flock` on `{data_dir}/lnrentd.lock` at the top of `run_daemon`
  (`main.rs:159`), BEFORE the IPC socket bind and before the maintenance loop starts. Hold the lock
  file handle for the daemon's lifetime (dropping it releases the lock).
- If the lock is already held, exit immediately with a STRUCTURED "daemon already running" error
  (matching the CLI/IPC error taxonomy), NOT a silent second start — so a systemd restart racing a
  manually-started daemon fails fast and loud instead of double-driving.
- Ledger-authoritative: no balance read; this is a process-safety guard around the single-writer
  discipline (ADR-0001).

## Acceptance criteria
- Starting a second daemon on the same `data_dir` while the first holds the lock exits with a
  structured "daemon already running" error and does NOT rebind the IPC socket or start a second
  maintenance loop.
- Releasing the first daemon (clean exit or crash) frees the lock so a subsequent start succeeds.
- The lock lives at `{data_dir}/lnrentd.lock`.
- Unit / integration tests: a second `run_daemon`-equivalent against a locked data dir returns the
  structured error; after the first handle drops, a fresh acquire succeeds; assert the first daemon's
  socket is NOT removed/rebound while it holds the lock.
- e2e / integration: drive a real daemon startup that holds the lock, attempt a second start on the
  same data dir, assert the structured refusal and that the running daemon's IPC socket keeps
  serving (detailed logging). No relay assertions needed here (pure process/FS guard).
- Gates: `cargo test -p lnrentd` green, `cargo clippy --all-targets` clean.

## Non-goals / scope discipline
No multi-box / HA coordination (single-box operator only). No RocksDB-level lock changes — the flock
is the guard. No change to IPC socket semantics beyond gating the bind behind the lock. No config
knob (the lock path is derived from `data_dir`).

## Dependencies
None.
close_reason: Merged as PR #18 (squash): exclusive flock on {data_dir}/lnrentd.lock acquired via config::prepare_data_dir BEFORE bootstrap opens sqlite (codex P2 fix); structured already-running error; 483 tests + clippy green

title: downtime-credit polish: refresh post-reminder auto-invoice expiry + migration hygiene
description:
## Outcome
Polish nits from the .22 downtime-credit review (no money/correctness bugs; the feature is sound + tested):
1. POST-REMINDER auto-invoice expiry refresh: when downtime credit moves an already-reminded ACTIVE sub's cursor to the credited floor, the existing renew:auto:<sub>:<paid_through> invoice keeps its pre-credit expires_at (raw paid_through+retention_s). If the daemon restarts after that old expiry but before the credited boundary B, expire_open_renewals marks the only auto-invoice EXPIRED and the soft-reminder won't re-fire, so the credited window has no auto-invoice — the buyer must use the (credit-aware) manual renew.request. Refresh the OPEN renew:auto invoice's expires_at to max(expires_at, B) in the credit path. (codex P2.)
2. MIGRATION HYGIENE: suspend_not_before is declared in BOTH the base SCHEMA (migration 0) and the M3 ALTER, so migrate() swallows a duplicate-column error on every fresh install. Safe today (the has_column recheck), but it edits the v1 baseline + leans on a string-match. Cleaner: keep the column only in M3 and standardize test setup on migrate() (as reconcile.rs tests now do) instead of execute_batch(SCHEMA). (claude P2.)
3. DEAD SEAM: order_intake::issue_soft_date_renewal (pub, test-only) still sizes its renew:auto invoice to a fixed 1h, not the credited window — route it through credited sizing or document it as intentionally dead. (claude P3.)

## Why
Surfaced during the .22 'design it properly' work; deferred to keep .22 focused on the correct + tested core. None is service-affecting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A post-reminder credited sub's auto-invoice remains payable through the credited boundary B (no stale-expiry gap forcing manual renew).
- Fresh-install migration does not rely on swallowing a duplicate-column error string.
- issue_soft_date_renewal either uses the credited expiry or is documented/removed.

title: store: retention/GC sweep for op_invocation + inbound_request caches
description:
## Outcome
Add a TTL retention sweep for the durable idempotency caches op_invocation and inbound_request (terminal rows older than a retention window), mirroring the seen_message TTL sweep (store.rs:935).

## Why
op_invocation and inbound_request have NO pruning. op-dispatch (.20) claims a RUNNING op_invocation row BEFORE authorization, so any sender can grow the table unbounded by delivering wraps with distinct request_ids (inbound_request similarly caches failures). seen_message is already TTL-swept; these durable request caches are not. Surfaced by both reviewers during .20; an accepted M1a pattern (not a loop blocker) but needed before real money/scale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A periodic sweep prunes terminal op_invocation (DONE/ERROR) and inbound_request rows older than a configured retention window; in-flight RUNNING rows are never pruned.
- The sweep mirrors the seen_message TTL approach and is wired into the daemon maintenance path (.21).
close_reason: DONE + reviewer-verified: prune_idempotency_caches TTL sweep for op_invocation (terminal, by finished_at) + inbound_request (by created_at), RUNNING never pruned, wired into the 60s reconcile tick. M5 migration adds the TTL indexes; retention 120d > the 90d seen_message window as a re-execute backstop; sweep is best-effort after the heartbeat. rb-lite clean 1 round + 3 review findings applied. Verified: daemon suite + migration tests.

title: Optional self-hosted HTTP bridge/gateway over the lnrent CLI (backlog)
description:
## Outcome
An OPTIONAL, self-hostable HTTP **bridge/gateway** that exposes the lnrent CLI(s) over HTTP for callers that can only reach things through web services. It is a thin shim, NOT a new surface: each endpoint maps 1:1 to a `--json` CLI verb and returns its output verbatim (CLI exit code -> HTTP status). Anyone runs it in front of THEIR OWN CLI/keys; lnrent never hosts it centrally.

## Why
ADR-0014 makes the complete CLI the agent/automation surface and ships NO MCP and NO central/required server. But some callers can only speak HTTP (locked-down environments, no-shell SaaS, simple webhooks). A thin self-hostable bridge over the `--json` CLI serves them WITHOUT reintroducing a central server, WITHOUT business logic, and WITHOUT breaking the no-central-server property (§1) — the bridge operator hosts it over their own CLI; nobody depends on an lnrent-run endpoint.

## Scope
- HTTP endpoints mirroring the CLI verbs 1:1 — buyer (listings / order / subs / ops) and/or operator (status / recipes / subs / admin) — each delegating to the `--json` CLI (or buyer-core directly) and returning its JSON; the CLI exit-code taxonomy maps to HTTP status.
- A reference implementation + docs noting anyone can write their own (it is ~a thin shim).
- It adds NO capability beyond the underlying CLI: it can only do what that CLI can.

## Non-goals
NOT a central / lnrent-hosted service; NOT MCP; NOT a home for business logic (delegates to the CLI / buyer-core); NOT a payer (returns invoices, never pays — §4.7); NOT required for agents (agents use the CLI directly — this exists ONLY for HTTP-only callers); NOT the static web SPA (that stays a no-API human surface, lnrent-7fp.18).

## Constraints / failure handling
Auth is the host's concern — the bridge runs with the host's keys/config and the host puts its own authn/z in front; the bridge introduces no new trust boundary. Payment stays out of scope (returns invoices). Keep it stateless over the CLI; never duplicate daemon/store state.

## Dependencies / sequencing
**BACKLOG — post-M1a, NOT on the M1a critical path.** Builds on the complete, agent-grade `--json` CLIs: operator (lnrent-7fp.12) and buyer (lnrent-7fp.13). Does not block M1a; pick up after the CLIs and their conformance gates land.

## Verification
- Golden-output parity: each endpoint returns the SAME JSON as the equivalent `--json` CLI command.
- A failing CLI command maps to a non-2xx HTTP status carrying the structured `error{code,message,retryable}`.
- The bridge holds no capability the CLI lacks (it cannot, e.g., pay an invoice).

## Spec refs
ADR-0014 (CLI is the surface; optional self-hosted bridge permitted), SPEC §4.7, §1 (no central server), §17 (out of v1 scope, backlogged); see [[feedback_cli_over_mcp]].

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each HTTP endpoint returns byte-for-byte the SAME JSON as the equivalent `--json` CLI command (golden-output parity test).
- A failing CLI command maps to a non-2xx HTTP status whose body carries the structured `error{code,message,retryable}` (the CLI exit-code taxonomy maps to HTTP status).
